background.name: Explorer

_: a scroll case containing maps in progress

entries: You've charted paths through the inhospitable jungles of Alchiere and the trackless wastes of the frozen north to fill in the blank areas on the map. You are more at home in a freshly unsealed Orgoth tomb than in the bustling streets of a major city.

entries.name: Feature: Patron
entries: You receive a stipend from a university, a wealthy individual, or another organization each month as long as you continue to explore new regions, report back regularly, and bring your patron the occasional gift from an exotic place. The amount of this stipend is {@dice 1d10} gp per character level. At the GM's discretion, your benefactor can give you heftier sums for noteworthy accomplishments.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: Explorers feel called to the unknown and forgotten. They are accustomed to packing light enough to travel but well enough to be prepared for whatever challenges await. Their motivations generally involve increasing either their general knowledge or their personal gain, while their flaws are often the consequence of too much curiosity.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: I crave the excitement of being the first to enter a long-forgotten place.
I can quote any of Professor Pendrake's essays; in fact, I do so whenever I get the chance.
Studying at a prestigious university has prepared me for life on the road.
I believe knowledge is for everyone, and I work to make sure all my discoveries end up in museums, where others can benefit from them.
I love learning new languages and can sit with a book of lore for hours.
I've spent so much time in the wilderness that I forget myself in polite company.
Despite wise counsel, if it involves a tomb, the Orgoth, or a curse on all who enter, I'm going in.
Campfires are the perfect stage, and I love telling stories.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Discovery. I want to discover all there is to know about the world, expand my understanding of it, and share that understanding with others. (Good)
Ambition. My name will go down in history as one of the greatest explorers of the age. (Any)
History. Understanding our nations' past is the only way to plot our path forward. (Good)
Profit. I want to sell my discoveries in order to acquire immense personal wealth. (Neutral)
Adventure. I cannot be content living an everyday life and seek adventure however I can find it. (Chaotic)
Power. I want to discover ancient relics and use them to amass power. (Evil)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: I owe a significant debt to a prestigious institution as a result of a previous expedition.
I "accidentally" defiled a sacred site and am no longer allowed in particular villages.
A rival I once beat to a find will stop at nothing to discredit me.
I will one day have my writings published in my university's library.
I will be the first to chart the unknown mysteries of Zu.
One day, I will be as famous as Viktor Pendrake.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: Being first to a discovery is more important than being safe, and I have the scars to prove it.
If presented with a riddle or puzzle, I can think of nothing else until it's solved.
I cannot abide a closed door. I must at least take a peek at what's behind it.
My obsession with being prepared often leaves me overly encumbered and broke.
I am irrationally terrified of an ordinary animal.
My overconfidence in my abilities is matched only by the inadequateness of my preparation.

background.name: Order of Wizardry

_: proof of membership in the Order of Wizardry

entries: Widely known and respected as an institution for training those of arcane aptitude, the Order of Wizardry has been home to you and many other mages eager to progress along the established ranks of their craft.

entries.name: Feature: Order Lodge
entries: The Order maintains public lodges in most of the major cities in Cygnar and Ord and recently began reestablishing its facilities in Llael. It also maintains secret facilities in other nations' population centers, but these are available to members only with specific permission. At the GM's discretion, you can contact one of these locations and request reasonable aid if you are in good standing with the organization.

entries.name: Feature: Magus Training
entries: {@b Prerequisite:} Spellcasting, Warcaster Magic, or Pact Magic class feature.
Add the spells on the Magus Training Spells table to the spell list for your spellcasting class. If you are a multiclass character with multiple spell lists, add these spells to all of them.

entries.caption: Magus Training Spells
rows: Cantrip
mage hand
1st
detect magic
2nd
magic weapon
3rd
dispel magic
4th
arcane eye
5th
scrying

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: Defined by their study and dedication to magecraft, members of the Order of Wizardry are well suited to long periods of research and introspection on arcane matters. Their greatest flaw is often hubris, a reflection of their elevated opinion of themselves.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: I like trying to figure out how things work. Don't leave mechanika around me if you don't want it taken apart.
There are rules to everything, and I remind people of that regularly.
I've seen a lot, so I'm tough to rattle.
I am comfortable being in charge, even if I was not asked.
I don't put myself above others, but I do hold myself to higher standards.
You can have all the money and fame in the world, but a fireball will still ruin your day.
I take pride in how much others rely on me, and I offer aid whenever I can.
I read every chance I get. An expansive vocabulary enables an extensive arsenal.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Glory. I will go down in history as one of the greatest arcanists of the age. (Any)
Comfort. I would like to live a life of quiet luxury, although I have to earn it first. (Neutral)
Knowledge. I seek to further my knowledge of the arcane, even if it means studying the darker side of things. (Any)
Order. I am committed to my order, not to ideals. (Lawful)
Power. I see the arcane as a tool to acquiring great personal power. (Evil)
Mastery. I seek to perfect my understanding of the arcane at any price. (Any)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: I made off from the Order's library with a tome I should have left behind.
My goal is to be published as one of the preeminent scholars of mechanikal theory.
I was run out of my institution and must work to clear my name.
I made a horrible miscalculation on a spell as a student, and someone was seriously injured. I will never be so careless again.
A patron paid for my studies, and I now owe that individual a rather large favor.
I am tortured by dreams of finding a relic long thought lost.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: I cannot walk away from any chance to increase my knowledge.
I cannot hide my disdain for those I scorn, and it gets me into trouble.
When something goes wrong, I start pointing fingers.
I have several vices that part me from my coins very quickly.
When things turn from good to bad, I am the first to flee.
For better or worse, I have absolute faith in the leaders of my order.

background.name: Greylords Covenant

_: a staff inlaid with Greylord runes

entries: You are a member of the Greylords Covenant, the arcane branch of both the Khadoran Army and the Khadoran government. As such, you've worked alongside accomplished arcanists, patriots, and soldiers in one of the most formidable occult organizations in western Immoren.

entries.name: Feature: Chapter House
entries: The Greylords Covenant maintains public chapter houses in most significant Khadoran towns and secret facilities in the major population centers of other nations. At the GM's discretion, you can contact one of these locations and request reasonable aid if you are in good standing with the organization.

entries.name: Feature: Uchenik Training
entries: {@b Prerequisite:} Spellcasting, Warcaster Magic, or Pact Magic class feature.
Add the spells on the Uchenik Training Spells table to the spell list for your spellcasting class. If you are a multiclass character with multiple spell lists, add these spells to all of them.

entries.caption: Uchenik Training Spells
rows: Cantrip
ray of frost
1st
fog cloud
2nd
gust of wind
3rd
glyph of warding (cold damage only)
4th
ice storm
5th
cone of cold

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: Greylords combine their mastery of magic with many other disciplines, such as intelligence gathering and national security. Their ideals are shaped by the militant training they undergo. Their flaws often emerge in response to the kind of work they do for their government.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: My loyalty to Khador and my Empress is undying.
People find me distant and cold, but I am watching and listening to everything.
Offer me a mystery or riddle to grab my attention.
I strive to excel at everything I put my mind to.
Failure is the best teacher, but it is harsh and unforgiving.
I am deeply concerned about the welfare of my comrades and will do anything for them.
The ends justify the means, and I will not waiver in my duty.
I require all available information about a situation before I commit to a course of action.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Nation. My nation and its people are all that matter. (Any)
Security. There is no price too high to keep the empire safe. (Lawful)
Influence. The higher I rise in the ranks of my order, the more significant my say in its future. (Neutral)
Law. Adherence to the law is the only thing that keeps civilization united. (Lawful)
Discipline. Because of my arcane power, I must maintain a strict code of personal conduct. (Good)
Secrets. Everyone is hiding something. By learning what that is, I gain power over them. (Evil)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: I am beholden to the patron who sponsored my training.
I am bound by duty to bring a fugitive of my chapter house to justice.
I work to be seen as a hero to my people.
A comrade died so that I might live. I work to honor that sacrifice.
I learned a dark secret and must keep it hidden.
I have a lost love whose heart I hope to reclaim through great deeds.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: Chain of command is to be obeyed, whether I agree or not.
I cannot abide insults to my integrity or honor.
I find solace only in the bottom of a bottle.
I am an exceptionally harsh judge of character, my own included.
My abilities make me superior to others.
Trusting others is nearly impossible for me.

background.name: Investigator
entries: You are a skilled detective adept in the techniques of deduction, criminology, and interrogation. In the past, you may have been a watch inspector, a former inquisitor, or a member of a military intelligence-gathering organization.

entries.name: Feature: Astute
entries: Your keen mind and sharp eyes miss very little. You can gain advantage when you make a Wisdom (Insight), Intelligence (Investigation), or Wisdom (Perception) check, but you can't use this feature again until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: Investigators are practical individuals who trust only their own eyes and try to back up their observations with other tools and methods. Discovering the truth often takes precedence over assigning blame.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: I pride myself on my part in keeping things from descending into chaos.
Trust must be earned. I never give it freely.
I am fascinated by people and love to study their mannerisms and interactions.
I am blunt and to the point. I have no time for pointless exposition.
I live for the thrill of the chase. I feel free only when I'm in pursuit.
I love recounting my exploits.
I am willing to overlook crimes of necessity in order to focus on those who harm others.
If I believe someone is hiding something, I cannot concentrate on anything else until the secret is uncovered.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Justice. I seek justice for all who have suffered wrongdoing. (Lawful)
Responsibility. I have a responsibility to others that guides my actions. (Good)
Truth. The truth, no matter how uncomfortable, must be discovered and told. (Lawful)
People. I believe people are worth preserving, not ideals or unfeeling laws. (Neutral)
Reason. Logic, not emotion, should guide my choices and actions. (Any)
Punishment. Lawbreakers must be discovered so that I may punish them. (Evil)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: I wasn't good enough to catch someone who ended up ruining my life, but I will have justice.
I owe everything to a mentor whose ideals I strive to live up to.
If anyone learned a secret about my past, all my honor would be lost.
I always take a stand whenever I witness injustice.
I made a mistake and ended up in a crime lord's pocket.
I constantly train to be at the peak of my ability.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: My career kept me from forming any real connections. I still have a hard time making friends.
I expect to be lied to at every turn.
I am quick to see people's flaws and can't help but point them out.
I see everything in black and white.
Things only work well when I am in full control.
The only thing I'm better at than pessimism is cynicism.

background.name: Iron Fang

_: a trophy taken from an enemy (a dagger, broken weapon, or tarnished emblem)

entries: You are a trained Iron Fang and were recently part of the mighty legions of Khador. Your grueling training and battlefield experience have made you dauntless and have inured you to the hardships of battle. Skilled at formation fighting, you feel most comfortable in action when allies are close at hand.

entries.name: Feature: Wall of Iron
entries: You are accustomed to fighting shoulder to shoulder with fellow members of the Iron Fang legions. You gain +1 AC while standing within 5 feet of an ally. Additionally, when you take the Help action to aid an ally's attack, you can make one weapon attack as a bonus action.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: Teamwork, trust, and fraternity are core values to all Iron Fangs. The regimented military life of an elite unit helps shape their ideals. Their flaws are often the byproduct of a life spent on the front lines of battle.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: Trust is key if I want to ensure success.
I am quick to aid others but slow to change my opinions of them.
I am implacable unless someone disparages my military unit or my Empress.
Risk is irrelevant. Achieving the goal is the only thing that matters.
I am cold and brutal on the battlefield but warm and gregarious off it.
I do not hesitate to lend aid to someone in need—even to my enemies.
I cannot abide those who prey on others.
Take care of your equipment, and it will take care of you.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Comrades. My loyalties lie with my comrades in arms, not with any code of ethics. (Neutral)
Duty. I swore an oath to fight to my last breath. I will not betray it. (Lawful)
Nation. Any threats to the Motherland must be destroyed. (Any)
Victory. By defeating others, I prove my strength. (Chaotic)
Protection. My might saves others from harm. (Good)
Violence. I thrill at destroying those who dare challenge me. (Evil)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: I would die to protect my comrades in arms.
My bond to my homeland is paramount. I will do anything Khador requires of me.
I seek renown for my prowess in battle.
My unit kommander betrayed us. I will never forget this treachery.
I cannot return home until I have reclaimed my father's shield.
I intervene in any situation that seems unfair.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: My default plan in any situation is to be stronger and tougher than the problem.
I act with haste but am slow to process information.
I can't keep out of trouble if provoked.
If everyone just did things my way, there wouldn't be any problems.
I am a coward when I am unarmed.
I regularly forget my strength and often pay a steep price for it.

background.name: Kayazy

_: a dark-colored set of common clothes with a hood

entries: You are related to one of the kayazy, Khador's infamous merchant princes. By tradition, members of the kayazy are leaders of both legitimate commercial industry and the criminal underworld. You are a valued member of the organization and can expect to draw support from it when needed.

entries.name: Feature: History of Violence
entries: You've been on both sides of so many ambushes that you are very accustomed to situations turning violent in the blink of an eye. You gain a +2 bonus to Dexterity checks when determining initiative. Additionally, you cannot be surprised by humanoids in an urban environment.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: Things are seldom black and white among the kayazy, and a pragmatic outlook is almost a requirement. Coin is the great equalizer, and everything has a price.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: Trust is only as strong as the coin backing it up.
I am swift in action, silent in deed.
I calculate every risk and am prepared in any situation.
I judge by actions, not words.
I'm always fidgeting if I'm not doing something.
Gallows humor is the only humor I have.
I'm haunted by the things I've done.
I believe nothing is straightforward or simple.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Cunning. You don't have to outfight an opponent you can outthink. (Any)
Tradition. The old ways must be respected. (Lawful)
Greed. Honor and duty are for poor people. I just want more wealth. (Evil)
Freedom. This life allows me to choose my path. (Chaotic)
Power. When you have power and influence, you can do anything. (Any)
Family. Without my family, I am nothing. (Any)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: I am still obligated to the terms of a contract I have yet to fulfill.
I seek to undo a horrible wrong from my past.
I was betrayed by a comrade. I will have my revenge.
I will do anything to acquire great wealth.
I believe in those I fight with, and I will atone for my past by ensuring their future.
I send most of my gains to my struggling family.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: I will take just about anything of value that isn't nailed down.
If it comes to money or those I care about, I care about the money.
I am confident in my abilities and have trouble tolerating differing opinions.
I put too much trust in family, and it might get me killed.
I fixate on problems and have trouble letting go of a goal.
I am secretly in the government's employ and have infiltrated the organization to keep tabs on its activities.

background.name: Knight Exemplar
entries: You are one of the elite armored warriors of the Menite faith. You live by a strict code of absolute obedience to the Menite Temple's priests, especially those in the scrutator caste, and follow every order without question or hesitation.

entries.name: Feature: Righteous Anger
entries: You have been trained to fight alongside a group of other knights and to protect those who stand beside you. When an enemy attack damages one or more of your allies while they are within 10 feet of you, you gain +2 STR and +2 AC for 1 round. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: Knights Exemplar tend to be both virtuous and vengeful. A Knight Exemplar who witnesses the sacrifice of a comrade in arms will stop at nothing to obtain vengeance.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: I am engrossed in the history of Menoth's greatest heroes and often discuss their words and deeds.
Failure to maintain my routine causes me great distress—distress that I am quite willing to pass on to others.
I am constantly optimistic, even when it isn't warranted.
Showing others respect is very important to me.
Having lost too many of my fellow knights, I am slow to make friends with my fellow warriors.
Overcoming great darkness has made me callous to many other dangerous situations.
I am jaded from constant battle and see little reason for hope in the material world.
Only humanity can feel Menoth's true righteousness and attest to his will. All other species are therefore innately suspect.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Scripture. I am personally dedicated to the laws and dictates of scripture, which I prioritize over my health and well-being. (Lawful)
Selfless. Sacrifice for others is my ultimate motivation. Only self-sacrifice will let me claim my place in Urcaen by Menoth's side. (Good)
Avenger. Those who have harmed the faithful deserve only death, no matter the cost. (Evil)
Beneficent. I fight for the betterment of my fellow man, either in deed or in battle. Being a knight means helping the helpless. (Good)
Aspiration. I seek to prove myself to both Menoth and his church in order to acquire a station worthy of my deeds. (Any)
Orthodox. All must adhere to the dictates of scripture. Failure to do so will only lead them to damnation. (Neutral)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: During the recent horrors, I rescued a small group of refugees who have become my surrogate family.
Several artifacts were stolen from my town's temple after its decline, and I will stop at nothing to reclaim them.
I owe a great deal to my spiritual mentor, an elderly scrutator.
I have become wary of a specific branch of the church, and I am locked in a dangerous game of cat and mouse with them.
My every service is rendered unto the ordinary people in the Creator's name.
A heretic killed a vital member of my temple, and I have devoted myself to righteous vengeance.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: I sometimes doubt the power of the Creator in light of all that has happened to western Immoren in the last decade.
My thinking is rigid, and I view the world in black and white.
Unless I have a specific reason to do otherwise, I place absolute faith in the clergy.
I have a penchant for claiming vengeance for even minor slights.
Once I have an important goal or quest, I fixate on it to the near exclusion of all else and am unwilling to accept failure.
I implicitly distrust those who have no specific faith or whose faith does not match my own.

background.name: Knights of the Prophet
entries: You are a member of one of the largest orders of knights serving the Church of Morrow. Although you are not forbidden from defending your nation, you have taken solemn oaths to protect and preserve the church itself above all else.

entries.name: Feature: Morrow's Guidance
entries: You felt the touch of Morrow on your life before you joined the order, and you can still feel his hand guiding you to defend his church. You can pray to Morrow to give you guidance in a task related to your calling. When you use this feature, you gain advantage on any roll for an action that takes no longer than a single round. Once you use this feature, you cannot use it again until you finish a short or long rest.
Additionally, at the GM's discretion, you can seek aid or council at the main chapter house for the Knights of the Prophet in the Sancteum or at any of the major chapter houses in Highgate and Ceryl.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: Knights of the Prophet act as the military wing of the Church of Morrow. A life of balancing goodness and fighting is challenging but ultimately fulfilling.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: I tend to be too quick to trust. People often call me gullible.
I hate even the smallest lies so much that I often say painfully true things that make others shy away from me.
The loss of many comrades in arms haunts me and makes it difficult for me to create new friendships.
I have a lesson for every situation drawn from either scripture or experience.
Very little can shake my optimistic outlook on the world—not even the Claiming itself.
I can find the right words to inspire people in even the darkest times.
I speak formally at all times and do not like it when others use vulgar language.
I look down on nobles who treat commoners poorly.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Valor. Vanquishing dark forces from the world for humanity's benefit is my ultimate duty and goal. (Good)
Knowledge. Gaining knowledge of the world is the best way to improve it. (Neutral)
Honesty. People's paths should reflect honestly on themselves. (Any)
Charity. I attend to the Prophet's will not only through conflict, but also by relieving the suffering of the downtrodden. (Good)
Faith. I am an instrument of the Prophet, and his revelations help me accomplish his will. (Lawful)
Freedom. Tyrants and dictators cannot be allowed to lord over the people. (Chaotic)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: A thief stole from my church and killed an innocent soul in the process. I will bring this criminal to justice.
I will follow any orders from the church, even if I harbor doubt about them.
Before becoming a knight, I learned the sword from a kindly swordmaster. I have lost track of my teacher but look forward to our reunion.
I was raised in an orphanage dedicated to my god. I return to offer aid whenever possible.
The Claiming left me with only one surviving family member, whose safety is paramount to me.
I made a powerful enemy of a specific group of cultists when I revealed them to the church.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: I am often far too truthful for my own good.
I find it difficult to imagine that a person can truly be good without faith in the Prophet.
I secretly take great pleasure in killing my foes and fear someone will uncover my true nature one day.
Once set on a path, I will ignore almost everything else to attain my goal.
Although it was not directly Morrow's doing, I have a hard time maintaining my faith after the culmination of the course he and his sister put humanity on.
I take tremendous pride in my skills and deeds. All those who fail to show me the respect I deserve are suspect.

background.name: Labor Boss

_: a set of rugged work clothes

entries: Before your life as an adventurer, you led laborers on the docks of a port city or in the heart of a factory district. You have experience directing the activities of those under you, even when they would rather be in charge, and you have an eye for the essential parts of a task.

entries.name: Feature: Easy Employment
entries: Your years of experience make you a highly desirable asset in any labor market, and you can easily find work during any downtime. If you spend your time between adventures practicing a profession, you can maintain a comfortable lifestyle. In addition, you save an amount of gold per day equal to half the result of an Athletics or Intimidation check.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: Labor bosses are stern folk who take pleasure in seeing a job through to completion. They are the first to step up to a challenge and pit their strength and wits against whatever problem is at hand.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: If a job's worth doing, it's worth doing right.
I work as hard as I play.
I am far craftier than I let on.
Camaraderie with my fellow workers is important to me.
I have always been a serious person. People often call me gruff.
At the end of the day, people only respect a firm hand.
I can maintain an upbeat attitude through most events.
Routine is important to me. Even if I'm on the road, having a ritual keeps me grounded.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Union. When the voices of the workers unite, we cannot be ignored. (Good)
Opportunity. Labor may be challenging, but it offers work in every city and port. (Neutral)
Independence. Being in charge means that no one can tie me down. (Chaotic)
Respect. I afford others the same respect they give me. (Neutral)
Profession. I'm good at what I do, and it puts food on the table. (Any)
Master. I like being able to order other people around and make them listen to me. (Evil)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: The first crew I ran became a surrogate family. I still have strong ties to them.
After an unfortunate workplace death, I have been blacklisted in a particular city.
A person I had to fire has it in for me.
I have a reputation for being a bit of a tyrant.
My sibling left to become an adventurer, and now I seek to reconnect us.
I ran such an efficient crew that I was able to create a good relationship with our wealthy benefactor.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: I am frugal to the point that people often call me a penny-pincher.
I'm quick to anger when confronted.
Some people I have worked with call me a bully.
My disillusionment with my day-to-day life has caused me to become a risk-taker.
I have a fairly severe drinking habit.
I have trouble keeping track of money. If I'm not very careful, I can lose an entire month's wages before I know it.

background.name: Maritime Order of the Trident

_: an insignia of rank in the Ordic navy

entries: Your early years were spent training as an arcanist working in concert with the Ordic navy. Although your service recently ended, you likely still serve in an auxiliary capacity alongside the kingdom's naval forces.

entries.name: Feature: Ordic Veteran
entries: You spent your early years as a member of the Ordic Royal Navy. Whether you left on good terms or less favorable ones, you still have many friends who serve. At the GM's discretion, you can secure passage on an Ordic ship if one of your friends has enough sway or is the vessel's captain.

entries.name: Feature: Magus-at-Arms Training
entries: {@b Prerequisite:} Spellcasting, Warcaster Magic, or Pact Magic class feature.
Add the spells on the Magus-at-Arms Training Spells table to the spell list for your spellcasting class. If you are a multiclass character with multiple spell lists, add these spells to all of them.

entries.caption: Magus-at-Arms Training Spells
rows: Cantrip
acid splash
1st
create or destroy water
2nd
acid arrow
3rd
water breathing
4th
control water
5th
conjure elemental (water only)

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: Members of the Maritime Order of the Trident are comfortable living a wanderer's life. Despite their seemingly chaotic bent, the Order instills a sense of patriotic duty, military ethic, and courage.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: I always feel at home when I'm on or around the sea.
Performing my duties precisely is extremely important to me.
I love telling tall tales of the sea.
Simple solutions to problems are often the best.
I try to prepare for any and all contingencies.
I often talk at length about the intricacies of sailing to anyone who will listen—and a few who won't.
I love vulgar banter, even if I'm the subject.
I am a very reliable friend.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Crew. I am committed to my crew, not to ideals. (Chaotic)
Exploration. I use my abilities to guide myself and others to undiscovered places. (Any)
Independence. This life allows me to change course whenever I feel like it. (Chaotic)
Protection. My abilities allow others to safely return home from the sea. (Good)
Duty. I have a duty to protect my nation's assets. (Lawful)
Greatness. I'll use my powers to sink anyone who tries to impede my rise to greatness. (Evil)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: My first captain and I enjoyed a great relationship and have become lifelong friends.
When the day comes to an end, I'm the first one to the tavern.
I was knocked out during a brutal storm and fell into the sea. I owe my life to the person who saved me.
I successfully thwarted a pirate raid years ago, and that crew has had it in for me ever since.
A strict hierarchy is extremely important. My allegiance is to the captain first and the crew second.
I lost a great deal of money gambling in a particular port and then skipped town to avoid paying my debt.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: I follow orders from my superiors even when I know full well they are wrong.
I have little respect for those who have not earned their sea legs.
I have been known to act somewhat wildly during shore leave.
My great pride in myself borders on hubris.
I enjoy starting a rousing sea shanty. Anyone who grumbles for me to stop had best grumble in tune.
I often pocket small trinkets I find interesting.

background.name: Mercenary
entries: The long history of bloody conflict in the Iron Kingdoms has created an endless opportunity for those willing to commit violence for coin. You are a mercenary: a soldier of fortune generally loyal only to whichever paymaster is currently filling your pockets.

entries.name: Feature: The Charter
entries: A long-standing code of conduct called simply "the Charter" defines the rules and strictures under which mercenaries operate. It clarifies how mercenaries are expected to treat employers, civilians, opposing and allied militaries, prisoners, and other mercenary companies. When interacting with other mercenaries or potential employers, you can expect them to understand the Charter's basics and not to impede your fulfillment of a contract unless it actively goes against their interests.

Additionally, you have advantage on Charisma (Persuasion) or Charisma (Intimidation) checks related to haggling over a contract, negotiating with a kingdom's military personnel, or interacting with anyone in a way that directly relates to your fulfillment of a contract.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: Although mercenaries might seem like nothing more than soldiers for hire, they are often far more complex. Because they are so well traveled, they often lack biases common among soldiers of national militaries.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: I tend to be rather selfish.
Combats and contracts are to be taken seriously; everything else is trivial.
Even though many see me as an unethical mercenary, I hold dear to my faith and its tenets.
I am slow to trust even my comrades in arms.
I live every day as if it might be my last. In this profession, it just might be.
I am deeply dedicated to my martial training.
I enjoy the work of calculating numbers, but only when it comes to making money.
Some have called me a conniver, and they aren't exactly wrong.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Gold. I kill people for coin. It's nothing personal. (Neutral)
Might. In battle, I can prove to others how strong I truly am. (Any)
The Charter. I follow my contracts to the letter. If you wanted something else, you should have negotiated better terms. (Lawful)
Protection. Though I fight to get paid, I go out of my way to defend the helpless. (Good)
Freedom. The mercenary life allows me to live how I choose. (Chaotic)
Battle. I feel truly alive only when I'm cutting down someone else. (Evil)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: One of my parents taught me arithmetic. It's turned out to be very important to me, but they aren't exactly pleased with how I use it.
I recently struck out on my own, but my old mercenary company will always be my home.
A retired mercenary taught me the ropes. I still look up to my mentor as a parental figure.
I burned a bridge with a particular mercenary group when I was forced to flee from a fight.
I have become acquainted with a Thamarite cleric who oddly shows up from time to time during my journeys.
I once saved the life of a merchant who hired me as a bodyguard. Now that word has spread, merchants are always trying to hire me for similar jobs.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: When combat begins to turn in an even slightly unfavorable direction, I am the first to run.
I have become too jaded by the callous nature of those who pay others to fight their battles.
Violence is the only language I truly understand.
If the coin is good, I'm willing to get my hands dirty working jobs that are considered questionable even by those in my profession.
I'm disillusioned with the mercenary life, but I can't afford to walk away.
Battle has scarred me physically and mentally, and I will never be the same again.

background.name: Northern Crusade

_: an emblem of the Northern Crusade

entries: The Protectorate of Menoth launched the Northern Crusade at the same time that Hierarch Voyle declared the nation's independence from Cygnar. No matter when you joined the Crusade, you were there when it ended—left without a purpose, your faith nearly shattered.

entries.name: Feature: Faith in What's Real
entries: Your experiences in Llael have left you disillusioned with your former faith. You have learned that blindly following the words of others is sometimes neither the best nor the safest path. You have advantage on Wisdom (Insight) checks the first time you interact with a creature.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: Former members of the Northern Crusade are often disillusioned and distrustful of others. Despite these changes, some maintain a semblance of firm conviction in their religion.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: I have a scriptural quote for any situation I find myself in.
I am quick to anger.
I have a soft spot for children.
I often take time to enjoy the simple things in life.
I have a somber demeanor.
My moral code is essential to me and often dictates my actions.
Others gain my trust slowly, but once they have it, it is hard to lose.
I carry out my goals with unflinching persistence.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Dedication. Despite the end of the crusade, I am still dedicated to its ideals. (Lawful)
Hopelessness. I have seen the ending of not just my organization, but the world I thought I knew. I seek new meaning but am uncertain I will ever find it. (Chaotic)
Reform. To create something useful and just, we must cast the old order aside. (Chaotic)
Faith. It is in the faith of the Creator, not the worldly church, that we find meaning. (Neutral)
Justice. I seek justice for all who have suffered wrongdoing. (Lawful)
Vengeance. Those who pervert the Creator's will deserve only punishment. (Evil)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: My time in Llael has created deeper connections there than in my homeland.
Powerful members of the church sponsored my training and were extremely angry with me when I joined the crusade rather than their force.
During my time in the crusade, I learned a terrifying truth about one of my superiors, who now has it in for me.
I became affiliated with a specific resistance group in Llael and still have friendly contacts there.
I have a secret in my past I will do anything to hide.
I was an orphan, and a kindly older couple raised me.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: Anyone who besmirches my honor deserves retribution.
Violence is always the ultimate solution.
I am brutally honest.
After losing many comrades, I have become extremely jaded.
I often find solace at the bottom of a tankard of ale.
After the horrific events of the Claiming and everything that transpired afterward, I have become disillusioned with my religion.

background.name: Privateer
entries: You are a privateer, buccaneer, or pirate. Issued a letter of marque from one of the Iron Kingdoms, you've most recently hunted and captured the ships of enemy nations or fought your former fellows as they preyed upon merchant ships.

entries.name: Feature: Legal Authority
entries: You have detailed documentation that grants you permission to prey upon the ships of enemy nations. Choose one nation: Cygnar, Khador, Ord, or the Protectorate of Menoth. You have a reasonable expectation of assistance from this nation's naval authorities and can, at the GM's discretion, avoid legal repercussions for your actions as long as you can prove that such actions hurt your nation's enemies.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: Privateers begin their careers for many reasons, but their ideals are shaped by a desire to stick with the trade because of their love of the sea and lack of faith in traditional authority. Superstition is common among them.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: I have been to many ports of call and have seen much of the world, so I have little bias when it comes to other nations' cultures.
I put absolute faith in a myriad of superstitions and apply them to numerous situations on both land and sea.
Almost any disagreement can be settled with a drink, and for those that can't—well, that's what these fists are for.
I always make sure everyone gets their fair share in any interaction.
I challenge any leaders I see as unfit or undeserving.
I value my freedom above all else and cannot abide seeing anyone else tethered or oppressed.
I have no interest in "saving my coin" or "hoarding wealth" if there is a game of chance to be had.
I will take any opportunity to show off or enhance my reputation.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Crew. My crewmates are more important to me than any code of ethics. (Any)
Plunder. In the end, all I'm looking for is the next big score. (Any)
Freedom. My ship gives me the freedom to go wherever my heart desires. (Chaotic)
Fear. If people fear me, it means they respect me. (Evil)
Charity. The wealth I steal on the seas can change the lives of the less fortunate. (Good)
Code of Conduct. I plunder only from those my letter of marque permits me to. (Lawful)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: There is a price on my head.
I am searching for a long-lost treasure.
I want to captain a vessel.
I have vowed revenge on the person who forced this life upon me.
I seek to be reunited with a lost love.
My crew is my life, and I protect them at all costs.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: If my real name were to become widely known, my family would be disgraced.
I react violently to any perceived slight.
I do not know when to quit if dice are involved.
I get despondent if I am away from the sea for too long.
I am incapable of running from a fight.
Since I expect everyone to take full advantage of any perceived weaknesses, I don't show any.

background.name: Resistance Veteran

_: a souvenir from your battles for the Resistance (a Khadoran insignia, a charred coin, etc.)
a set of common clothes with a dark hooded cape

entries: Whether you were a loyal citizen of Llael or a Cygnaran soldier fighting for Llael's independence, you were trapped in southeast Llael after the fall of the capital. You spent many long, bleak years fighting but never gave up the dream of restoring the nation.

entries.name: Feature: Underground Networks
entries: You have first-hand knowledge of the secrets a city can hold and know how to communicate with the many underground elements found in urban settings. While you are in an urban area, you have advantage on Intelligence (Investigation) checks to find information about the location of black markets, criminal enterprises such as smuggling rings, and other similar subversive aspects of a city. While you are in Llael, you double your proficiency bonus for these checks.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: Veterans of the Resistance tend to be driven individuals. Their drive to realize their ideals borders on obsession, if not outright mania. Although pragmatic or even cynical, they care deeply for their fellows and all who are oppressed.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: I have great faith that we ordinary people can overcome any hardship if we all work together.
I am and always have been a realist. The occupation of Llael only solidified my outlook.
I have a lackadaisical attitude, but it is merely cover for my scheming nature.
When forced into a leadership role during the Resistance, command came naturally to me.
I never trust those with authority; instead, I work under them only out of necessity.
For anything done in the name of the greater good, the ends always justify the means.
After seeing the horrors of war, I have come to view violence as a last resort.
I am always optimistic and happy, even when faced with difficult circumstances.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Change. The old world is gone. It is time to make a new one. (Chaotic)
People. The people who struggled by my side matter much more than ideals. (Neutral)
Tradition. If we do not preserve what remains of our culture, we will forget who we truly are. (Lawful)
Obligation. My people have suffered. I fight every day so that they will never go through such pain again. (Good)
Punishment. Some of those responsible for our suffering have yet to be punished for their actions, but I will see to it that they are. (Evil)
Reconstruction. One way or another, I'll see my home return to its former glory. (Any)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: During the occupation, I was secretly working for a deposed politician who now has power again.
I was tasked with assassinating a Khadoran official. I succeeded, but that person's family now knows my name and face and has sworn to claim vengeance.
I was a government official before the occupation, but after everything was taken from me, I gave up my old identity entirely.
While on a mission into occupied Llael, I took several street urchins under my wing. To this day, I still look after them.
I lost track of my sibling during the occupation and the Claiming. I hope one day we will be reunited.
When I first joined the Resistance, a grizzled veteran taught me the ropes. I still owe this old-timer a great deal.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: I have become irrevocably scarred after watching the massacre of my friends and family during the Claiming.
I am deeply devoted to the idea that embracing anarchy is the only way to truly claim one's freedom.
I have a troubling vice in alcohol and an addictive personality.
I lie so often, not even I can unravel all the untruths anymore.
There can be no peace until vengeance has been wrought in full. Those who do not understand this are naive.
I can't resist a pretty face.

background.name: Retribution of Scyrah

_: a ring with a concealed Retribution of Scyrah emblem

entries: You are one of the Iosan zealots who believe that human arcanists' eradication will restore your gods. Your devotion to the Retribution of Scyrah is certain, no matter how you served.

entries.name: Feature: Intense Training
entries: Members of the Retribution undergo intensive training that breaks them down and remakes them into weapons for their gods. Whenever you make a skill check using tools you are proficient with and spend at least 10 minutes working on the task, you can treat a d20 roll of 9 or lower as a 10.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: Firm resolve is a hallmark of most members of the Retribution, but this steadfast determination can blind them to alternative ways of thinking. By keeping themselves moving toward their goal, members of the Retribution hope to outpace the cruel reality of their situation.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: Since birth, I have had a hard time expressing nearly any emotion.
For anything done in the name of the greater good, the ends always justify the means.
I have a short temper.
I am incredibly dedicated. Once I set a goal, I will do everything in my power to attain it.
I tend to be too serious for my own good.
I have always found it easier to lie than to be honest.
I find dangerous situations exhilarating.
Performing my duties with precision is extremely important to me.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Obedience. Following the orders of those above us keeps us all safe. (Lawful)
Duty. I swore an oath to fight until my last breath. I will not betray it. (Lawful)
Nation. Ios and its people are all that matter. (Any)
Vengeance. I seek to bring death to those who have sullied Scyrah. (Evil)
Comrades. My loyalties lie with my comrades in arms, not with any code of ethics. (Neutral)
Discipline. I am extremely disciplined when it comes to honing my martial prowess. (Any)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: A swordmaster taught me my skills at a young age. I still look up to this teacher.
A particular human mage has eluded me but will fall to my blade one day.
When I first joined the Retribution, I angered a superior officer, who still has it in for me.
I joined the Retribution in order to be seen as a hero to my people.
All I do with my free time is train.
I fight to honor the memory of a close comrade in arms.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: I follow any order even if, deep down, I know it is wrong.
I try to hide it, but I delight in violence.
I stay and fight even when all hope is lost.
My great pride in myself borders on hubris.
I will have my revenge on anyone who sullies my honor.
Ever since the Claiming, I have secretly been losing faith that the gods can be restored.

background.name: Seeker

_: a journal for cataloging your findings

entries: Driven by the goal of rectifying the troubles that plague your people, you are a member of a minority religious sect among some Iosans who voluntarily live as exiles from Ios. Unlike those in the Retribution, you believe in finding answers rather than assigning blame.

entries.name: Feature: Secretive Purpose
entries: Seekers rarely identify themselves as members of the sect. You can read and write messages written in Seekers' code, a secret mix of signs and symbols that allows Seekers to hide messages in the borders of roadside signs and to incorporate information into what appears to be common graffiti. This code allows you to keep your correspondence with others of your order secret, and to contact other sect members in your area. You can usually rely on these fellow Seekers to lend you their aid from time to time.
Additionally, you can be a member of any adventuring company and do not need to fulfill any membership prerequisites.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: Adaptive and bold, Seekers possess a well of pervasive hope. Sometimes painfully optimistic, they prefer to seek solutions rather than languish in defeat.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: I enjoy a good puzzle—maybe a little too much, in fact.
I find humor in things others find odd.
When people lie, I make a game out of finding out why they are lying.
I am quite fond of thought experiments and bring them up frequently.
I give my trust freely to others, but those who lose it will find it all but impossible to regain.
I have always been a bit dour.
People truly fascinate me. I can sit and watch them interact for hours.
I work well in a group and consider myself a team player.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Discovery. Experiencing new cultures, places, and events is the only correct path to salvation. (Any)
Collaboration. I seek to exchange information with knowledgeable individuals from all over the world. (Any)
Truth. The truth, no matter how uncomfortable, must be discovered and told. (Lawful)
Knowledge. Learning creates new possibilities all on its own. One can touch the divine purely by pondering the theoretical. (Neutral)
Journey. The journey itself and the connections I make will teach me what I need to know to better the lives of my people. (Chaotic)
Service. My many travels have taught me that service to others can teach you much about the world. (Good)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: I have grown rather close to a young student during my travels, and I return to this individual's university from time to time to check in.
I do not know what happened to my mentor, who was near Ios when tragedy struck. I aim to find out one day.
I was once part of a close-knit adventuring company but had to move on in order to continue my journey as a Seeker.
During my travels, I mistakenly angered a human noble, who still has it in for me.
I loved a fellow Seeker dearly but never revealed my feelings. I hope one day our paths will cross again.
My entire family is part of the Retribution. I long for the day when they will leave the group.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: I believe every situation—even personal ones—should be approached intellectually.
I am radically blunt and often point out people's flaws.
Those without a proper education are beneath me.
Once I find something new to learn, I can't stop until I understand it.
I can do no wrong. Should something go awry, it is certain to be someone else's fault.
I have begun to doubt that a solution to my people's plight can be found.

background.name: Spy

_: your choice of one set of tools (disguise kit, forgery kit, or thieves' tools)
a badly forged passport (a souvenir of your first attempt)

entries: You are an intelligencer, either a freelance operative or an agent of one of the Iron Kingdoms. Until recently, you've lived in a shadowy world of hidden truths and shifting alliances. From simple research to more direct means, you know numerous ways to gain the information your clients seek.

entries.name: Feature: Cover Identity
entries: In addition to establishing numerous cover identities for yourself, you have hidden troves of corroborating evidence in multiple cities across western Immoren. These stashes contain disguises, bogus documentation, forgery kits, and other useful items created for the purpose of proving that you are whoever you say you are. For each location you visit, you and your GM can discuss the likelihood that it contains one of these caches, as well as the difficulty of accessing it.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: Whether for purposes nefarious or benign, spies look to glean the truth of any matter laid before them. Trust doesn't come easy, if ever, but it can be the difference between life and death.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: I am a consummate flatterer.
Collecting and telling stories are two of the greatest tools in my arsenal.
Gambling is one of my vices, but I try my best to resist it while on the job.
I always have a plan. I'm a born schemer.
I often use humor to deflect attention.
My patience is nearly endless.
I fall in and out of love easily and always have my eye on my next paramour.
I sometimes find it hard not to be sarcastic.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Independence. The best part of being a spy is that I can operate with nearly complete independence. (Chaotic)
Dedication. Being a spy is the greatest part of my life. (Neutral)
Aspiration. I will use anything and everything at my disposal to move up in the world. (Any)
Nation. I owe absolute loyalty to my home country. (Any)
Creativity. Never tell the same lie twice. (Chaotic)
Deception. If you want to keep everyone on their toes, learn to lie well. If you have to tell a truth, make sure it's only a partial one. (Chaotic)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: I fell in love with someone while on duty but had to leave after my cover was blown.
Part of my agreement to become a spy was that my employer would take care of my sibling.
I was forced to commit a vile crime during one of my missions. No one must ever discover the truth.
I had to abandon a child in my care while pursuing one of my missions. I regret not making a different choice.
I will do anything to prove myself to my mentor.
The family of a powerful noble found out I was the one who revealed incriminating information about some unsavory activities. Despite losing most of their power, they still have it in for me.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: In my line of work, it is incredibly hard for me to trust anyone—even my allies.
I am at the apex of my craft. No one could ever fool me.
Living to fight another day sure beats dying and giving up the fight entirely.
I lie so often that the truth has become mostly irrelevant to me.
I'm always fretting that the other shoe will drop at any moment.
I can't resist a pretty face.

background.name: Temple Flameguard

_: a holy symbol of Menoth called the Menofix

entries: You are a true soldier of the Menite faith. Originally tasked with guarding a sacred temple, you have since marched to war, guided by the hand of the Creator and his priests.

entries.name: Feature: Fiery Courage
entries: Your years of service to the Temple have been filled with flame—both in the rituals of your belief and in the arms you carried into battle. While carrying a fiery item such as a torch or a flame spear, you have advantage on saving throws against being frightened, and effects caused by flames cannot frighten you.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: Faith honed to a razor's edge by a life spent in service has left little room for doubt in the minds of the Temple Flameguard. Quick to act, they know right from wrong, whether or not others agree.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: I have an uncanny knack for twisting every conversation to the subject of my faith.
I'm boisterous whenever I'm off duty, which is all the time now.
Scripture is my guide to most moral dilemmas.
I'm a serious person and have little time for those who aren't.
Once I set my mind to something, I do my best to achieve it.
I revere a particular hero of the Protectorate and often inject my idol into conversations.
I have always been an optimistic soul.
I see the world in black and white.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Duty. Even though I no longer serve the Protectorate directly, I still aim to better the lives of the faithful through my actions. (Lawful)
Power. I joined the Flameguard as a means of gaining strength and standing. (Evil)
Greater Good. I fight for the good for the many, not just the good of the church. (Good)
Faith. The Creator's tenets are sacrosanct and should be followed to the letter. (Lawful)
Ambition. I left the Flameguard in order to fulfill my great potential. (Any)
Vengeance. Realizing that I could not claim true vengeance against those who blaspheme the Creator, I struck out on my own to rectify this impiety. (Evil)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: The Claiming left me with only one family member, whose safety and happiness are very important to me.
During my service, I made friends with a superior officer who still holds a position in the Flameguard.
I left the Flameguard in order to recover an ancient relic.
I was left at a church as a baby. I still owe a debt to the clergy there for raising me.
Despite having left the church's direct service, I still follow any command it gives.
I left the Flameguard because an officer had it in for me. Time has clouded the reason for this conflict but has not lessened the officer's enmity.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: No one sees my true greatness; clearly, others are just jealous of me.
I judge everyone harshly, myself included.
I am so courageous that many call me foolish.
Violence is always the correct solution.
Those who do not show my faith the proper respect shall be punished.
I lost my faith years ago. Guilt gnaws at my heart, and I fear the gaze of the scrutators.

background.name: Trader
entries: You might have been a member of the Searforge Commission in charge of one of their trading posts outside of Rhul, or perhaps you spoke for a group of traveling gobber bodgers who hoped to pick up some odd jobs in the wake of passing armies. Whatever your past, you've always had a nose for a deal.

entries.name: Feature: Master Haggler
entries: Your business sense allows you to easily read markets and identify opportunities to make or save a little bit of coin. You have advantage on Charisma checks made while trying to establish the price of goods and services. Additionally, when setting out on a long journey, you can negotiate with a fellow trader either for passage in exchange for guarding a shipment or for information about which goods may be needed in your destination's markets. At the GM's discretion, if you know the destination of a significant journey, you can purchase any one type of common good in your starting location and sell it for a profit at your destination.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: Whether they are of noble or common birth, traders know how to interact with—and get the best deal from—nearly everyone. A careful balance of pragmatism and optimism helps ensure they're prepared for the next deal that comes their way.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: I am committed to fairness in all transactions, and I respect those who feel the same.
I love turning a phrase. No blade is sharper than my wit.
Those who can't appreciate the finer things in life are barely worth my time.
I take great delight in completing a transaction.
Learning what makes people tick is an important part of my profession.
If it's worth doing at all, it should be done right.
I am always upbeat. Few events, if any, can sour my mood.
I could go on forever when someone gets me talking about my profession.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Profit. Honestly, I'm just looking to get paid. (Neutral)
Honesty. I'll never cheat or swindle someone who offers me a fair deal. (Lawful)
Superiority. Pulling one over on someone else means I'm cleverer than they are. (Evil)
Rarity. Common baubles don't interest me. I'm looking for the most unusual things I can find. (Any)
Freedom. This life makes me the master of my destiny. (Chaotic)
A Bargain. I'm happiest when I know I've gotten the best possible deal on something—on anything, really. (Any)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: I have a deep hatred for a powerful noble who went back on a lucrative deal.
I work hard to provide for my family.
During the course of my dealings, I came across some scandalous information about a powerful person.
One of my trade deals went poorly. Even though it wasn't my fault, the other trader still has it in for me.
I hope that becoming wealthy will allow me to attain a specific person's love.
I am part of a merchant's guild that helped me a great deal when I was just getting started as a trader. I still feel I owe that organization a debt.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: I'm constantly saving for a rainy day, to the point of almost never spending money.
Acquiring a noble title is a dream I would kill for.
I never trust anyone in a transaction. Everyone else is out to cheat me until proven otherwise.
I have sticky fingers and often pocket small tokens or coins even if they have little or no value.
Risking my life for rare and priceless items is certainly not out of the question.
To me, money is equivalent to power. Amassing both is my ultimate goal.

background.name: Trencher

_: a small souvenir of a specific battlefield

entries: You've fought on the front lines of numerous conflicts and have spent many miserable hours hunkered down in a trench or a foxhole between firefights. These experiences have taught you not only the value of allies, but also the importance of looking out for yourself first if necessary.

entries.name: Feature: Sleep Anywhere
entries: You have years of practice falling asleep in the most uncomfortable positions, even when battle looms. If you choose to spend one or more Hit Dice to regain hit points at the end of a short rest, you regain the maximum number of hit points for the first Hit Die you spend.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: A mix of stoicism, grit, and sheer stubbornness keeps a trencher alive on the battlefield. Having been through the worst, they tend to be unflappable in bad situations.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: I have a story to match every situation I find myself in before a fight.
I lead the charge when a battle begins, providing encouragement to allies who might hesitate.
I gesticulate a lot when I talk.
I can be rather callous and often do not recognize how others are feeling.
When I'm down, I try to hide it by staying positive and upbeat.
I often use vulgar language and tell bawdy jokes.
I don't consider myself lazy, but I try to avoid work when I can.
When faced with a problem, I prefer a simple, straightforward solution.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Responsibility. I always do what I can to adhere to authority. (Lawful)
Comrades. I fight for those who stand next to me. (Good)
Might. At the end of the day, power rests with those whose forces are strongest. (Evil)
Nation. I fight for my nation above all else. (Any)
Ambition. Rising through the ranks is my paramount goal. (Any)
Independence. My time as a trencher has taught me that blindly following orders is a path to ruin. (Chaotic)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: A comrade died saving me on the field of battle, and I do my best to provide for the surviving spouse.
My unit was ambushed and defeated. I will never forgive the commanding officer who led us into the trap.
There is little I wouldn't do for those I served beside.
I fight in the hope that no one else will have to experience this level of calamity.
I fight for those who cannot fight for themselves.
One of my parents was a high-ranking military figure who still holds sway within the Corps.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: I react violently to those who question my courage.
There's nothing quite like the thrill of gambling.
Years of bitter war have left me with a blind hatred of my enemies.
My experiences on the battlefield have led me to believe that I am rarely wrong.
Memories of war often lead me to the bottom of a bottle.
I made a critical mistake that led to the deaths of several comrades. I will live with that shame for the rest of my life.

background.name: Vagabond

_: a small memento from your homeland

entries: In the last few decades, life in certain parts of the Iron Kingdoms has been irrevocably disrupted. Although many exiles found a new place to settle, some embraced the life of a wanderer and found themselves drifting from one adventure to the next.

entries.name: Feature: Danger Sense
entries: Your life as a wanderer began in the aftermath of an unfortunate event. You have advantage on initiative rolls. When you roll a 1 on a Wisdom (Perception) or Wisdom (Insight) check, you can reroll the die but must use the new roll.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: Whether they bemoan or celebrate the turn of events that led them to life on the road, vagabonds call wherever they are home. Adaptable and energetic, they are inspired by the hope that whatever they seek might be just over the next hill.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: I've seen it all. Nothing surprises me anymore.
I truly enjoy serving those in need.
I am keenly aware of how others are feeling.
My constant interactions with refugees have left me jaded.
I'm a daydreamer who often gets lost in potential futures.
I make friends quickly. For whatever reason, people tend to like me.
Having seen several different cultures now, I have great respect for different perspectives.
I'm always prepared for things to go wrong and am often the first to react.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Community. I work to better the lives of those who suffer as I have. (Good)
Tradition. Preserving the ways of the past keeps me going. (Lawful)
Reform. The world must change if we are to survive. (Chaotic)
Gloom. The world is a cruel and heartless place. I will do anything to survive it. (Evil)
Dignity. No matter what the circumstances, everyone deserves to be treated with dignity. (Neutral)
Liberty. I will maintain my freedom even if I have to sleep in a gutter. (Any)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: I lost everything after the Claiming, but I continue to search for my long-lost sibling.
For whatever reason, I keep coming across the same antagonist.
Staying on the move is the only thing that brings me solace anymore.
I always do what I can to help those who are lost.
One day, I hope to thank the soldier who saved my life.
My journey has become one of self-improvement.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: I have dark thoughts of eventually getting my revenge on all who look down on me.
I am easily distracted by minor mysteries.
A life on the road has made me slow to trust others.
I tend to overindulge in all kinds of intoxicants.
I don't respect anyone who hasn't had a life as hard as mine.
I believe in throwing caution to the wind and living life to its fullest.

background.name: Winter Guard

_: a trophy taken from an enemy (a dagger, broken weapon, or tarnished medal)

entries: Having failed to prove yourself as capable as the best conscripts your age, you spent your required years of service to the Motherland in the Khadoran Winter Guard. As a soldier, you engaged the Motherland's enemies on the front lines, where you formed lasting bonds of fellowship and learned of numerous possibilities beyond the township of your birth.

entries.name: Feature: Bond of Service
entries: Every Khadoran town and village is filled with former veterans and trained military personnel. You have advantage on Charisma (Persuasion) and Charisma (Intimidation) checks when interacting with current or former members of the Winter Guard. In addition, you can call on aid from older veterans when times are tough. Your GM determines the amount and extent of any aid other Winter Guard provide.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: Veterans of the Winter Guard are proud and notoriously durable. Service is a badge of honor they carry with them into many situations, even long after they've served.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: My friends know that they can rely on me.
I am haunted by the memories of all the comrades I have lost.
The horrors of war have left me nearly fearless.
I am always polite and respectful.
I try to insert my war stories into as many conversations as possible.
Once I'm off duty, I'm the life of the party.
I enjoy making crude jokes.
I always prefer to respond in a calm, measured manner.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Protection. I use my talents to protect the vulnerable. (Good)
Obedience. Following the orders of those above us keeps us all safe. (Lawful)
Variety. I approach each task as a new opportunity to prove myself. (Chaotic)
Comrades. I am committed to those who fight by my side, not just to ideals. (Neutral)
Ambition. By rising in rank, I can wield more power over others. (Evil)
Uniformity. A simple life is its own reward. (Any)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: Before joining the Winter Guard, I was a mess. I owe a debt to my superior officer for whipping me into shape.
I fight so that others don't have to.
An enemy once spared my life. I hope to meet that person again one day.
My comrades in arms are worth dying for.
I became close to a fellow Winter Guard but never confessed my attraction.
Honor is the most important thing in my life.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: My experiences on the battlefield have left me skittish.
Those who have never experienced a harsh winter cannot be trusted.
I'm quick to anger and slow to forgiveness.
I always try to avoid responsibility.
I require a very regimented lifestyle.
As a child, I overheard many terrible stories told by traveling sages. I still have nightmares about those tales.

background.name: Arcane Order
entries: {@b Adventuring Company.} The Iron Kingdoms are home to many guilds and orders that exist to promote the arcane arts. This company might represent a small chapter house of a larger organization or be the sole members of a smaller order.

entries.name: Prerequisites
entries: Any character can belong to an Arcane Order company, but it must include at least one character with the Spellcasting or Pact Magic feature. Those without are assumed to be guards and skilled experts in the order's employ.

entries.name: Downtime: Formula Experimentation
entries: If you have the Spellcasting, Warcaster Magic, or Pact Magic feature, you can spend downtime experimenting with modified arcane formulas. You gain your choice of a bonus to your next cantrip damage roll or advantage on Intelligence (Arcana) checks for a number of days equal to the number of days spent studying, up to your Intelligence modifier (minimum 1 day).

entries.name: Company Accomplishments
entries: Prestige increases through acts such as: using arcane magic to protect a notable figure; recovering a rare or ancient book of arcane lore; discovering a unique magic item; destroying an infernal, powerful grymkin, or undead creature; developing a powerful new spell; or fabricating a mechanikal item worth at least 1,500 gp.

entries.name: Tier 1 – Local Wizards
entries: {@b Guild House.} Your company begins with a small guild house including servants' quarters, a stable, a kitchen, a great hall, a meeting room, bedchambers, guest rooms, and an alchemical workshop.

{@b Knowledge of the Arcane.} You can replace one proficiency you gain from your race or background with proficiency in the Arcana skill. If you are a spellcaster, you learn one additional cantrip from your spell lists.

entries.name: Tier 2 – Skilled Spellcasters
entries: {@b Assistants and Retainers.} You gain {@dice 1d6} hirelings (CR 1/4 or lower) as guards, servants, apprentices, or research assistants.

{@b Research Library.} You gain access to a library for arcane research. You can spend 8 hours copying arcane formulas to: replace one known 1st-level spell with another; copy a 1st-level spell into a spellbook; produce {@dice 1d4} cantrip spell scrolls; or learn one mechanika rune with a rune point cost of 3 or lower.

entries.name: Tier 3 – Arcane Authorities
entries: {@b Improved Research Library.} You can now replace or reproduce spells of 3rd level or lower, produce {@dice 1d4} 1st-level spell scrolls, or learn one mechanika rune with a rune point cost of 3 or lower.
{@b Respected Arcanists.} You gain a +2 bonus to Charisma (Persuasion) checks when interacting with other practitioners of the arcane.

entries.name: Tier 4 – Masters of Magic
entries: {@b Advanced Research Library.} You can now replace or reproduce spells of 5th level or lower, produce {@dice 1d4} 3rd-level spell scrolls, or learn one 5th-level mechanika rune.
{@b The Great and Terrible.} You can replace Charisma (Intimidation) or Charisma (Persuasion) checks with Intelligence (Arcana) checks when interacting with others.

background.name: Cult Assembly
entries: {@b Adventuring Company.} You are part of a group devoted to an unpopular, or perhaps dangerous, deity—remnants of an infernalist cult, adherents to an aspect of the Devourer Wurm, fringe worshippers of Cyriss, or devotees of one of the Defiers of the Wicked Harvest.

entries.name: Prerequisites
entries: Any character can belong to a Cult Assembly company, but it must include at least one character proficient in the Religion skill.

entries.name: Downtime: Indoctrination Rituals
entries: By spending at least 5 days in intense religious rituals, you can indoctrinate attendees into your beliefs. At the end of this time, your company earns {@dice 2d6} new followers who are commoners devoted to the cult's leaders. Followers pay 1 gp each day to the cult and obey the orders of the cult's leadership.

entries.name: Company Accomplishments
entries: Prestige increases through acts such as: inducting an influential person into your cult; defeating a rival organization; recovering a relic or religious text important to your deity; performing a significant ritual; witnessing a manifestation of your god; or consecrating a place of significance.

entries.name: Tier 1 – Fringe Cult
entries: {@b Life in the Shadows.} Your company can easily find a safe place in slums, abandoned warehouses, derelict factories, and similar locations to convene and practice your faith.
{@b Coded Speech.} You can replace one language you gain from your race or background with a unique language based on your worship.

entries.name: Tier 2 – Devoted Following
entries: {@b Acolytes.} You gain {@dice 2d10} new members (CR 1/4 or lower) who serve the cult as aides, guards, and messengers and attend important rituals.

entries.name: Tier 3 – Keepers of the Faith
entries: {@b Faithful Masses.} You can expect a 10 percent discount on goods and services from vendors in your local area. Additionally, you gain {@dice 2d10} acolytes with a challenge rating of 1/2 or lower.
{@b Tithes.} Each week, you receive {@dice 1d6} gp per class level as a tithe from the faithful.

entries.name: Tier 4 – Eye of the Gods
entries: {@b Devotion.} You and all members of your cult gain advantage on saving throws against being charmed or frightened. You also gain advantage on Charisma (Persuasion) checks made to convince cult members to follow your commands.

background.name: Intrepid Investigators
entries: {@b Adventuring Company.} You are part of a group that seeks a greater understanding of the mysteries of western Immoren—explorers, ghost hunters, tomb robbers, or perhaps an expedition from Corvis University.

entries.name: Prerequisites
entries: Any character can belong, but the company must include at least one character with the Explorer, Investigator, or Seeker background, or proficiency in Investigation or Survival.

entries.name: Downtime: Exhaustive Research
entries: Before an adventure, you can spend time in archives and libraries uncovering pertinent accounts (cost: {@dice 1d6} gp per day). After 3 days, each member of your company gains one Education die (a d6). Once during the adventure, you can roll the die and add the number to one ability check, attack roll, or saving throw you make, after rolling but before the GM reveals the result.

entries.name: Company Accomplishments
entries: Prestige increases through acts such as: discovering an ancient ruin; recovering ancient treasure or an artifact worth at least 1,000 gp; observing a dangerous animal up close; confronting a powerful undead creature or grymkin; solving an antiquated mystery; or using specialized knowledge to save someone's life.

entries.name: Tier 1 – Fledgling Adventurers
entries: {@b Linguistics.} You can replace one language from your race or background with an ancient or uncommon language (e.g., ancient Caspian, Khurzic, Old Morridane, Orgoth, or a Molgur dialect), including reading and writing it.
{@b An Eye for the Abnormal.} You gain a +2 bonus to passive Wisdom (Perception) checks to notice hidden entrances, ruins, ancient inscriptions, and similar elements.

{@b Expedition Opportunities.} You begin the game with a network of scholars and travelers. If your company agrees to join an expedition, each member receives 50 gp for equipment and supplies. New expedition opportunities arrive from your network once every {@dice 1d10} days.

entries.name: Tier 2 – Notable Explorers
entries: {@b Outfitters.} You receive a 10 percent discount on adventuring gear, ammunition, equipment packs, tools, and mounts and vehicles.

{@b Sponsorship.} A sponsor (noble patron, university, or religious organization) establishes an expense account of 100 gp per month. Any unspent gold is lost at month's end. In return, your sponsor receives monthly reports and first chance to purchase significant artifacts.

entries.name: Tier 3 – Famous Investigators
entries: {@b Applied Knowledge.} Members of the company gain advantage on Dexterity saving throws to avoid traps or environmental hazards (not if blinded, deafened, or incapacitated).
{@b Noteworthy Investigators.} You gain a +2 bonus to Charisma (Persuasion) checks when interacting with explorers or wilderness tribes.

entries.name: Tier 4 – Masters of Discovery
entries: {@b Improved Sponsorship.} Your sponsor increases your expense account to 500 gp per month.
{@b Remarkable Accomplishments.} You can replace Charisma (Intimidation) or Charisma (Persuasion) checks with Intelligence (Investigation) or Wisdom (Survival) checks.

background.name: Law Dogs
entries: {@b Adventuring Company.} You and your fellow characters are professional law enforcers and bounty hunters who make your living tracking the most dangerous criminals, dismantling criminal enterprises, and putting a stop to nefarious activities.

entries.name: Prerequisites
entries: Any character can belong, but the company must include at least one character proficient in Investigation or Insight.

entries.name: Downtime: Uphold the Law
entries: You can patrol the streets during downtime, reassuring locals and deterring lawbreakers. For a number of days equal to the days spent, you gain advantage on Charisma (Persuasion) checks to gain information from local citizens.

entries.name: Company Accomplishments
entries: Prestige increases through acts such as: foiling a criminal plot; placing a known criminal into custody; recovering stolen goods; preventing an innocent person from being wrongfully punished; resolving a case with irrefutable evidence; or protecting the innocent and upholding the law.

entries.name: Tier 1 – Rookies
