entries: {@b Badges.} You have a badge or writ of authority giving you the legal right to enforce laws, take captives, and interrogate suspects.
{@b Precinct House.} You have access to a minor precinct house with a small barracks, a holding cell, and a secure storage room. You are not expected to pay to maintain it but do not own it outright.

entries.name: Tier 2 – Law Enforcers
entries: {@b Armory.} You have access to an armory affiliated with a major law-enforcing body. It contains adventuring gear and weapons worth 25 gp or less. You can requisition items but are responsible for their care and must return them after completing a case.
{@b Fearful Reputation.} You gain a +2 bonus on Charisma (Intimidation) checks involving lawbreakers and their associates.

entries.name: Tier 3 – Peacekeepers
entries: {@b Broad Authority.} You have complete access to the facilities of any city watch or garrison while pursuing a criminal target.

{@b Request Backup.} While pursuing a criminal, you can request {@dice 1d6} city watch plus one watch commander, or one laborjack. These forces remain under your command for 24 hours or until a task is completed. You cannot request backup again for 24 hours after using this benefit.

entries.name: Tier 4 – Justiciars
entries: {@b Legal Immunity.} Your company can operate with near impunity. Barring extraordinary circumstances, you are immune to reprisal for actions that occur during your pursuit of a criminal. Abuse of this privilege could lead to its revocation.

background.name: Mercenary Charter
entries: {@b Adventuring Company.} You and your fellow characters represent a recognized mercenary company with a charter sanctioned by most nations and authorities in the Iron Kingdoms.

entries.name: Prerequisites
entries: Any character can belong to a Mercenary Charter company.

entries.name: Downtime: Guard Duty
entries: Between contracts, you can earn extra funds by keeping watch over a client's property. For each day spent on guard duty, each member receives {@dice 2d4} gp.

entries.name: Company Accomplishments
entries: Prestige increases through acts such as: winning a battle against overwhelming odds; defending a village, stronghold, or military asset; negotiating a favorable contract; acquiring a warjack; destroying a warjack under enemy control; or capturing an enemy commander.

entries.name: Tier 1 – Freelance Fighters
entries: {@b Mercenary Charter.} Your company holds a mercenary charter that outlines conduct, looting, and contract obligations. Common stipulations include: civilians must not come to undue harm; loot is distributed evenly; no obligation to follow laws that violate natural law; 30-day non-compete clause after completing a contract.
{@b Military Supplier.} You can purchase weapons and armor at a 10 percent discount.

{@b Contracts.} Members can hit the streets to gather information about potential contracts (downtime activity, cost: {@dice 1d4} gp per day, takes at least {@dice 1d4} days to find a lead). Tier 1 contracts are worth up to 200 gp.

entries.name: Tier 2 – Soldiers of Fortune
entries: {@b Fresh Recruits.} You gain {@dice 1d10} hirelings (CR 1/4 or lower) to act as privates and support staff.
{@b Garrison.} Your company gains a modest chapter house with a barracks, mechanik's workshop, muster field, smithy, and stables. You can maintain a modest lifestyle there at no expense.

entries.name: Tier 3 – Professional Soldiers
entries: {@b Better Negotiators.} When beginning negotiations for a new contract, one character can barter for improved pay with a Charisma (Persuasion) check (DC 12 + 2 for each 100 gp over standard pay). Success means the extra gold is paid as an advance.
{@b Improved Recruits.} You gain your choice of {@dice 1d10} hirelings (CR 1 or lower) or {@dice 1d4} command staff (CR 2 or lower).

entries.name: Tier 4 – Lords of War
entries: {@b Fearsome Reputations.} You gain a +2 bonus on Charisma (Intimidation) checks involving enemy forces and Charisma (Persuasion) checks involving friendly forces.
{@b Superior Recruits.} You gain your choice of {@dice 1d10} hirelings (CR 2 or lower) or {@dice 1d4} command staff (CR 4 or lower).

background.name: Outlaws
entries: {@b Adventuring Company.} Crime does pay, but sometimes in blood. You and your fellow characters are members of a close-knit gang of criminals, ruffians, and thugs motivated by the promise of coin or blood for blood's sake.

entries.name: Prerequisites
entries: Each member must have at least one of: the Criminal or Kayazy background; proficiency in Deception, Intimidation, Sleight of Hand, or Stealth; or proficiency with thieves' tools.

entries.name: Downtime: Crime Spree
entries: You can launch a reckless spree of thieving, picking pockets, looting, and hijacking goods. For each day spent, each member rolls their choice of {@dice d4}, {@dice d6}, {@dice d8}, or {@dice d10} and earns 10 gp × the result in stolen goods. If any die roll is a 1, or if the total of all rolls exceeds the character's Wisdom modifier + proficiency bonus, that member draws the attention of local law enforcement and is incarcerated for 1 day per 10 gp worth of stolen goods.

entries.name: Company Accomplishments
entries: Prestige increases through acts such as: destroying a rival gang and claiming its territory; breaking someone out of prison; stealing an item worth at least 1,000 gp; hijacking a steamboat, train, or military vehicle; or smuggling illegal goods worth at least 1,000 gp.

entries.name: Tier 1 – Local Hoods
entries: {@b Hideout.} Your company begins with a hideout watched over by gang affiliates. The hideout has a meeting area, administrative office, sleeping chambers, hidden cells, and several secret exits.
{@b Five Cant.} You can replace one language from your race or background with Five Cant.
{@b Criminal Info.} You can gather information about potential targets (downtime activity, cost: {@dice 1d4} gp per day, takes at least {@dice 1d4} days). Tier 1 targets are worth up to 100 gp.

entries.name: Tier 2 – Criminal Outfit
entries: {@b Improved Criminal Info.} Targets increase in value to up to 200 gp. You may also learn about alternative entry methods, guard numbers, and other information.
{@b Protection Racket.} Local vendors provide a steady income of 100 gp in protection money each month.
{@b New Recruits.} You gain {@dice 1d4} criminal hirelings (CR 1/4 or lower) to gather information, shake down businesses, or take the fall for your crimes.

entries.name: Tier 3 – Underworld Bosses
entries: {@b Eyes on the Street.} Criminal info arrives daily; citizens of your territory treat gang members like nobles; protection money increases to 200 gp per month.
{@b Additional Hideouts.} Lesser criminal operations provide safe and secure hideouts (equivalent to the tier 1 Hideout benefit) wherever you travel within your territory.
{@b Better Recruits.} Your number of hirelings increases by {@dice 1d4}, and hirelings can have CR 1 or lower.

entries.name: Tier 4 – Crime Syndicate
entries: {@b Gang Tithes.} Lesser gangs pay a percentage of their scores to you, amounting to 500 gp each month.
{@b Premier Recruits.} Your number of hirelings increases by {@dice 2d4}, and these NPCs can have CR 2 or lower.

background.name: Pirate Crew
entries: {@b Adventuring Company.} Whether pirates or privateers, you and your fellow characters are the ranking officers of a small frigate. You might be criminals of the sea, pillaging and looting the coasts of the Iron Kingdoms, or officially recognized privateers bearing a letter of marque.

entries.name: Prerequisites
entries: Any character can be a member, but at least one member must be proficient with vehicles (water).

entries.name: Downtime: Smuggle Cargo
entries: You can earn extra finances by smuggling illicit cargo or passengers. This activity requires at least 3 days finding an employer and securing cargo, plus travel days to the destination. For each day of travel, each member earns {@dice 1d10} gp.

entries.name: Company Accomplishments
entries: Prestige increases through acts such as: plundering at least 1,000 gp in goods from a merchant vessel; navigating a dangerous storm; defeating a significant beast of the sea; stealing a navigational rudder from a rival captain; surviving a battle against a military vessel; or plundering a coastal village.

entries.name: Tier 1 – Local Corsairs
entries: {@b Any Port in a Storm.} Your company knows a network of hidden pirate lairs and smuggling ports along the coast.
{@b Five Cant.} You can replace one language from your race or background with Five Cant.
{@b Starter Vessel.} Your company has a small keelboat crewed by three loyal sailors (commoner stat block). One or more systems has been damaged to half total hit points.

entries.name: Tier 2 – Privateers
entries: {@b Letter of Marque.} Your company gains a letter of marque authorizing you to take rival vessels as prizes. For each rival vessel defeated in battle, your company earns 1 prestige.

{@b Variant – Hostis Humani Generis.} Rather than taking a letter of marque, you can choose to be true pirates. For each vessel your crew defeats in battle, you earn 1 prestige and gain a +1 bonus to Charisma (Intimidation) checks. However, you are subject to legal consequences and are not welcomed in civilized ports.
{@b Sea Dogs.} You gain {@dice 1d4} hirelings (CR 1/4 or lower) for boarding actions and coastal raids.

entries.name: Tier 3 – Infamous Marauders
entries: {@b Dread Pirates.} While flying your colors, any creature not part of your crew who recognizes your flag must make a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw or be frightened of all crew members for 10 minutes. A creature can repeat the save at the start of each of its turns.
{@b Better Recruits.} Your number of hirelings increases by {@dice 1d4}, and hirelings can have CR 1 or lower.

entries.name: Tier 4 – Sea Reavers
entries: {@b Commodores.} When launching a pirate raid, you can call upon {@dice 1d4} keelboats each crewed by {@dice 1d10+10} NPCs (CR 1 or lower). These vessels accompany your crew for the duration before looting what plunder they can.
{@b Premier Recruits.} Your number of hirelings increases by {@dice 2d4}, and these NPCs can have CR 2 or lower.

background.name: Spy Ring
entries: {@b Adventuring Company.} You and the other members of your party are intelligencers employed by a kingdom, major organization, or private concern—perhaps members of the Cygnaran Reconnaissance Service, agents of a noble house, or simply freelancers in the employ of the highest bidder.

entries.name: Prerequisites
entries: Each member must have the Greylords Covenant, Investigator, Resistance Veteran, Spy, or Vagabond background, or proficiency in Deception or Stealth.

entries.name: Downtime: New Identity
entries: If a cover identity becomes compromised, you can spend 7 days establishing new cover identities for all members. You purchase forged documents, develop cover stories, and seed the local region with rumors. Cost: 25 gp per member. Each member receives a plausible cover with appropriate documentation.

entries.name: Company Accomplishments
entries: Prestige increases through acts such as: blackmailing someone beholden to a rival power; forging documents to misdirect opponents; uncovering a valuable secret known only to foes; infiltrating the inner circle of your target's trust; rescuing a captured ally; or sabotaging enemy assets worth at least 1,000 gp.

entries.name: Tier 1 – Infiltrators
entries: {@b Cover Story.} Your employer has set up your company with a credible cover and all documentation required for travel. Any papers you carry are forgeries sufficient to pass a cursory inspection.
{@b Expense Account.} You have access to a company account containing 50 gp per member, replenished at the start of each month. Any money not spent by month's end is reclaimed.

entries.name: Tier 2 – Operatives
entries: {@b Safe Houses.} Your employer sets up a network of safe houses with strong locks, stout doors, and at least one back entrance for quick escape.
{@b Contact Network.} You learn the identity of multiple contacts in your region who can provide intelligence, act as getaway drivers, and help find alternative routes. Contacts have CR 2 or lower.

entries.name: Tier 3 – Espionage Experts
entries: {@b Advanced Intel.} When you gain intelligence from your contact network, you can request specific information about a target. Depending on quality, you gain a +1 or +2 bonus on related ability checks and saving throws.
{@b Increased Expenses.} Your expense account increases by 50 gp per member per month.

entries.name: Tier 4 – Spymasters
entries: {@b Black Market.} Your company finds a supplier of black-market goods. You receive a 10 percent discount on illegal or difficult-to-obtain items and the vendor purchases stolen goods at three-quarters of their value.

{@b Everyone's Secrets.} If you spend at least 8 hours surveilling a location or shadowing a target, you learn one piece of information your target would prefer to keep undisclosed. You can use this to gain advantage on Charisma (Persuasion) or Charisma (Intimidation) contests against that target.

backgroundFluff.name: Explorer
entries: You've charted paths through the inhospitable jungles of Alchiere and the trackless wastes of the frozen north to fill in the blank areas on the map. You are more at home in a freshly unsealed Orgoth tomb than in the bustling streets of a major city. You've been lost more times than you can count but have always found your way back to civilization.

backgroundFluff.name: Order of Wizardry
entries: Widely known and respected as an institution for training those of arcane aptitude, the Order of Wizardry has been home to you and many other mages eager to progress along the established ranks of their craft, learn the secrets of wielding and controlling spells, and apply those skills to the benefit of their nations and the world at large.

backgroundFluff.name: Greylords Covenant
entries: You are a member of the Greylords Covenant, the arcane branch of both the Khadoran Army and the Khadoran government. As such, you've worked alongside accomplished arcanists, patriots, and soldiers in one of the most formidable occult organizations in western Immoren.

backgroundFluff.name: Investigator
entries: You are a skilled detective adept in the techniques of deduction, criminology, and interrogation. In the past, you may have been a watch inspector who used your abilities to catch criminals and keep your city safe, a former inquisitor, or a member of a military intelligence-gathering organization who ferreted out the secrets of your nation's enemies, both foreign and domestic.

backgroundFluff.name: Iron Fang
entries: You are a trained Iron Fang and were recently part of the mighty legions of Khador. Your grueling training and battlefield experience have made you dauntless and have inured you to the hardships of battle. Skilled at formation fighting, you feel most comfortable in action when allies are close at hand and you can watch each other's backs.

backgroundFluff.name: Kayazy
entries: You are related to one of the kayazy, Khador's infamous "merchant princes." By tradition, members of the kayazy are leaders of both legitimate commercial industry and the criminal underworld, something they perceive as a natural and inevitable extension of their role in mercantile and industrial business. Whether or not you are related by blood to your family's leader, you are a valued member of the organization and can expect to draw support from it when needed.

backgroundFluff.name: Knight Exemplar
entries: You are one of the elite armored warriors of the Menite faith. You live by a strict code of absolute obedience to the Menite Temple's priests, especially those in the scrutator caste, and follow every order without question or hesitation. You fight with a faith that borders on the inhuman. Able to withstand punishment that would cripple those of lesser faith, you are made nearly unstoppable by the divine gifts bestowed on you by the Creator.

backgroundFluff.name: Knights of the Prophet
entries: You are a member of one of the largest orders of knights serving the Church of Morrow. Although you are not forbidden from defending your nation, you have taken solemn oaths to protect and preserve the church itself above all else. You may have felt called to join, or you may have been knighted as a notable defender of the faith and a pious warrior who acted on your initiative in times of great peril. You've frequently been dispatched to protect Morrowan temples in war-torn regions, where you were required to remain neutral in the battles taking place around you unless they threatened the sacred sites you were sent to protect.

backgroundFluff.name: Labor Boss
entries: Before your life as an adventurer, you led laborers on the docks of a port city or in the heart of a factory district. You have experience directing the activities of those under you, even when they would rather be in charge, and you have an eye for the essential parts of a task.

backgroundFluff.name: Mercenary
entries: The long history of bloody conflict in the Iron Kingdoms has created an endless opportunity for those willing to commit violence for coin. War is so ubiquitous across western Immoren, it is only natural that a certain breed approaches it like any other trade. You are one of these mercenaries: a soldier of fortune generally loyal only to whichever paymaster is currently filling your pockets. Where war thrives, you inevitably follow.

backgroundFluff.name: Northern Crusade
entries: The Protectorate of Menoth launched the Northern Crusade at the same time that Hierarch Voyle declared the nation's independence from Cygnar. You may have been in the initial wave of soldiers who marched hundreds of miles through the Bloodstone Marches, then into Cygnar, and finally into eastern Llael. Or perhaps you were one of the faithful thousands who joined the Crusade in the years of the occupation, fighting to spread the truth of the Lawgiver to the faithless. No matter when you joined the Crusade, you were there when it ended. You were left behind when the bulk of the army marched south to protect Tower Judgement from skorne invaders, and you witnessed the slow decline of the Protectorate's presence in Llael, the horrors of the Claiming, and—finally—the restoration of Llael's independence. You've been left without a purpose, and your faith is nearly shattered.

backgroundFluff.name: Privateer
entries: You are a privateer, buccaneer, or pirate. Although your victims make no distinction, you have always prided yourself on having a code that sets you apart from the common raiders on the high seas and rivers of western Immoren. You may have gained your skills and reputation as a crew member on an infamous ship or as part of a legitimate mercantile or naval force. Whether you served with the Mercarian League, sailed aboard a Cygnaran or Khadoran warship, or scavenged trading routes as part of a ruthless pirate crew, you learned your lessons well. You're a deadly close-quarters combatant who can manage a shipboard gun in a pinch. Lately, you've plied your trade more legitimately. Issued a letter of marque from one of the Iron Kingdoms, you've most recently hunted and captured the ships of enemy nations or fought your former fellows as they preyed upon merchant ships.

backgroundFluff.name: Resistance Veteran
entries: Whether you were a loyal citizen of Llael or a Cygnaran soldier fighting for Llael's independence, you were trapped in southeast Llael after the fall of the capital. You spent many long, bleak years fighting but never gave up the dream of restoring the nation. You performed some dark deeds while the country was divided, but you did what had to be done in order to hold on to what little of Llael was still free.

backgroundFluff.name: Retribution of Scyrah
entries: You are one of the Iosan zealots who believe that human arcanists' eradication will restore your gods. Whether you were a lifelong believer in the cause or one of the thousands who joined the movement more recently, your devotion is certain. You may have been part of the Retribution's newly gathered army in Ios or one of the far-flung agents who worked in human lands for centuries, but no matter how you served, you believe fighting is better than accepting extinction.

backgroundFluff.name: Seeker
entries: Driven by the goal of rectifying the troubles that plague your people, you are a member of a minority religious sect and movement among some Iosans who voluntarily live as exiles from Ios. Unlike those in the Retribution, you believe in finding answers rather than assigning blame or seeking vengeance, and your search for a solution to whatever mysterious ailment has afflicted the goddess Scyrah has taken you far from home. You cling to the hope that the Vanished are awaiting rediscovery somewhere beyond Ios' borders, and you remain optimistic that engaging in active investigation abroad and developing strong alliances with knowledgeable individuals of other races will help you find a remedy for the current state of your race still in Ios.

backgroundFluff.name: Spy
entries: You are an intelligencer, either a freelance operative or an agent of one of the Iron Kingdoms. Until recently, you've lived in a shadowy world of hidden truths and shifting alliances. From simple research to more direct means, you know numerous ways to gain the information your clients seek.

backgroundFluff.name: Temple Flameguard
entries: You are a true soldier of the Menite faith. Originally tasked with guarding a sacred temple, you have since marched to war, guided by the hand of the Creator and his priests. Although you no longer serve directly as a member of the Protectorate army, you are still true to your faith and the will of the priesthood.

backgroundFluff.name: Trader
entries: You might have been a member of the Searforge Commission in charge of one of their trading posts outside of Rhul, or perhaps you spoke for a group of traveling gobber bodgers who hoped to pick up some odd jobs in the wake of passing armies. Whatever your past, you've always had a nose for a deal and the ability to out-haggle almost anyone you've come across.

backgroundFluff.name: Trencher
entries: You've fought on the front lines of numerous conflicts and have spent many miserable hours hunkered down in a trench or a foxhole between firefights. These experiences have taught you not only the value of allies, but also the importance of looking out for yourself first if necessary.

backgroundFluff.name: Vagabond
entries: In the last few decades, life in certain parts of the Iron Kingdoms has been irrevocably disrupted. A great tragedy befell the entire Nyss homeland, driving many of the survivors south as refugees. Lands that trollkin kriels had called home for centuries were destroyed by war and the greed of human nations. More recently, thousands of members of every race have had to pack up their belongings and flee as hordes of infernals swept across the lands. Although many of these exiles found a new place to settle, some embraced the life of a wanderer and found themselves drifting from one adventure to the next.

backgroundFluff.name: Winter Guard
entries: Having failed to prove yourself as capable as the best conscripts your age, you spent your required years of service to the Motherland in the Khadoran Winter Guard. As a soldier, you engaged the Motherland's enemies on the front lines, where you formed lasting bonds of fellowship and learned of numerous possibilities beyond the township of your birth. You are grateful for the experience but are glad it's all behind you now.

backgroundFluff.name: Arcane Order
entries: The Iron Kingdoms are home to many guilds and orders that exist to promote the arcane arts and esoteric sciences. You belong to one such group, which might represent a small chapter house of a larger organization or be the sole members of a smaller order.

entries.name: Prerequisites
entries: Any character can belong to an Arcane Order company, but it must include at least one character with the Spellcasting or Pact Magic feature. Those with the Spellcasting or Pact Magic feature are full members of the organization with an equal vote on decisions pertaining to the good of the order or chapter house. Those who are not spellcasters are assumed to be guards and skilled experts in the order's employ.

entries.name: Formula Experimentation
entries: If you have the Spellcasting, Warcaster Magic, or Pact Magic feature, you can spend your downtime experimenting with modified versions of known arcane formulas. If you do, you gain your choice of a bonus to your next cantrip damage roll or advantage on Intelligence (Arcana) checks for a number of days equal to the number of days spent studying, up to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of 1 day).

entries.name: Company Accomplishments
entries: Your company's prestige increases whenever you prove your arcane supremacy through acts such as the following: • Using arcane magic to protect a notable figure • Recovering a rare or ancient book of arcane lore • Discovering a unique magic item • Destroying an unnatural threat such as an infernal or a powerful grymkin or undead creature • Developing a powerful new spell or rediscovering a forgotten one • Fabricating a mechanikal item worth at least 1,500 gp TIER 1: LOCAL WIZARDS At this tier, your company is a small independent order or a small local chapter of a larger group such as the Order of Wizardry or the Greylords Covenant. You have limited influence in the world of the arcane and might be just starting out in your pursuit of esoteric knowledge.

entries.name: Benefits
entries: Guild House. Your company begins the game with a small guild house that includes servants' quarters, a stable, a kitchen, a great hall, a meeting room, bedchambers for the members, guest rooms, and an alchemical workshop. Knowledge of the Arcane. You can replace one proficiency

backgroundFluff.name: Cult Assembly
entries: Not all belief systems in western Immoren are viewed equally. You are part of a group devoted to an unpopular, or perhaps dangerous, deity. Your organization could be the remnant of an infernalist cult that remains hidden in the shadows, adherents to an aspect of the Devourer Wurm, fringe worshippers of Cyriss, or perhaps devotees of one of the Defiers of the Wicked Harvest. Your cult might be a fringe group of a dominant faith, or you might be the only known worshippers of your deity. Many cults operate at the edges of society, working in secret to grow their power and swell their ranks as they pay obeisance to the figure they worship. In return, some of the cult's inner circle receive blessings from their unusual gods.

entries.name: Prerequisites
entries: Any character can belong to a Cult Assembly company, but the company must include at least one character proficient in the Religion skill.

entries.name: Indoctrination Rituals
entries: By spending multiple days in intense religious rituals, you can indoctrinate attendees into your beliefs and cause. Indoctrination requires at least 5 days spent praying, chanting, and performing obeisance to your cult's deity. At the end of this time, your company earns 2d6 new followers. These followers are commoners devoted to the cult's leaders. Followers pay 1 gp apiece to the cult each day from their meager wages and obey the orders of the cult's leadership to the best of their abilities, with some even willing to lay down their lives for the cult.

entries.name: Company Accomplishments
entries: Your company's prestige increases whenever you prove your faith and devotion through acts such as the following: • Inducting an influential person into your cult • Defeating a rival organization that wants to destroy your cult • Recovering a relic or religious text important to your deity • Performing a significant ritual • Witnessing a manifestation of your god in action • Consecrating a place of significance to your deity TIER 1: FRINGE CULT At this tier, your cult is a small but devoted group of individuals. Gathering in back rooms and dim alleys to hold your services, you have yet to curry the favor of your deity and must keep your organization secret in order to avoid the attention of those who fear your ultimate goals.

entries.name: Benefits
entries: Life in the Shadows. Your company operates in the shadows, away from the prying eyes of those who find your beliefs unsavory and dangerous, but you can easily find a safe place in slums, abandoned warehouses, derelict factories, and similar locations to convene and practice your faith.

entries.name: Quirks
entries: Many adventuring companies have quirks that set them apart from other such groups. Choose a quirk for your adventuring company, or roll on the table below. important ritual whenever one of the moons is full. masks that depict an aspect of your deity in order to conceal your identities during gatherings. a symbol that represents your devotion. Examples include ceremonial weaponry, consecrated talismans, and concealed tattoos. threatening not only to undermine its efforts, but also to reveal its members to local authorities. temple to your god. foremost among your god's worshippers and are willing to absorb or destroy all other sects you encounter. night. Holding religious services during the day is considered blasphemy. competing halves that trade leadership and responsibilities based on their accomplishments. arduous physical, mental, or spiritual ordeal before being initiated as members. partake of a particular food, drink, or activity.

entries.name: Character Options
entries: Coded Speech. You and your fellow cultists share a language known only to each other and other members of your faith.

backgroundFluff.name: Law Dogs
entries: You and your fellow characters are professional law enforcers and bounty hunters who make your living tracking the most dangerous criminals and army deserters, dismantling criminal enterprises, and putting a stop to the nefarious activities of outlawed organizations. Your company might be a branch of a city watch, a specialist military outfit, or a group of independent bounty hunters. A good law-enforcing bounty hunter must be ready to go wherever the trail leads, and so you regularly traverse the Iron Kingdoms with little concern for national borders or boundaries in pursuit of a target. In the end, bagging your quarry is all that matters.

entries.name: Prerequisites
entries: Any character can belong to a Law Dogs company, but it must include at least one character proficient in Investigation or Insight.

backgroundFluff.name: Outlaws
entries: Crime does pay, but sometimes in blood. This lesson is learned by criminals across the Iron Kingdoms, from the syndicates of Five Fingers to the vicious bratyas of the Korsk underworld to the feral gangs of the Cryxian port of Blackwater. You and your fellow characters are members of a close-knit gang of criminals, ruffians, and thugs motivated by the promise of coin or blood for blood's sake.

entries.name: Prerequisites
entries: Each member of an Outlaws company must have at least one of the following: • The Criminal or Kayazy background • Proficiency in Deception, Intimidation, Sleight of Hand, or Stealth • Proficiency with thieves' tools

backgroundFluff.name: Pirate Crew
entries: Whether pirates or privateers, you and your fellow characters are the ranking officers of a small frigate. You might be criminals of the sea, pillaging and looting the coasts of the Iron Kingdoms for your own purposes, perhaps even in the employ of Cryx's Black Fleet. Or you might be officially recognized privateers bearing a letter of marque issued by the naval authorities of Cygnar, Khador, or Ord.

entries.name: Prerequisites
entries: Any character can be a member of a Pirate Crew company, but at least one member must be proficient with vehicle (water).

backgroundFluff.name: Spy Ring
entries: You and the other members of your party are intelligencers employed by a kingdom, major organization, or private concern. You could be members of the Cygnaran Reconnaissance Service, agents of a noble house, or simply freelancers in the employ of the highest bidder. In any case, all of you are professionals practiced in the art of espionage and surveillance.

entries.name: Prerequisites
entries: Each member of a Spy Ring company must have the Greylords Covenant, Investigator, Resistance Veteran, Spy, or Vagabond background or proficiency in Deception or Stealth.

entries.name: New Identity
entries: If the cover identity of any member of your company becomes compromised, or if your company's members need cover identities for a specific task, you can spend 7 days establishing new cover identities. You and the other members of your company spend this time purchasing forged documents, developing cover stories, researching details significant to your unique stories, and seeding the local region with rumors about your new identities in order to establish a sense of validity. When your company creates new cover identities, each member receives a plausible cover, along with appropriate documentation. The cost of these new identities is 25 gp per member.

entries.name: Company Accomplishments
entries: Your company's prestige increases whenever you undermine the security of your rivals through acts such as the following: • Blackmailing someone beholden to a rival power • Forging documents to misdirect your opponents • Uncovering a valuable secret known only to your foes • Infiltrating the inner circle of your target's trust • Rescuing a captured ally • Sabotaging enemy assets worth at least 1,000 gp TIER 1: INFILTRATORS At this tier, your company is a small group of infiltrators newly established in a target location. You occasionally receive coded messages from your employer directing you to a specific location or individual to uncover information the employer requires.

entries.name: Benefits
entries: Cover Story. Your employer has set up your company with a credible cover designed to help you pursue your employer's goals. This includes all documentation required for travel. Any papers you carry are forgeries whose quality is sufficient to pass any cursory inspection. Expense Account. You have access to a company account set up by your employer. You can use this account to purchase mission-critical equipment, secure services and hirelings, or do anything else you need to do while executing your duties. The account contains 50 gp per member of the company,

entries.name: Quirks
entries: Many adventuring companies have quirks that set them apart from other such groups. Choose a quirk for your adventuring company, or roll on the table below. is known to you only by a pseudonym and who communicates through proxies and mysterious letters. intelligence of your nation's armed forces. company became spies unwillingly after your current employer threatened you with unpleasant blackmail about your past misdeeds. person, such as an influential merchant, one of the High Captains of Five Fingers, or one of the Stone Lords of Rhul. institution such as the Church of Morrow in Caspia or the Temple of the Flame in Sul. You gather intelligence about potential heresies and enemies of the faith for your masters. of a resistance cell or rebel group, such as the Vinter loyalists of Cygnar or former Inquisitors. organization such as the Order of the Golden Crucible, Order of Wizardry, or Steam and Iron Workers Union. mercenary spies with no single employer. noble who seeks to undermine the authority of your kingdom's current ruler. inhuman authority such as Lord Toruk or one of the remaining infernal masters. which is replenished at the start of each month. Any money not spent by the end of the month is reclaimed by the employer and lost. TIER 2: OPERATIVES Your company has proved its capability and value to its employer, who will begin diverting additional resources to help you pursue your missions.

entries.name: Benefits
entries: Safe Houses. Your employer sets up a network of safe houses where you can lie low, plan, or recover after a mission. Many of these accommodations are cramped and tucked away in an unsavory part of the world, but each is secured with strong locks and stout doors and has at least one back entrance to facilitate a quick escape. Contact Network. Having proved your capability to your employer, you learn the identity of multiple contacts in your region. These contacts do not typically join you on your missions but can provide other assistance, such as presenting intelligence about a target location or individual, acting as getaway drivers, and helping you find alternative routes into a location. If the GM requires stats for a contact, these contacts can be any NPC with a challenge rating

monster.name: Clapped-Out Steamjack
languages: understands commands from its warcaster controller but can't speak

trait.name: Clapped Out
entries: This steamjack cannot take the Dash or Disengage actions. When it rolls a 1 on an attack roll, it becomes {@condition stunned} until the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Cortex
entries: The steamjack can follow simple instructions from a bonded warcaster or operator. It acts on its controller's initiative.

trait.name: Steam Powered
entries: The steamjack weighs approximately 200 pounds. It requires coal and water to operate, functioning for up to 7 hours normally or 1 hour under strenuous activity. Note: used as the Warcaster Controller companion (CR scales with wielder's proficiency bonus).

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 3} plus proficiency bonus to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}1d8 + proficiency bonus bludgeoning damage.

monster.name: Justicer Laborjack
languages: understands commands from its operator but can't speak

trait.name: Cortex
entries: The steamjack can follow simple instructions from a bonded warcaster or operator.

trait.name: Heavy Metal
entries: The steamjack is immune to being knocked {@condition prone}. It has disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

trait.name: Steam Powered
entries: The steamjack weighs approximately 600 pounds. It requires coal and water to operate for up to 5 hours normally, or 1 hour under strenuous activity.

action.name: Multiattack

action.name: Combat Truncheon
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}3d10 + 5 bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Net Launcher
entries: The steamjack fires a net at a 10-by-10-foot area within 30 feet. Each creature in that area must succeed on a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw or become {@condition restrained}. A {@condition restrained} creature can use its action to make a DC 15 Strength check, freeing itself on a success. The net can also be destroyed (AC 10; 5 slashing damage).

languages: understands commands from its operator but can't speak

trait.name: Heavy Metal

trait.name: Steam Powered

action.name: Slam

languages: understands commands from its operator but can't speak

trait.name: Cortex

trait.name: Heavy Metal

trait.name: Steam Powered
entries: The steamjack weighs approximately 600 pounds. It requires coal and water to operate for up to 6 hours normally, or 1 hour under strenuous activity.

action.name: Flensing Blade
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}3d6 + 5 slashing damage, plus 2d6 slashing damage if the target is a Beast.

action.name: Harpoon Cannon
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 3} to hit, range 50/150 ft., one target. {@h}3d6 piercing damage, and the harpoon embeds in the target. While embedded, the whaler can use a bonus action to make a contested Strength check; if it wins, it pulls the target up to 5 feet closer (Large or smaller creatures only). The harpoon line has AC 10 and 5 hit points; it is destroyed if it takes 5 or more slashing damage.

trait.name: Steam Powered

trait.name: Steam Powered
entries: The steamjack weighs approximately 300 pounds. It requires coal and water to operate for up to 7 hours normally, or 1 hour under strenuous activity.

action.name: Gaff Hook
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}2d8 + 4 piercing damage.

action.name: Harpoon Cannon
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 3} to hit, range 50/150 ft., one target. {@h}3d6 piercing damage, and the harpoon embeds in the target. While embedded, the angler can use a bonus action to make a contested Strength check; if it wins, it pulls a Large or smaller target up to 5 feet closer. The harpoon line has AC 10 and 5 hit points.

monster.name: Nomad Heavy Warjack
languages: understands commands from its warcaster but can't speak

trait.name: Heavy Metal

trait.name: Steam Powered

trait.name: Tried and True
entries: Whenever the nomad regains hit points, it regains 5 additional hit points.

action.name: Multiattack

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}1d8 + 5 bludgeoning damage.

languages: understands commands from its warcaster but can't speak

trait.name: Steam Powered

trait.name: Tried and True

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}1d8 + 4 bludgeoning damage.

trait.name: Hold the Line
entries: While the constable is within 5 feet of another city watch member, it gains a +1 bonus to its AC.

action.name: Halberd
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}1d10 + 2 slashing damage.

action.name: Pistol
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 3} to hit, range 30/90 ft., one target. {@h}1d10 + 1 piercing damage.

trait.name: Hold the Line
entries: While the inspector is within 5 feet of another city watch member, it gains a +1 bonus to its AC.

trait.name: Eye for Detail
entries: The inspector adds its Intelligence modifier to one damage roll per turn.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The inspector makes two attacks.

action.name: Sword
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}1d10 + 4 slashing damage.

action.name: Hand Cannon
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 4} to hit, range 80/240 ft., one target. {@h}2d6 + 4 piercing damage.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The thug has advantage on attack rolls against a creature if at least one of its allies is within 5 feet of the creature and isn't {@condition incapacitated}.

action.name: Club
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 3} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}1d4 + 1 bludgeoning damage.

trait.name: Nimble Escape
entries: The cutthroat can take the Disengage or Hide action as a bonus action on each of its turns.

trait.name: Sneak Attack (1/Turn)
entries: The cutthroat deals an extra 2d6 damage to one creature it hits with a weapon attack if it has advantage on the roll or if another enemy of the target is within 5 feet.

action.name: Shortsword
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 3} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}1d6 + 1 piercing damage.

action.name: Pistol
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 3} to hit, range 30/90 ft., one target. {@h}1d8 + 1 piercing damage.

trait.name: Cunning Action
entries: On each of its turns, the assassin can take a bonus action to take the Dash, Disengage, or Hide action.

trait.name: Surprise Attack (1/Rest)
entries: If the assassin surprises a creature and hits it with an attack during the first round of combat, the target takes an extra 2d8 damage.

action.name: Assassin's Blade
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}1d8 + 3 piercing damage plus 3d6 poison damage.

trait.name: Dread Reputation
entries: The underboss has advantage on Charisma (Intimidation) checks.

trait.name: Kill Stroke (1/Day)
entries: When the underboss hits a creature that has 30 or fewer hit points with a melee attack, the creature must succeed on a DC 14 Constitution saving throw or drop to 0 hit points.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The underboss makes two attacks.

action.name: Assassin's Blade
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}1d8 + 3 piercing damage plus 3d6 poison damage.

action.name: Pistol
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 5} to hit, range 30/90 ft., one target. {@h}1d8 + 3 piercing damage.

reaction.name: Watch My Back
entries: When an enemy within 5 feet of the underboss makes an attack roll against it, and an ally of the underboss is also within 5 feet of the enemy, the attacker has disadvantage on that attack roll.

trait.name: Marked Soul
entries: The cultist is marked by an infernal master. It cannot be magically charmed or possessed by non-infernal entities.

trait.name: Reckless
entries: At the start of its turn, the cultist can gain advantage on all melee weapon attack rolls during that turn, but attack rolls against it have advantage until the start of its next turn.

action.name: Ritual Dagger
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 3} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}1d4 + 1 piercing damage plus 1d4 necrotic damage.

action.name: Spellcasting
entries: The cultist is a 1st-level spellcaster (Wisdom, spell save {@dc 11}, {@hit 3} to hit). It has the following spells prepared:
Cantrips (at will): guidance, resistance
1st level (2 slots): bane, inflict wounds

trait.name: Blood Fuel
entries: When the sentinel reduces a creature to 0 hit points, it can cast a 1st-level spell as a bonus action on the same turn without expending a spell slot.

trait.name: Marked Soul
entries: The sentinel is marked by an infernal master and cannot be magically charmed or possessed by non-infernal entities.

trait.name: Second Wind (1/Rest)
entries: As a bonus action, the sentinel can regain 1d10 + 1 hit points.

action.name: Greatsword
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}2d6 + 3 slashing damage plus 1d4 necrotic damage.

action.name: Spellcasting
entries: The dark sentinel is a 3rd-level spellcaster (Wisdom, spell save {@dc 13}, {@hit 5} to hit). It has the following spells prepared:
Cantrips (at will): {@spell guidance}, {@spell resistance}, {@spell shocking grasp}
1st level (4 slots): {@spell bane}, {@spell hellish rebuke}, {@spell inflict wounds}, {@spell shield}
2nd level (2 slots): {@spell darkness}, {@spell hold person}

trait.name: Dusk Prowler
entries: The wretch has advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks made in dim light or darkness.

trait.name: Entropic Force
entries: Creatures within 5 feet of the wretch cannot regain hit points. Any creature that starts its turn within 5 feet of the wretch must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or its death saving throw DCs increase by 2 (to 12+) until the wretch moves away.

trait.name: Marked Soul
entries: The wretch is marked by an infernal master and cannot be magically charmed or possessed by non-infernal entities.

action.name: Cursed Touch
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 2} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}1d4 − 1 bludgeoning damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or become {@condition poisoned} for 1 minute. While {@condition poisoned} in this way, the creature has disadvantage on saving throws against the wretch's spells.

action.name: Spellcasting
entries: The wretch is a 5th-level spellcaster (Wisdom, spell save {@dc 13}, {@hit 5} to hit). It has the following spells prepared:
Cantrips (at will): {@spell guidance}, {@spell mending}, {@spell resistance}, {@spell sacred flame}, {@spell thaumaturgy}
1st level (4 slots): {@spell bane}, {@spell command}, {@spell inflict wounds}, {@spell shield of faith}
2nd level (3 slots): {@spell hold person}, {@spell silence}
3rd level (2 slots): {@spell bestow curse}

trait.name: Blood Fueled
entries: When the infernalist reduces a creature to 0 hit points, it can cast a 2nd-level spell as a bonus action on the same turn without expending a spell slot.

trait.name: Marked Soul
entries: The infernalist is marked by an infernal master and cannot be magically charmed or possessed by non-infernal entities.

action.name: Dark Iron Dagger
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 3} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}1d4 + 1 piercing damage.

action.name: Spellcasting
entries: The infernalist is a 7th-level spellcaster (Intelligence, spell save {@dc 13}, {@hit 5} to hit). It has the following spells prepared:
Cantrips (at will): {@spell acid splash}, {@spell minor illusion}, {@spell poison spray}, {@spell true strike}
1st level (4 slots): {@spell disguise self}, {@spell false life}, {@spell Tasha's hideous laughter}, {@spell ray of sickness}
2nd level (3 slots): {@spell darkness}, {@spell hold person}, {@spell suggestion}
3rd level (2 slots): {@spell bestow curse}, {@spell fear}

action.name: Summon Infernal (1/Day)
entries: The infernalist calls an infernal creature to its side. The type of infernal summoned is determined by the GM. The infernal acts on the infernalist's initiative and obeys its commands. It remains for 1 hour or until reduced to 0 hit points.

trait.name: Tempered by War
entries: The sharpshooter has advantage on saving throws against being {@condition frightened}.

action.name: Sword
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 3} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}1d8 + 1 slashing damage.

action.name: Military Rifle
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 4} to hit, range 60/180 ft., one target. {@h}2d8 + 2 piercing damage. Firearm (misfire 1). Reload (1 shot).

trait.name: Charge
entries: If the man-at-arms moves at least 20 feet in a straight line toward a target and then hits it with a melee weapon attack on the same turn, the attack deals an extra 1d6 damage.

trait.name: Tempered by War
entries: The man-at-arms has advantage on saving throws against being {@condition frightened}.

action.name: Halberd
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}1d10 + 2 slashing damage.

trait.name: Tempered by War
entries: The veteran has advantage on saving throws against being {@condition frightened}.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The veteran makes two attacks.

action.name: Caspian Battleblade
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}1d8 + 2 slashing damage.

action.name: Repeating Pistol
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 4} to hit, range 30/90 ft., one target. {@h}1d10 + 2 piercing damage. Magazine (5 shots).

trait.name: Second Wind (1/Rest)
entries: As a bonus action, the elite can regain 15 hit points.

trait.name: Tempered by War
entries: The elite has advantage on saving throws against being {@condition frightened}.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The elite makes two attacks.

action.name: Greatsword
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}2d6 + 3 slashing damage.

action.name: Hand Cannon
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 5} to hit, range 80/240 ft., one target. {@h}2d6 + 3 piercing damage. Firearm (misfire 1). Reload (1 shot).

trait.name: Focus Points (3)
entries: The warcaster has 3 focus points. It can expend them to: add up to 3 points as a bonus to an attack roll or damage roll (divided as it chooses), reduce incoming damage by 5, or use Shake It Off (remove the {@condition stunned} or {@condition frightened} condition as a bonus action).

trait.name: Magic Weapons
entries: The warcaster's mechanikal weapons are treated as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity.

action.name: Mechanikal Sword or Greatsword
entries: Sword: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}1d8 + 2 slashing damage. Greatsword: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}2d6 + 2 slashing damage.

action.name: Mechanikal Pistol
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 4} to hit, range 30/90 ft., one target. {@h}1d10 + 2 piercing damage. Magelock (firearm usable only by warcasters and gun mages). Reload (1 shot).

action.name: Spellcasting
entries: The warcaster is a 4th-level spellcaster (Intelligence, spell save {@dc 13}, {@hit 5} to hit). It has the following spells prepared:
Cantrips (at will): {@spell arcane bolt|IRKCore}, {@spell light}, {@spell sense cortex|IRKCore}
1st level (4 slots): {@spell burning hands}, {@spell jump start|IRKCore}, {@spell razor wind|IRKCore}, {@spell shield}
2nd level (3 slots): {@spell battering ram|IRKCore}, {@spell flashing blade|IRKCore}

trait.name: Force Barrier
entries: The trancer has a +2 bonus to AC against ranged attacks.

trait.name: Psychokinetic Blast
entries: When the trancer drops to 0 hit points, each creature within 15 feet must succeed on a {@dc 13} Intelligence saving throw or take 2d6 psychic damage.

action.name: Mental Force
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 5} to hit, range 60 ft., one target. {@h}2d10 psychic damage. The trancer can choose to take 5 psychic damage (reducing its own maximum hit points by 5 until a long rest) to impose disadvantage on the target's saving throws until the end of the trancer's next turn.

trait.name: Breather
entries: The guard's gas mask makes it immune to inhaled poisons and toxins.

trait.name: Combined Attack
entries: When two or more infantry of the same faction attack the same target, each attacker deals an extra 1d6 damage on a hit.

action.name: Hand Weapon
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}1d8 + 2 bludgeoning or slashing damage.

action.name: Crucible Arms Model 609
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 4} to hit, range 60/180 ft., one target. {@h}2d8 + 2 piercing damage plus 1d4 acid, fire, or poison damage (soldier's choice). Firearm (misfire 1). Reload (1 shot).

trait.name: Man-O-War Armor
entries: The man-o-war's steam armor provides a damage threshold of 10 (attacks that deal 10 or fewer damage are reduced to 0). The armor requires 20 pounds of coal and 8 gallons of water to operate for 5 hours (1 hour in strenuous combat). If the armor loses power, the man-o-war's speed drops to 0.

trait.name: Northern Resilience
entries: The man-o-war has a +2 bonus to Constitution checks and saving throws.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The man-o-war makes one Annihilator Blade attack and one Shield Bash attack, or it uses Cannon Shield.

action.name: Annihilator Blade
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}2d10 + 3 slashing damage.

action.name: Shield Bash
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}2d6 + 3 bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Cannon Shield
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 1} to hit, range 10/30 ft., one target. {@h}1d10 − 2 piercing damage. Firearm. Reload (1 shot).

trait.name: Burning Devotion
entries: The knight has advantage on saving throws against being {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened}.

trait.name: Driven by Battle
entries: When a creature friendly to the knight takes damage within 30 feet of it, the knight's next attack roll before the start of its next turn deals an extra 2d6 damage on a hit.

trait.name: Magic Weapon
entries: The knight's relic blade is treated as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The knight makes two Relic Blade attacks.

action.name: Relic Blade
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}2d6 + 3 slashing damage plus 1d8 radiant damage if the target is a fiend, fey, or undead.

trait.name: Action Surge (1/Short Rest)
entries: The stormblade can take one additional action on its turn.

trait.name: Combined Attack
entries: When two or more stormblades attack the same target, each attacker deals an extra 1d6 damage on a hit.

trait.name: Special Equipment
entries: One in three stormblades carries a storm thrower instead of a standard pistol.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The stormblade makes two Storm Glaive attacks.

action.name: Storm Glaive
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}1d8 + 3 slashing damage plus 1d4 lightning damage. Chain Lightning: a second creature within 5 feet of the target takes 1d8 lightning damage (no attack roll needed).

action.name: Storm Glaive Blast
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 6} to hit, range 20/60 ft., one target. {@h}1d8 + 3 lightning damage. Chain Lightning: a second creature within 5 feet of the target takes 1d8 lightning damage.

action.name: Storm Thrower
entries: (Special Equipment only) {@atk rw} {@hit 6} to hit, range 80/320 ft., one target. {@h}1d8 + 3 lightning damage. The next attack roll made against the target before the start of the stormblade's next turn has advantage.

trait.name: Burning Devotion
entries: The flameguard has advantage on saving throws against being {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened}.

trait.name: Shield Wall
entries: When the flameguard is within 5 feet of at least two other flameguards carrying shields, it gains a +2 bonus to its AC.

action.name: Flame Spear
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 3} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}1d6 + 1 piercing damage plus 1d6 fire damage.

trait.name: Combined Attack
entries: When two or more winter guard attack the same target, each attacker deals an extra 1d6 damage on a hit.

trait.name: Northern Resilience
entries: The winter guard has a +2 bonus to Constitution checks and saving throws.

trait.name: Special Equipment
entries: One in four winter guard carries a rocket tube with 3 rockets instead of a blunderbuss.

action.name: Handaxe
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}1d6 + 2 slashing damage.

action.name: Blunderbuss
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 4} to hit, range 30/90 ft., one target. {@h}1d8 + 2 piercing damage. AOE: each creature adjacent to the target takes half damage on a failed {@dc 12} Dexterity save. Firearm (misfire 1). Reload (1 shot).

action.name: Rocket Tube
entries: (Special Equipment only) {@atk rw} {@hit 4} to hit, range 80/240 ft., one target. {@h}4d10 fire damage in a 10-foot radius. Each other creature in the area must succeed on a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw or take half damage. Reload (1 shot per rocket; 3 rockets total).

trait.name: Combined Attack
entries: When two or more trenchers attack the same target, each attacker deals an extra 1d6 damage on a hit.

trait.name: Dig In
entries: The trencher can spend 10 minutes to dig a foxhole that provides three-quarters cover (+5 to AC and Dexterity saves).

trait.name: Special Equipment
entries: One in four trenchers carries a grenade rifle. Each trencher carries 3 smoke grenades.

action.name: Bayonet
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}1d6 + 2 piercing damage.

action.name: Military Rifle
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 4} to hit, range 60/180 ft., one target. {@h}2d8 + 2 piercing damage. Firearm (misfire 1). Reload (1 shot).

action.name: Rifle Grenade
entries: (Special Equipment only) {@atk rw} {@hit 4} to hit, range 60/180 ft., one target. {@h}3d10 piercing damage in a 10-foot radius. Each creature in the area must succeed on a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw or take full damage (half on success). Reload.

action.name: Smoke Grenade
entries: The trencher throws a smoke grenade at a point within 30 feet. A 10-foot-radius sphere of thick smoke appears centered on that point. The sphere spreads around corners, and its area is heavily obscured. It lasts for 1 minute or until a strong wind disperses it.

trait.name: Point Blank
entries: The pirate doesn't have disadvantage on ranged attack rolls from being within 5 feet of a hostile creature.

trait.name: Sea Rover
entries: The pirate ignores movement penalties from waves and rough water. It can stand from {@condition prone} with a DC 10 Dexterity check instead of using half its movement.

action.name: Cutlass
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}1d8 + 2 slashing damage.

action.name: Pistol
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 4} to hit, range 30/90 ft., one target. {@h}1d8 + 2 piercing damage. Firearm (misfire 1). Reload (1 shot).

trait.name: Sea Rover
entries: The press ganger ignores movement penalties from waves and rough water. It can stand from {@condition prone} with a DC 10 Dexterity check instead of using half its movement.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The press ganger makes two Blunt Instrument attacks.

action.name: Blunt Instrument
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}1d6 + 2 bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Knockout Strike (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The press ganger makes a Blunt Instrument attack. If it hits, the target must succeed on a {@dc 12} Constitution saving throw or become {@condition stunned} until the end of the press ganger's next turn. A target that is already {@condition stunned} and fails this save falls {@condition unconscious} for 1 hour or until it takes damage.

trait.name: Old Salt
entries: The officer has advantage on Wisdom (Survival) checks related to weather and navigation.

trait.name: Sea Rover
entries: The officer ignores movement penalties from waves and rough water. It can stand from {@condition prone} with a DC 10 Dexterity check instead of using half its movement.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The officer makes three attacks: two with its cutlass and one with its pistol, or three with its cutlass.

action.name: Cutlass
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}1d8 + 3 slashing damage.

action.name: Pistol
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 5} to hit, range 30/90 ft., one target. {@h}1d8 + 3 piercing damage. Firearm (misfire 1). Reload (1 shot).

action.name: No Sleeping on the Job!
entries: The officer calls out to up to 3 allies within 30 feet. Each ally can immediately reroll a saving throw it is currently failing due to a condition and take the new result.

reaction.name: Slip Away
entries: When an enemy ends its movement within 5 feet of the officer, the officer can move up to half its speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

classFeature.name: Field Alchemy
entries: Beginning at 1st level, you know the basics of crafting alchemy in the field. During a short or long rest, you can use alchemist's supplies to craft alchemical items if you have the required ingredients and know the appropriate formula. You can create one alchemical item during a short rest or two items during a long rest.
Some of your features and formulas require your target to make a saving throw to resist the effects. You use your Intelligence modifier when setting the saving throw DC for these effects.

entries.name: Formulas Known
entries: When you create your character, choose two alchemical formulas. You learn additional formulas of your choice when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown in the Formulas Known column of the Alchemist table.
Whenever you gain a level in this class, you can replace one of the alchemical formulas you have learned with a new one.

entries.name: Discovering New Formulas
entries: When you find a formula during your travels, you can add it to the formulas you know by spending 2 hours and 50 gp. Formulas copied in this way do not count against the total number of formulas you know.

classFeature.name: Poison Resistance
entries: At 1st level, you have resistance to poison damage and have advantage on saving throws against being poisoned.

classFeature.name: Fast Brew
entries: At 2nd level, you learn to create a number of quick, simple recipes that do not require a complex process of brewing and stabilizing.

As a bonus action, you can create one of the following simple alchemical items. Your creation remains potent until the end of your next turn. You must have a field alchemy kit to create a fast brew item but do not need to expend a use of the kit to do so.

items: {@b Alkalic Fluid.} A syrupy blue liquid that neutralizes acids. A 1-ounce dose renders 1 pint of acid inert. A creature splashed with it has resistance to acid damage for 1 round.
{@b Blackbond.} A thick black adhesive. A 1-ounce dose covers a 1-foot-square surface and holds up to 200 pounds. Loses coherency after 10 minutes.

{@b Firestifle.} An expanding white foam that extinguishes flames. Applied to a point within 5 feet, it snuffs natural flames in a 5-foot area. A creature exposed to firestifle has resistance to fire damage for 1 round.
{@b Simple Smoke.} A thick cloud of smoke filling a 5-foot sphere centered on a point within 5 feet, heavily obscuring the area for 1 round or until dispersed by wind.

{@b Stink Gas.} A cloud of noxious gas filling a 5-foot sphere within 5 feet. Until the end of your next turn, each creature starting its turn in the cloud or entering it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your alchemy save DC or become poisoned until the end of its next turn. Has no effect on constructs or undead.

classFeature.name: Quick Swig
entries: At 3rd level, you can use one alchemical item or potion as a bonus action on your turn.

classFeature.name: Specialization
entries: When you reach 3rd level, you choose to focus your skills and knowledge on a particular alchemical discipline. Choose Combat Alchemist, Synthesist, or Rogue Alchemist, all detailed at the end of the class description. Your choice grants you features at 3rd level and again at 6th, 10th, 14th, and 18th level.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your campaign uses the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

classFeature.name: Extra Attack
entries: Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

classFeature.name: Tool Expertise
entries: Starting at 6th level, your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses your proficiency with a tool.

classFeature.name: Frenzied Concoction
entries: Starting at 7th level, you can shortcut your recipes in times of need. You can craft one formula you know as a bonus action, ignoring the normal time required to brew it.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (a minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your campaign uses the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

classFeature.name: Hit the Deck
entries: Beginning at 11th level, you become accustomed to dealing with the sudden explosions and violent reactions that come with the study of alchemy. When you are subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to avoid damage, you can use your reaction to go prone. If you use your reaction to go prone, you take no damage from the effect.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your campaign uses the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

classFeature.name: Tailored Concoctions
entries: Starting at 15th level, you customize your alchemical items to deal with a particular type of opponent, increasing the harm you inflict on it. As a bonus action, you can designate one creature you can see within 60 feet of you as the target of this feature. The first time each turn that the target is exposed to one of your alchemical items or grenades, you can ignore one of the creature's damage resistances or force it to make saving throws against your class features and alchemical formulas with disadvantage.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (a minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your campaign uses the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your campaign uses the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

classFeature.name: Master Alchemist
entries: Starting at 20th level, you know the formula for every alchemical item, and alchemical items you create do not lose potency. Additionally, you gain a +1 bonus to all saving throws for each alchemical item currently affecting you.

classFeature.name: Gun Mage Order
entries: At 1st level, you have begun training in one of the orders of gun mages. Your choice grants you features at 1st level and again at 6th, 10th, and 14th level.

classFeature.name: Spellcasting
entries: Through dedicated training, you have molded your innate spark of magic to augment your martial skill with firearms. See the {@filter gun mage spell list|spells|class=Gun Mage} in Chapter 3.

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: At 1st level, you know two cantrips of your choice from the {@filter gun mage spell list|spells|class=Gun Mage}. You learn additional cantrips at higher levels, as shown in the Cantrips Known column.

entries.name: Spell Slots
entries: The Gun Mage table shows how many spell slots you have; all of your spell slots are the same level. To cast a spell of 1st level or higher, you must expend a slot. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Charisma is your spellcasting ability for your gun mage spells.

entries.name: Spellcasting Focus
entries: You can use a magelock or bonded firearm as a spellcasting focus for your gun mage spells.

classFeature.name: Rune Shots
entries: At 1st level, you gain two rune shot options of your choice from the list below. Once per turn when you fire a firearm as part of the Attack action, you can apply one of your rune shot options to that attack. You must apply the option before making the attack roll.

You have two uses of this ability, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest. All rune shots are magical effects. If a rune shot requires a saving throw, its DC equals your spell save DC.

entries.name: Expending Spell Slots
entries: You can convert available spell slots to additional uses of your Rune Shots feature. You gain one additional use for each level of the spell slot you expend.

classFeature.name: Fighting Style
entries: Beginning at 2nd level, you adopt a particular style of fighting. Choose one of the following options. You can't take a Fighting Style option more than once, even if you later get to choose again.

classFeature.name: Weapon Bond
entries: At 3rd level, you forge a deep bond with one of your firearms. To bond with a firearm, spend at least 1 hour in meditation inscribing protective runes into it. A bonded weapon counts as one of the total items you can be attuned to. You may have up to three bonded weapons at any one time.

A bonded firearm counts as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage. In addition, a bonded weapon never suffers harmful effects from firing rune shots.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your campaign uses the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your campaign uses the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

classFeature.name: Extra Rune Shot
entries: At 11th level, you can use your Rune Shots feature three times between rests.

classFeature.name: Multiple Runes
entries: At 11th level, you can layer the effects of multiple rune shots onto a single bullet. When you expend a use of your Rune Shots feature to make a rune shot attack, you can choose an additional rune shot effect to affect the target.
Once you use this feature, you must finish a long rest before you can use it again.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your campaign uses the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

classFeature.name: Extra Rune Shot
entries: At 13th level, you can use your Rune Shots feature four times between rests.

classFeature.name: Extra Rune Shot
entries: At 15th level, you can use your Rune Shots feature five times between rests.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your campaign uses the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

classFeature.name: Extra Rune Shot
entries: At 17th level, you can use your Rune Shots feature six times between rests.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your campaign uses the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

classFeature.name: Arcane Precision
entries: At 20th level, when you use your Rune Shots feature or your Multiple Runes feature on an Attack action and fail to hit, you can choose to not expend the feature on that attack.
Once you use this feature, you must finish a short or long rest before you can use it again.

classFeature.name: Fighting Style
entries: Beginning at 1st level, you adopt a particular style of fighting. Choose one of the following options. You can't take a Fighting Style option more than once, even if you later get to choose again.

classFeature.name: Trick Shots
entries: You learn to perform signature trick shots with a pistol or rifle. When you use this feature, choose an effect from the options below. You must be wielding a loaded pistol or rifle.

A trick shot can only modify a single attack. Once you use this feature, you must finish a short or long rest before you can use it again. Beginning at 6th level, you can use it twice between rests; at 18th level, three times.

entries.name: Trick Shot Options
items: {@b Golden Bullet.} Make a ranged attack against a target within normal range. This attack ignores damage resistances.

{@b Piercing Shot.} As an action, fire a shot in a line 5 feet wide extending to your weapon's normal range. Creatures in the line must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take damage equal to your weapon's damage.

{@b Pinning Shot.} Make a ranged attack against a target within normal range. On a hit, the target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be knocked prone and become frightened until the start of your next turn.
{@b Ricochet Shot.} If you are aware of a creature in total cover, you can target it if there is a clear path for the bullet to ricochet along.

{@b Wounding Shot.} Make a ranged attack. On a hit, the creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take additional damage equal to your Dexterity modifier (minimum 1) at the start of each of its turns until a creature uses an action and succeeds on a DC 10 Wisdom (Medicine) check, or until the creature receives healing. Works on living creatures and steamjacks, not other constructs or undead.

classFeature.name: Trick Shots
entries: Beginning at 6th level, you can use your Trick Shots feature twice between rests.

classFeature.name: Trick Shots
entries: Beginning at 18th level, you can use your Trick Shots feature three times between rests.

classFeature.name: Fast Reload
entries: At 2nd level, if a firearm you wield does not have any rounds loaded, you can reload the weapon as part of a ranged attack.

classFeature.name: Forge Shot
entries: Beginning at 2nd level, after you complete a short or long rest, you can produce ammunition for a firearm you are proficient with. Producing ammunition requires you to expend 1 gp worth of lead, blasting powder, and casings. You can craft a number of bullets equal to 10 + your proficiency modifier (minimum 1).

classFeature.name: Gunfighter Archetype
entries: At 3rd level, you choose an archetype that you emulate in your approach to gunfights. Choose Commando, Pistoleer, or Sharpshooter, all detailed at the end of the class description. Your choice grants you features at 3rd level and again at 6th, 11th, and 17th level.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your campaign uses the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

classFeature.name: Extra Attack
entries: Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

classFeature.name: Overwatch
entries: Starting at 7th level, if you are currently wielding a firearm, you can use your reaction to make a ranged attack against a creature that moves within your line of sight and is within the firearm's normal range.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your campaign uses the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

classFeature.name: Fast Reflexes
entries: At 9th level, the multiple firefights you've survived have honed your reflexes to respond to danger. You have advantage on Dexterity saving throws against effects you can see.
Additionally, when an attacker that you can see hits you with an attack, you can use your reaction to halve the attack's damage against you.

classFeature.name: Suppressing Fire!
entries: At 10th level, you can use your action to expend the magazine of a weapon you're holding against multiple targets. Choose a point you can see within your weapon's range. Any creature within 10 feet of that point must make a Wisdom saving throw against your trick shot DC. On a failed save, the creature's speed is reduced to 0, and it can't use reactions until the start of your next turn.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
