entries: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your campaign uses the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

classFeature.name: Hit and Run
entries: Starting at 13th level, once per turn after hitting a creature with a ranged weapon attack, you may immediately move a distance up to your speed.

classFeature.name: Low Profile
entries: At 14th level, you have learned how to maximize cover during a prolonged firefight. You treat half cover as three-quarters cover and three-quarters cover as total cover.

classFeature.name: Keen Eyed
entries: At 15th level, you increase the normal range of firearms you wield by 20 feet and the maximum range by 60 feet.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your campaign uses the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your campaign uses the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

classFeature.name: Dead-Eye
entries: At 20th level, when you perform a ranged weapon attack with a pistol or rifle and miss, you may choose to hit instead. You may use this feature a number of times equal to your Dexterity modifier (a minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

classFeature.name: Tinkering
entries: You can tinker with devices and machines to make them more effective. As a bonus action, choose yourself or a creature within 5 feet of you. That creature gains one Tinkering die (a d4 at 1st level).

Once within the next 10 minutes, the creature can roll the die and: add the result to one attack roll or damage roll made with a weapon; add the result to one ability check made with an item; or subtract the result from the damage of a single enemy attack dealing bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage. The Tinkering die can be used after seeing the d20 roll but before the GM reveals the outcome. Once the Tinkering die is rolled, it is lost. A creature can have only one Tinkering die at a time.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest. Starting at 5th level, you regain all uses when you finish a short or long rest.
Your Tinkering die increases as you gain levels: d6 at 5th level, d8 at 11th level, d10 at 18th level.

entries.name: Crafting Through Tinkering
entries: As part of light activity during a long rest, roll your Tinkering die. You can craft nonmagical items worth a total of 10 gp × the number rolled, providing materials equal to half the items' value.

classFeature.name: Tinkering
entries: At 5th level, your Tinkering die becomes a {@dice d6}.

classFeature.name: Tinkering
entries: At 11th level, your Tinkering die becomes a {@dice d8}.

classFeature.name: Tinkering
entries: At 18th level, your Tinkering die becomes a {@dice d10}.

classFeature.name: Mechanik's Savvy
entries: At 1st level, your time spent in workshops and around machines has provided you with an intuitive sense of danger. You have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks while in an industrial environment (workshops, docks, or anywhere relying on heavy industrial equipment). You can also determine whether a machine is operating within normal tolerance or is in danger of failure.
Starting at 18th level, you also add your Intelligence modifier to your initiative rolls, with advantage on initiative in industrial locales.

classFeature.name: Expertise
entries: At 2nd level, choose two of your skill proficiencies, or one skill proficiency and your proficiency with a mechanik's toolkit. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check using either of the chosen proficiencies.
At 6th level, you choose two more proficiencies (skills or mechanik's toolkit) to gain this benefit.

classFeature.name: Expertise
entries: At 6th level, you choose two more of your proficiencies (in skills or with a mechanik's toolkit) to double your proficiency bonus for ability checks using them.

classFeature.name: Field Mechanik
entries: At 2nd level, you learn how to repair items quickly in the field. As a bonus action, choose a construct, vehicle, mechanikal device, or steam armor within 5 feet of you and expend one of your uses of Tinkering to roll a Tinkering die. The chosen creature or object regains hit points equal to the number rolled.

classFeature.name: Mechanik Archetype
entries: At 3rd level, you choose an archetype that shapes your path as a mechanik. Choose the Arcane Mechanik, Combat Mechanik, or Ironhead, all detailed at the end of the class description. Your choice grants you features at 3rd level and again at 7th, 10th, 15th, and 18th level.

classFeature.name: Bodging
entries: At 3rd level, you can combine your knack for tinkering with scrap materials to fashion temporary items. Using a mechanik's toolkit, you can spend 10 minutes to create a piece of gear. You can only bodge items that can be used more than once (a crossbow or lantern, not a poison or flask of oil).

The maximum value of an item you can bodge is equal to 10 × the roll of your Tinkering die (you do not expend the die; the roll simply shows effectiveness). Upon being used, a bodged item lasts for 1 hour or until an attack roll or skill check involving the item results in a 1 on the d20. Bodged items cannot be repaired, but their parts can be returned to your toolkit.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your campaign uses the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

classFeature.name: Inspired Genius
entries: Beginning at 5th level, you regain all of your expended uses of Tinkering when you finish a short or long rest.

classFeature.name: Scrounging
entries: At 6th level, you develop a knack for scrounging up parts for building or repairing mechanika. You can spend 10 minutes and take the Search action to make a DC 15 Wisdom (Perception) check through junkyards, scrap heaps, old battlefields, and refuse piles. On a success, you find enough spare parts to reduce the cost of crafting an item by half.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your campaign uses the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

classFeature.name: Brilliant Design
entries: Beginning at 9th level, when you expend a Tinkering die, you can choose to take the highest value on your die instead of rolling. The die is still expended as normal.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

classFeature.name: Master Bodger
entries: At 9th level, when you bodge an item, you can expend a Tinkering die and roll it to further enhance the bodge. Your bodged item functions for a number of hours equal to the number rolled, and the maximum value of the item becomes 20 times the number rolled.

classFeature.name: Improved Crafting
entries: Starting at 13th level, when you roll a Tinkering die while crafting an item during a long rest, double the number rolled to determine the value of your bodge.

classFeature.name: Enduring Tinkering
entries: Starting at 14th level, when you use Tinkering, the Tinkering die lasts until the chosen creature finishes a long rest, rather than 10 minutes.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your campaign uses the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your campaign uses the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

classFeature.name: Hold Together
entries: Beginning at 17th level, the first time a friendly construct drops to 0 hit points but is not killed outright, you can use your reaction to expend a Tinkering die. The construct continues to function for a number of rounds equal to the roll of your Tinkering die. The construct still makes death saving throws and suffers normal effects of taking damage while at 0 hit points.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your campaign uses the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

classFeature.name: Always Tinkering
entries: At 20th level, when you roll initiative and have no uses of Tinkering left, you regain one use.

classFeature.name: Bond
entries: Starting at 1st level, you discover an innate ability to mentally contact and control steamjack cortexes. You can attune to a mechanikal weapon or suit of warcaster armor by spending an action in physical contact with the item, rather than a short rest. You must still follow the normal rules of attunement for magic items of other types.

classFeature.name: Warcaster Tradition
entries: At 1st level, you have undergone journeyman training that defines how you wield your warcaster talents. Choose Arcanist, Controller, or Soldier, all detailed at the end of the class description. Your choice grants you features at 1st level and again at 6th, 10th, 14th, and 18th level.

classFeature.name: Warcaster Magic
entries: You were born with an intrinsic ability to manipulate arcane energy. See chapter 3 for the {@filter warcaster spell list|spells|class=Warcaster}.

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: At 1st level, you know two cantrips from the {@filter warcaster spell list|spells|class=Warcaster}. You learn additional warcaster cantrips at higher levels, as shown in the Cantrips Known column of the Warcaster table.

entries.name: Focus Points
entries: Rather than spell slots, you use focus points to cast your spells. The Warcaster table shows how many focus points you have at each level. To cast a warcaster spell of 1st level or higher, expend a number of focus points equal to the spell's level. You cannot exceed the Maximum Spell Level shown in the Warcaster table. You regain 1 focus point every 10 minutes, and all expended focus points when you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Preparing Spells
entries: You prepare a list of warcaster spells equal to your Intelligence modifier + your warcaster level (minimum 1). The spells must be of a level no higher than your Maximum Spell Level. You can change prepared spells on a long rest (1 minute per spell level per spell).

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for warcaster spells.

entries.name: Control Range
entries: Your control range (the distance within which you can interact with steamjack cortexes) equals your maximum focus points × 10 feet.

entries.name: Spellcasting Focus
entries: You can use a bonded mechanikal weapon as a spellcasting focus for your warcaster spells.

classFeature.name: Focus Manipulation
entries: At 2nd level, you learn to channel raw arcane energy to augment your mechanikal weapons, armor, and combat abilities. You can spend focus points for one of the following effects:

items: {@b Attack Bonus.} Spend 1 focus point when making an attack with a bonded mechanikal weapon to make the attack roll with advantage.
{@b Damage Bonus.} Spend any number of focus points after hitting with a bonded mechanikal weapon to deal an extra {@damage 1d8} weapon damage per point spent.
{@b Reduce Damage.} When wearing bonded warcaster armor and taking damage, use your reaction and spend 1 focus point to reduce the damage by double your proficiency bonus.
{@b Shake It Off.} If suffering from a condition or enemy effect that can be ended with a successful saving throw, spend 1 focus point to make the saving throw with advantage.

classFeature.name: Steamjack Bond
entries: At 3rd level, you develop the ability to forge lasting bonds with steamjacks. Touch a steamjack and spend 10 minutes to forge a bond. You can communicate telepathically with bonded steamjacks; they obey your commands and act on their own initiative in combat, with you determining their actions. A bonded steamjack counts as an attuned item.

If your bonded steamjack is destroyed, you can recover its cortex and fit it into a new chassis, requiring 8 hours of work and 250 gp in materials. If the cortex is destroyed, the bond is severed and you can form a new bond. You can voluntarily end a bond as a bonus action.
If you are incapacitated in combat, all steamjacks bonded to you suffer backlash and become inert.

classFeature.name: Focus Allocation
entries: As a bonus action on your turn, you can allocate focus points to a bonded steamjack within your control range. The maximum number you can allocate is limited by the quality of the steamjack's cortex. You cannot allocate to a steamjack without a functioning cortex.

During its turn, a bonded steamjack within your control range can spend allocated focus points to use the Attack Bonus, Damage Bonus, or Shake It Off effects from your Focus Manipulation feature. Allocated focus points are lost after 10 minutes.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your campaign uses the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

classFeature.name: Power Up
entries: Starting at 5th level, at the start of your turn, all your bonded steamjacks within your control range gain 1 focus point. A steamjack cannot gain a focus point from this feature if it does not have a functioning cortex. Focus from Power Up cannot exceed the maximum focus of the steamjack's cortex.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your campaign uses the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

classFeature.name: Direct Control
entries: Starting at 11th level, on your turn, you can take direct control of one bonded steamjack within your control range. You see through its eyes and control its actions directly.

While in direct control, the steamjack uses your proficiency bonus for attacks (if higher) and gains the benefit of your feats and class features. You can cast bonus-action spells while in direct control, but you—not the steamjack—are the point of origin, with line of sight and range determined by your position.
You remain in direct control as long as you concentrate (as if concentrating on a spell), or until the steamjack leaves your control range or you choose to end control.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your campaign uses the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

classFeature.name: Refined Focus
entries: Starting at 15th level, your Focus Manipulation improves:

items: {@b Attack Bonus.} If both dice in the advantage roll would hit the target, the attack is a critical hit.
{@b Damage Bonus.} The damage bonus increases to {@dice d10} per focus point spent.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your campaign uses the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

classFeature.name: Battlegroup
entries: Starting at 17th level, when you allocate focus to a steamjack, each other steamjack bonded to you within your control range gains 1 focus point.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your campaign uses the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

classFeature.name: Focus Recovery
entries: At 20th level, when you roll initiative or start your turn during a combat encounter, you regain 4 focus points if you have no focus points left.

classFeature.name: Specialization feature
entries: At 6th level, you gain a feature granted by your Specialization.

classFeature.name: Specialization feature
entries: At 10th level, you gain a feature granted by your Specialization.

classFeature.name: Specialization feature
entries: At 14th level, you gain a feature granted by your Specialization.

classFeature.name: Specialization feature
entries: At 18th level, you gain a feature granted by your Specialization.

classFeature.name: Gun Mage Order feature
entries: At 6th level, you gain a feature granted by your Gun Mage Order.

classFeature.name: Gun Mage Order feature
entries: At 10th level, you gain a feature granted by your Gun Mage Order.

classFeature.name: Gun Mage Order feature
entries: At 14th level, you gain a feature granted by your Gun Mage Order.

classFeature.name: Gunfighter Archetype feature
entries: At 6th level, you gain a feature granted by your Gunfighter Archetype.

classFeature.name: Gunfighter Archetype feature
entries: At 11th level, you gain a feature granted by your Gunfighter Archetype.

classFeature.name: Gunfighter Archetype feature
entries: At 17th level, you gain a feature granted by your Gunfighter Archetype.

classFeature.name: Mechanik Archetype feature
entries: At 7th level, you gain a feature granted by your Mechanik Archetype.

classFeature.name: Mechanik Archetype feature
entries: At 10th level, you gain a feature granted by your Mechanik Archetype.

classFeature.name: Mechanik Archetype feature
entries: At 15th level, you gain a feature granted by your Mechanik Archetype.

classFeature.name: Mechanik Archetype feature
entries: At 18th level, you gain a feature granted by your Mechanik Archetype.

classFeature.name: Warcaster Tradition feature
entries: At 6th level, you gain a feature granted by your Warcaster Tradition.

classFeature.name: Warcaster Tradition feature
entries: At 10th level, you gain a feature granted by your Warcaster Tradition.

classFeature.name: Warcaster Tradition feature
entries: At 14th level, you gain a feature granted by your Warcaster Tradition.

classFeature.name: Warcaster Tradition feature
entries: At 18th level, you gain a feature granted by your Warcaster Tradition.

subclass.name: Order of the Lone Gun
subclassFeatures: A Few More Shots|Gun Mage|IRKCore|Lone Gun|IRKCore|10

subclass.name: Guile
subclassFeatures: Channel Divinity: What You Can't See|Cleric|PHB|Guile|IRKCore|2

subclass.name: Way of Deception
subclassFeatures: The Art of the Twisted Visage|Monk|PHB|Deception|IRKCore|3

subclassFeature.name: Bonus Proficiencies
entries: When you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you gain proficiency with medium armor and martial firearms.

subclassFeature.name: Grenadier
entries: Beginning at 3rd level, you increase the range of your thrown weapons and grenades by 10 feet and the maximum range by 30 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Potent Grenades
entries: Starting at 6th level, when you create grenades, you add a custom catalyzing ingredient to increase the yield of the payload. Increase the Area of Effect (AOE) of grenades you make by 5 feet. In addition, you add your Intelligence modifier to the damage roll of your grenades.

subclassFeature.name: Fire in the Hole!
entries: Starting at 10th level, friendly creatures that can hear you have advantage on saving throws to resist the effects of your grenades. In addition, friendly creatures in the blast radius have resistance to damage caused by your grenades.

subclassFeature.name: Prowl
entries: At 10th level, while you are lightly obscured, creatures have disadvantage on ranged weapon attacks against you.

subclassFeature.name: Parting Gift
entries: At 14th level, if you take the Disengage action, you can drop a primed grenade at your feet before moving. The grenade detonates after you finish moving.

subclassFeature.name: Wild Shot
entries: Starting at 18th level, you can take the Attack action as a bonus action on your turn to throw a grenade. You make the attack roll with disadvantage.

subclassFeature.name: New Fast Brew: Simple Stimulant
entries: Starting when you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you gain the following fast brew option.

entries.name: Simple Stimulant
entries: You mix ingredients to create a dose of simple stimulant, a bitter, milky fluid that gives a creature a surge of energy and alertness. As an action, a creature can consume a dose to ignore the highest level of exhaustion it currently suffers for 1 hour. In addition, a creature can consume a dose during a short rest to reduce its exhaustion level by 1.

subclassFeature.name: Revitalizing Alchemy
entries: At 3rd level, the alchemical items you create have an added revitalizing effect. Any creature that consumes an alchemical item you created regains hit points equal to your Intelligence modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Brew Master
entries: Starting at 6th level, when you use an alchemical item, you can reroll any number of dice and must use the new roll. Once you use this feature, you must finish a short or long rest before you can use it again.

subclassFeature.name: Efficient Brewing
entries: Beginning at 6th level, when you create an alchemical item or grenade, you produce twice the normal amount.

subclassFeature.name: Ingredient Improvisation
entries: Starting at 10th level, you learn to make do without the proper ingredients. When you create alchemical items, you can ignore a number of required ingredients equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum 1).

subclassFeature.name: Liquid Courage
entries: When you reach 10th level, while you are under the influence of an alchemical item, you have advantage on saving throws against being charmed or frightened.

subclassFeature.name: Volatile Reaction
entries: By 14th level, you can turn any alchemical substance into a powerful weapon. As a bonus action, you can mix 1 unit of alchemical waste (liquid) into any alchemical item. You can throw the item as if it were an AOE 10 grenade. The item deals {@damage 4d10} acid, fire, necrotic, or poison damage (your choice).

subclassFeature.name: Synthesize
entries: At 18th level, you become proficient in mixing otherwise incompatible formulas. When you create an alchemical item, you can choose to combine the effects of two formulas. In addition, when you create a grenade, you can choose to combine the effects of two grenade types.

subclassFeature.name: Bonus Proficiencies
entries: When you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you gain proficiency with Stealth and your choice of a poisoner's kit or thieves' tools.

subclassFeature.name: New Fast Brews
entries: Starting when you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you gain the following fast brew options.

items: {@b Glasseater.} You produce 1 ounce of glasseater, a thick blue-green paste that silently dissolves glass into a harmless vapor. 1 ounce covers a 1-square-foot surface.

{@b Umbroculant Drops.} You mix compounds to create a dose of umbroculant drops, an inky-black substance. As an action, you can apply the drops to your own eyes or those of a willing creature, granting darkvision out to 60 feet for a number of minutes equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum 1).

subclassFeature.name: Ingredient Scrounger
entries: By 6th level, you have developed a keen eye for finding natural ingredients in the environment. Once per hour while you travel, you can make a Wisdom (Perception) check to scrounge for an alchemical ingredient. The DC equals 10 + the ingredient's cost in gp. On a successful check, you discover {@dice 1d4} units of the ingredient. The GM has final say on whether a particular ingredient is available nearby.

subclassFeature.name: Waste Not, Want Not
entries: Beginning at 6th level, you can use alchemical waste as a weapon. You can splash a vial of alchemical waste onto a creature within 5 feet of you or throw the vial up to 20 feet. In either case, make a ranged attack against a creature or object, treating the alchemical waste as an improvised weapon. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 2d6} necrotic damage.

subclassFeature.name: Contaminator
entries: Starting at 10th level, you can coat a weapon with poison as a bonus action instead of an action, and your attacks that deal poison or necrotic damage deal extra damage equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum 1).

subclassFeature.name: Alchemical Magpie
entries: Starting at 14th level, you can attempt to determine the effects of an alchemical item by studying it for 1 minute and making a Wisdom (Perception) check (DC = the creator's Intelligence score + proficiency bonus).
If you spend 1 hour distilling an alchemical item, you can discover how it was brewed and add its formula to the formulas you know without needing a copy of the specific formula.

subclassFeature.name: Life on the Run
entries: At 18th level, when you attack a target you can't see, you do not have disadvantage on your attack rolls against it just because you can't see it. In addition, you are aware of the location of any invisible creature within 30 feet of you if the creature isn't hiding from you and if you aren't blinded or deafened.

subclassFeature.name: Order Rune Shot
entries: Starting at 1st level when you choose this order, you learn the Thunderbolt rune shot. This rune shot does not count toward the total number of rune shots you can know.

subclassFeature.name: Gunfighter (Arcane Tempest)
entries: At 1st level, you no longer have disadvantage on attack rolls made against targets within 5 feet of you if you are attacking with pistols or carbines.

subclassFeature.name: Reciprocate
entries: Starting at 6th level, when a hostile creature misses you with a ranged attack, you can use your reaction to perform an Attack action with a bonded weapon against the attacking creature. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short rest.

subclassFeature.name: Runesmith
entries: Beginning at 10th level, you can channel your arcane power into the ammunition fired by a steamjack you command. Only a steamjack armed with a ranged weapon firing pre-etched shots can benefit. Etching a single round takes 1 hour and 5 gp in rare metals.

During your turn, as a bonus action, you can expend one use of your Rune Shots feature to empower the next rune-etched round fired by a steamjack within 30 feet of you with a single rune shot you know.

subclassFeature.name: Moment of Clarity
entries: When you reach 14th level, on your turn you can use a bonus action to gain truesight out to your bonded weapon's normal range until the start of your next turn. Additionally, you deal an extra {@damage 3d6} weapon damage on the first rune shot attack you make during your turn.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Order Rune Shot
entries: Starting at 1st level when you choose this order, you learn the Silencer rune shot. This rune shot does not count toward the total number of rune shots you can know.

subclassFeature.name: Ambusher
entries: At 1st level, you master ambushing techniques. When you roll initiative, you can choose to roll with advantage. Additionally, during your first turn each combat, before making an attack, you can take the Dash or Hide action as a bonus action.

subclassFeature.name: Martyr of the Resistance
entries: Starting at 6th level, when you make an ability check or a saving throw, you can spend one of your Hit Dice and add the result to your roll. You can do so after seeing the initial roll but must do so before any of the roll's effects occur. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Slayer of Tyrants
entries: Beginning at 10th level, the first time each turn you hit a creature with a rune shot attack, you deal an extra {@damage 1d10} force damage. The creature cannot benefit from any damage resistances it possesses against this damage.

subclassFeature.name: Emergency Rune Shots
entries: When you reach 14th level, if you roll initiative and have expended all uses of your Rune Shots feature, you regain all expended uses. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Order Rune Shot
entries: Starting at 1st level when you choose this order, you learn the Trick Shot rune shot. This rune shot does not count toward the total number of rune shots you can know.

subclassFeature.name: Wild Shot
entries: At 1st level, when you make a ranged attack with a firearm with which you have proficiency, you can expend a gun mage spell slot to enhance the bullet with raw magical energy. The attack deals an extra {@damage 1d8} damage per level of the spell slot used. Roll a d8 to determine the damage type: 1 Acid, 2 Cold, 3 Fire, 4 Force, 5 Lightning, 6 Necrotic, 7 Poison, 8 Thunder.

subclassFeature.name: Extra Attack
entries: Starting at 6th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, when you take the Attack action on your turn.

subclassFeature.name: A Few More Shots
entries: Starting at 10th level, when you expend a gun mage spell slot of 1st level or higher to regain uses of the Rune Shots feature, treat the spell slot as one level higher. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Runic Improvisation
entries: Beginning at 14th level, when you use your Rune Shots feature, you can use a rune shot you don't already know to resolve your next rune shot attack. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Gunslinger
entries: At 3rd level, you no longer have disadvantage on attack rolls made against targets within 5 feet of you if you are attacking with pistols.

subclassFeature.name: Quick Draw
entries: At 3rd level, you add your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1) to your initiative rolls, and you can draw or stow two pistols when you would normally be able to draw or stow only one.

subclassFeature.name: Additional Trick Shots
entries: At 6th level, you learn the Return Fire and Snipe trick shots.

items: {@b Return Fire.} When a creature targets you with a ranged attack, before it makes the attack roll, you can use your reaction to make a ranged attack against the creature. If your attack deals damage, the creature makes its ranged attack roll with disadvantage.
{@b Snipe.} As part of an attack, when you attack a creature beyond normal range, you do not have disadvantage on the attack roll.

subclassFeature.name: Chain Shot
entries: At 11th level, when you perform a ranged weapon attack against a target you have already hit with a ranged weapon attack during your turn, you make the attack roll with advantage.

subclassFeature.name: Barrage
entries: At 17th level, when you take the Attack action, you can choose to fire a barrage with a single pistol or rifle. As part of the attack, make an attack roll against a number of creatures you can see within 30 feet equal to the number of bullets in your weapon's magazine. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Crack Shot
entries: At 3rd level, your ranged weapon attacks ignore half cover, and you do not have disadvantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks in a lightly obscured area.

subclassFeature.name: Steady Aim
entries: At 3rd level, if you do not move during your turn and make a ranged attack with a rifle at long range, you do not have disadvantage on the attack roll.

subclassFeature.name: Prone Shooter
entries: At 6th level, while you are prone, you do not have disadvantage on attack rolls you make with a rifle.

subclassFeature.name: Trick Shot: Dual Shot
entries: At 6th level, you learn the Dual Shot trick shot.

entries.name: Dual Shot
entries: You can use your Trick Shots feature to fire two bullets in quick succession. Once per turn, as part of the same Attack action, you can perform an additional attack against the same target.

subclassFeature.name: Targeteer
entries: At 11th level, as an action you can study a creature you can see and make a Wisdom (Insight) check (DC = 5 + the creature's CR). On a success, you roll one extra damage die on ranged weapon attacks against that creature. This effect lasts until you finish a short or long rest or designate a different creature.

subclassFeature.name: Sniper
entries: Starting at 17th level, once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a ranged weapon attack, choose one of the following options:

items: {@b Headshot.} The target takes additional damage equal to your Wisdom modifier. If the target is a living creature, it must make a Constitution saving throw against your trick shot DC or be stunned until the end of its next turn.

{@b Crippling Shot.} The target takes additional damage equal to your Wisdom modifier. If the target is a creature, it must make a Constitution saving throw against your trick shot DC or have its speed halved and have disadvantage on Dexterity saving throws for 1 minute.

subclassFeature.name: Bonus Proficiencies
entries: When you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you gain proficiency with martial weapons and grenades, as well as proficiency in Stealth. If you already have proficiency in Stealth, you double your proficiency bonus on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

subclassFeature.name: Combat Awareness
entries: At 3rd level, you gain a +5 bonus to your passive Wisdom (Perception) score and a +5 bonus when you roll initiative.

subclassFeature.name: Rapid Strike
entries: At 6th level, if you hit a creature with a ranged weapon attack and the creature has not yet acted during the round, you deal an extra {@damage 1d6} damage. This increases to {@damage 2d6} at 11th level and {@damage 3d6} at 17th level.

subclassFeature.name: Brutal Assault
entries: Beginning at 6th level, once per turn, after making a ranged attack targeting a creature you can see, you can make a melee attack as a bonus action.

subclassFeature.name: Stalker
entries: Starting at 11th level, you make Dexterity (Stealth) checks with advantage, and you can take a bonus action on each of your turns to take the Hide action.

subclassFeature.name: Tactical Flexibility
entries: Starting at 17th level, at the start of each of your turns, choose one of the following options:

items: {@b Silent Operation.} You only give away your position when you attack from hiding if your attack hits. On a miss, a creature must succeed on a Perception check contested by your Stealth check to know your position.
{@b Tactical Reposition.} You can take the Dash action as a bonus action on your turn.
{@b Sabotage.} The first ranged attack you make each turn against an object, structure, or vehicle deals double damage.

subclassFeature.name: Bonus Proficiencies
entries: When you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you gain proficiency with infantry armor, martial pistols, and martial rifles.

subclassFeature.name: Sterner Stuff
entries: At 3rd level, your hit point maximum increases by 2, and it increases by 2 every time you gain a level in this class.

subclassFeature.name: Iron Sentinel
entries: Starting at 7th level, while you are within 5 feet of a friendly steamjack or vehicle, you gain a +2 bonus to AC and cannot be knocked prone.

subclassFeature.name: Sabotage
entries: Starting at 10th level, when you attack a construct, vehicle, or structure, you have advantage on the attack roll. The target must make a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Intelligence modifier). On a failure, the attack deals extra damage equal to your Intelligence modifier, and the target is incapacitated if it is a construct or vehicle. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns.
Once you use this feature, you must finish a short or long rest before you can use it again.

subclassFeature.name: Applied Mechaniks
entries: Starting at 15th level, when you hit a construct, vehicle, or structure with a weapon attack, you can reroll the weapon's damage dice. If you do so, you must use the new roll.

subclassFeature.name: Tune Up
entries: Starting at 18th level, as an action or part of an attack action, you can tune up your weapons or those of a friendly creature or steamjack within 5 feet. The tuned weapons gain the following benefits for 1 round:

items: The first attack roll made with the weapon has advantage.
The first hit deals extra weapon damage equal to your Tinkering die.
The weapon loses the Misfire property.

subclassFeature.name: Ironclad
entries: At 3rd level, you gain proficiency with steam armor. In addition, you construct a suit of standard-class steam armor that you wear.

subclassFeature.name: Custom Modifications
entries: At 3rd level, choose one of the following modifications for your steam armor:

items: {@b Amphibious Construction.} Your armor is fully enclosed and waterproof, functioning for up to 5 minutes while fully submerged.
{@b Heavy Boiler.} You can perform the Dash action as a bonus action. Once used, you can't use it again until you finish a short rest.
{@b Ejection Rig.} You can take off your steam armor as a bonus action (even if destroyed), but must spend 1 hour reassembling it before wearing it again.

subclassFeature.name: Powerstrain
entries: Starting at 7th level, while wearing steam armor, you can take a bonus action to use one of the following options. Once used, you must finish a short rest before using this feature again.

items: {@b Powercharge.} Your speed in steam armor increases by 10 feet until the start of your next turn.
{@b Haul.} You have advantage on Strength checks for a number of rounds equal to your proficiency bonus.
{@b Mechanikal Smite.} If you hit a creature with a melee attack this round, it takes extra damage equal to your Tinkering die.
{@b Steam Warrior Stance.} You can't be knocked prone and cannot be moved by nonmagical enemy effects for a number of rounds equal to your proficiency bonus.

subclassFeature.name: Additional Modifications
entries: At 10th level, choose one additional modification for your steam armor:

items: {@b Ablative Armor.} Resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage from nonmagical weapons (choose one).
{@b Corrosive Shielding.} Resistance to acid damage.
{@b Electrical Shielding.} Resistance to lightning damage.
{@b Fire Shielding.} Resistance to fire damage.
{@b Temperature Regulators.} Resistance to cold damage.

subclassFeature.name: Improved Powerstrain
entries: Starting at 15th level, you can use your Powerstrain feature three times between rests.

subclassFeature.name: Hot Swap
entries: At 18th level, you add a final modification to your armor. In addition, at the end of a long rest, you can replace one of your modifications with another option. Choose one of the following:

items: {@b Artillery Mount.} You add a mount that can accommodate a light steamjack's ranged weapon.
{@b Cargo Crane.} Your crane can lift up to 8,000 pounds, but you cannot move and use the crane in the same round.
{@b Hardened Case.} Your armor's damage threshold increases by 5.

{@b Steam Venting.} As an action, vent steam in a 20-foot square centered on you. Each other creature must make a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 3d6} fire damage on a failure, or half on a success. The cloud dissipates at the start of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Spellcasting
entries: By 3rd level, you have learned to cast spells from the {@filter arcane mechanik spell list|spells|class=Arcane Mechanik} (see Chapter 3).

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: You know two cantrips from the {@filter arcane mechanik spell list|spells|class=Arcane Mechanik}. You gain a third at 10th level.

entries.name: Spell Slots
entries: See the Arcane Mechanik Spellcasting table. You regain all expended spell slots on a long rest. Spell levels available: 1st at level 3; 2nd at level 7; 3rd at level 13; 4th at level 19.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Intelligence is your spellcasting ability.

entries.name: Ritual Casting
entries: You can cast a known spell as a ritual if it has the ritual tag.

subclassFeature.name: Tool Proficiency
entries: When you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you gain proficiency with a rune-etching kit. If you are already proficient with a rune-etching kit, you gain proficiency with one other type of artisan's tools of your choice.

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Crafting
entries: By 3rd level, you have developed the ability to inscribe rune formulae on runeplates, design and construct mechanikal housings, and build capacitors. When you craft items, you can choose to craft mechanikal items. You can still benefit from your Tinkering die when crafting these items.

subclassFeature.name: Resourceful
entries: Starting at 7th level, you have advantage on saving throws to maintain concentration.

subclassFeature.name: Know the Machine
entries: Beginning at 7th level, you can use your Intelligence modifier instead of Strength or Dexterity for the attack and damage rolls of your mechanikal weapons.

subclassFeature.name: Empower
entries: Starting at 10th level, as a bonus action you can expend any level of spell slot to replenish the capacitor of a mechanikal item you have equipped or can touch. The item regains a number of charges equal to 10 × the spell slot's level.

subclassFeature.name: Mechanikal Unity
entries: Starting at 15th level, you can attune to mechanikal items as normal magical items. When attuned to a mechanikal item, at the start of a combat encounter choose one effect lasting 1 minute:

items: {@b Precise Mechanikal Effect.} If a mechanikal weapon deals extra damage, you can reroll the extra damage dice but must keep the new roll.
{@b Prolonged Mechanikal Effect.} You double the duration of the mechanikal item's effects.

entries: Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Arcantrik Absorption
entries: Starting at 18th level, as an action you can drain all remaining charges of a capacitor from a mechanikal item not worn or carried by another creature (must have at least 1 charge). You replenish one spell slot with a level equal to the capacitor's power output. If you have no expended spell slots, you instead gain {@dice 1d6} temporary hit points per point of the capacitor's power output, lasting 10 minutes or until you finish a short rest.

subclassFeature.name: Masterwork
entries: At 18th level, your progress toward building a capacitor increases by a number of gp equal to your Mechanik level, and the time required to inscribe runeplates and build capacitors is halved. When you use Tinkering dice during crafting, you can expend and roll any number of them, increasing your daily progress by an additional 10 gp × the number rolled.

subclassFeature.name: Bonus Proficiencies
entries: At 1st level, you gain proficiency with a mechanik's toolkit.

subclassFeature.name: Patch Up
entries: At 1st level, you can make minor repairs to a bonded steamjack. When you finish a long rest, you can restore a number of hit points equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum 1) to a bonded steamjack in your control range. You must have access to a mechanik's toolkit to make these repairs.

subclassFeature.name: Controller Bond
entries: When you choose this tradition at 1st level, you refine your ability to bond with steamjacks. You can choose to replace your bond with a mechanikal weapon with a steamjack bond, which works as described in the Steamjack Bond feature. Bonding with that steamjack does not count toward the total number of items you can be attuned with.
When you gain a level in this class, you can choose a new steamjack to bond to. If you do so, the bond with your previously bonded steamjack is severed.

subclassFeature.name: Clapped-Out Steamjack
entries: When you choose this tradition at 1st level, you gain control of a broken-down steamjack made of salvaged components, junkyard scrap, and bolted-on plates. This steamjack is much smaller than a normal laborjack due to missing hull plating, ersatz replacement components, and other workarounds. The clapped-out steamjack does not come with weapons or equipment and has a Ferrum-grade cortex.

entries.name: Clapped-Out Steamjack
entries: Medium construct, unaligned

rows: 12 (natural armor)
30 ft.

entries: {@b Damage Immunities} poison, psychic
{@b Condition Immunities} charmed, frightened, paralyzed, poisoned
{@b Senses} passive Perception 10
{@b Languages} Understands the language of its manufacturer but can't speak
{@b Challenge} —

{@b Clapped Out.} The steamjack cannot take the Dash action or the Disengage action. At the end of a turn in which the steamjack rolls a 1 on a skill check or an attack roll, the steamjack is stunned until the end of its next turn.

{@b Cortex.} The steamjack's cortex allows it to understand basic commands from its controller (spoken verbally by most but usually communicated telepathically by warcasters). Verbal commands must be akin to those issued to a trained animal, such as "stay," "guard," "attack," and so on. The steamjack will carry out these commands with no regard for its own safety.

{@b Steam Powered.} The steamjack requires coal and water to function. When not in combat, it can function for 7 hours with 200 pounds of coal and fresh water in its boiler. While in combat, it can function for 1 hour with a full fuel load. If its coal and water are not refilled at the end of this time, the steamjack suffers 1 level of exhaustion at the end of each minute.
{@b Actions}
{@b Slam.} Melee Weapon Attack: +3 plus your proficiency bonus to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: {@damage 1d8} bludgeoning damage plus extra bludgeoning damage equal to your proficiency bonus.

subclassFeature.name: Focus Manipulation: Steamjack Attack
entries: Starting at 6th level, a bonded steamjack in your control range can spend 1 allocated focus point to attack twice, instead of once, whenever it takes the Attack action on its turn.

subclassFeature.name: Overpower
entries: Starting at 10th level, you can direct a surge of arcane power to your steamjacks. When a steamjack gains focus from your Power Up feature, you can increase the amount of focus it receives to 2. You must finish a long rest before you can use this feature again.

subclassFeature.name: Field Marshal
entries: Starting at 14th level, you can spend 1 focus point to grant one of the following benefits to all bonded steamjacks that are under your control and in your control range. This benefit lasts until the start of your next turn.

items: {@b Aggressive.} As a bonus action, the steamjack can move up to its speed toward a hostile creature that it can see.

{@b Flank.} When the steamjack makes a melee attack against a hostile creature within reach of a friendly creature, the steamjack gains a +2 bonus to the attack roll and scores a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20.
{@b Unyielding.} Melee attacks against the steamjack are made with disadvantage.

subclassFeature.name: United Battlegroup
entries: Starting at 18th level, when you cast a spell with a range of self, you can spend 1 additional focus point while casting the spell to have it affect all bonded steamjacks that are currently under your control and in your control range.

subclassFeature.name: Bonus Cantrip
entries: When you choose this tradition at 1st level, you learn one additional warcaster cantrip of your choice.

subclassFeature.name: Sharper Focus
entries: Beginning at 1st level, your maximum number of focus points increases by 1. Starting at 6th level, the focus cost of your spells decreases by 1 to a minimum of 1.

subclassFeature.name: Spell Manipulation
entries: Starting at 6th level, you learn how to channel focus to augment and modify the power of your spells. Once per round, you can spend focus points for one of the following effects.

items: {@b Enhanced Concentration.} When you are forced to make a Constitution saving throw to maintain concentration on a spell, you can spend 1 focus point to make the roll with advantage.

{@b Enhanced Damage.} When you roll damage for a spell, you can spend 1 focus point to reroll a number of damage dice up to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of one die). You must use the new rolls.
{@b Enhanced Range.} When you cast a spell that has a range of 5 feet or greater, you can spend 1 focus point to double the range of the spell.

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Mastery
entries: Starting at 10th level, choose one 5th-level spell from the {@filter warcaster spell list|spells|class=Warcaster} as your signature spell. You can cast your signature spell once without expending focus points.
Once you use this feature, you must finish a long rest before you can use it again.

subclassFeature.name: Spell Resistance
entries: Starting at 14th level, you have advantage on saving throws against spells. Furthermore, you have resistance against spell damage.

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Dominance
entries: Starting at 18th level, when you cast a spell of 1st to 5th level that deals damage, you can spend focus points to ignore the damage resistance of creatures affected by the spell. You ignore one type of resistance per focus point spent.

subclassFeature.name: Bonus Proficiencies
entries: At 1st level, you gain proficiency with martial weapons, martial firearms, and shields.

subclassFeature.name: Soldier Bond
entries: When you choose this tradition at 1st level, you refine your ability to bond with mechanikal weapons and armor. Choose one mechanikal weapon or suit of warcaster armor in your possession that you are proficient with. You bond to that item. Bonding with that item does not count to the total number of items you can be attuned with.
When you gain a level in this class, you can replace your bonded item with another mechanikal weapon or suit of warcaster armor.

subclassFeature.name: Focus Manipulation: Extra Attack
entries: Starting at 6th level, you can spend 1 focus point to attack twice, instead of once, when you take the Attack action on your turn.

subclassFeature.name: Revitalizing Focus
entries: Starting at 10th level, you can draw on your focus to replenish your strength and stamina. On your turn, you can use a bonus action and spend 1 focus point to gain temporary hit points equal to {@dice 1d10} + your warcaster level.
Once you use this feature, you must finish a long rest before you can use it again.

subclassFeature.name: Focus Fueled
entries: Starting at 14th level, you gain the ability to further augment your attacks with your focus. As a bonus action, you can expend 1 focus point to grant one mechanikal weapon you touch a bonus when you attack with it. While affected by this bonus, the weapon scores a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20. This bonus lasts for 1 minute or until you use it again.

subclassFeature.name: Vengeance Strike
entries: Starting at 18th level, when you take damage from a creature that is within 5 feet of you, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack against that creature.

subclassFeature.name: Fell Call
entries: Fell callers combine terrifying shout attacks with respectable combat ability. All fell callers in the Iron Kingdoms are trollkin, descendants of the ancient trollkin hero Bragg.

At 3rd level, you learn how to perform various fell calls, bellowing shouts that can cause your enemies to tremble in fear or instill your allies with unnatural confidence. You can use a bonus action to begin a fell call, or you can begin a fell call when you use Bardic Inspiration. You can maintain a fell call for up to 1 minute but must use either a bonus action or Bardic Inspiration each round to do so.

Performing a fell call does not prevent you from speaking other words or from casting spells with verbal components. If you are unable to speak at any time while maintaining a fell call, the fell call ends immediately.

You can begin a fell call a number of times equal to your Constitution modifier (a minimum of once). You regain any expended uses when you finish a long rest. When you begin a fell call, choose one of the following options.

entries.name: Fell Howl
entries: While you perform this fell call, each enemy creature that can hear you within 60 feet of you has disadvantage on saving throws against being frightened or charmed and takes a −1 penalty on attack rolls and damage rolls.

entries.name: Quaking Call
entries: While you perform this fell call, all nonmagical objects with an AC of 15 or lower that are not being worn or carried within 60 feet of you take 1 thunder damage at the end of each of your turns. You can expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration to increase the damage of this fell call. If you do, roll a Bardic Inspiration die and add the number to the damage dealt at the end of each of your turns.

entries.name: Reveille
entries: While you perform this fell call, each friendly creature that can hear you within 60 feet of you has advantage on saving throws against being frightened or charmed and cannot be knocked prone. Any friendly creature in range that is prone when you begin this fell call may immediately stand up.

entries.name: Stay Death
entries: While you perform this fell call, each friendly creature within 60 feet of you becomes stable at the end of your turn. This fell call has no effect on undead or constructs.

subclassFeature.name: Sonic Blast
entries: Beginning at 6th level, you can use an action to channel your shouts into sonic waves of pure destruction. Each creature in a 15-foot cone originating from you must make a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC. A creature takes {@damage 3d6} thunder damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Any exposed brittle objects that an affected creature in the cone is carrying are destroyed.
Your shout is so potent that it can penetrate spells that negate sound, such as {@spell silence}.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Constitution modifier (a minimum of once). You regain any expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Signal Call
entries: At 14th level, you learn to project your voice across vast distances. You can use Signal Call for up to 1 minute. While you use this feature, your fell calls affect creatures within 120 feet of you, the range of your Sonic Blast increases to a 40-foot cone, and the damage of your Sonic Blast increases to {@damage 5d6} thunder damage.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Constitution modifier (a minimum of once). You regain any expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Benefaction Domain Spells
entries: Morrow emphasizes the pursuit of the greater good, and his followers believe that living a good life requires benevolence, mercy, compassion, and self-sacrifice.
You gain domain spells at the cleric levels listed in the Benefaction Domain Spells table. See the Divine Domain class feature for how domain spells work.

entries.caption: Benefaction Domain Spells
rows: 1st
{@spell Blessing of Health|IRKCore}, {@spell Solovin's Boon|IRKCore}
3rd
{@spell Divine Sight|IRKCore}, {@spell Force of Faith|IRKCore}
5th
{@spell Banishing Ward|IRKCore}, {@spell Blade of Radiance|IRKCore}
7th
{@spell Blessings of Morrow|IRKCore}, {@spell Star Fire|IRKCore}
9th
{@spell Circle of Renewal|IRKCore}, {@spell Dayspring|IRKCore}

subclassFeature.name: Blessing of Self-Sacrifice
entries: Starting at 1st level, whenever you use a spell to restore hit points to a creature, you can choose a number of hit points up to your cleric level or your current hit point total, whichever is lower. You restore an additional number of hit points equal to the amount chosen, and you reduce your current hit points by an equal amount.
Once you use this feature it cannot be used again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Empowerment
entries: When you choose this domain at 1st level, you can use your action to touch a willing creature other than yourself and empower it with strength. When the chosen creature takes the Attack action, it can make one weapon attack as a bonus action. This blessing lasts for 1 hour or until the creature makes an attack granted by this feature.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (a minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: Hallowed Radiance
entries: Starting at 2nd level, you can use your Channel Divinity to banish darkness and aid those in need. As an action, you present your holy symbol, causing a sphere of radiance to emanate from you. When you use this feature, you have a pool of hit points equal to 5 × your cleric level that you can use to manipulate the vitality of creatures. Choose any creatures within 30 feet of you, and divide those hit points among them. For each creature, you can choose whether you restore lost hit points to the creature or the creature takes radiant damage instead.

This feature can restore a creature to no more than half of its hit point maximum and cannot reduce a creature below half of its hit point maximum. You can't use this feature on a construct or to restore hit points to an undead.

subclassFeature.name: Sacred Volition
entries: At 6th level, the companions you empower fight with greater zeal and divinely inspired strength. When a creature under the effect of your Empowerment feature makes an attack as a bonus action, it deals extra radiant damage equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1).
In addition, your Empowerment feature lasts for 1 hour or until the affected creature makes a number of bonus attacks granted by Empowerment equal to your proficiency bonus.

subclassFeature.name: Divine Strike
entries: At 8th level, you gain the ability to infuse your weapon strikes with divine energy. Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra {@damage 1d8} radiant damage. When you reach 14th level, the extra damage increases to {@damage 2d8}.

subclassFeature.name: Supreme Empowerment
entries: Starting at 17th level, you can use a bonus action to use your Empowerment feature. Additionally, you can expend a spell slot of 2nd level or higher and choose a number of friendly creatures within 30 feet of you equal to the level of the spell slot you expend. Each creature you choose is affected by your Empowerment feature.

subclassFeature.name: Guile Domain Spells
entries: Thamar, the Dark Twin, claims that she wants to liberate minds and free them from the shackles of morality. Her priests wield divine magic for the purpose of acquiring power and punishing those who stand in their way.
You gain domain spells at the cleric levels listed in the Guile Domain Spells table. See the Divine Domain class feature for how domain spells work.

entries.caption: Guile Domain Spells
rows: 1st
{@spell Bleed|IRKCore}, {@spell Cloak of Fear|IRKCore}
3rd
{@spell Roth's Madness|IRKCore}, {@spell Chains of Ekris|IRKCore}
5th
{@spell Dark Fire|IRKCore}, {@spell Devil's Tongue|IRKCore}
7th
{@spell Stacia's Hellfire|IRKCore}, {@spell Stygian Abyss|IRKCore}
9th
{@spell Scourge of Khorva|IRKCore}, {@spell Aidan's Ghostly Shroud|IRKCore}

subclassFeature.name: Initiate of Deception
entries: When you choose this domain at 1st level, you gain proficiency in the {@skill Deception} skill. In addition, you know the {@spell eldritch blast} cantrip. This cantrip counts as a cleric cantrip for you, but it doesn't count against the number of cleric cantrips you know.

subclassFeature.name: Shrouded Magic
entries: At 1st level, you learn to weave magic with a subtlety others lack. You can suppress the verbal and somatic components of your spells to a point where only those who are paying close attention can notice them, and your spells do not produce spell runes when cast.

If you are hiding from the target of a spell when you cast it, make a Charisma ({@skill Deception}) check contested by the creature's Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) check. If you win the contest, you remain hidden.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: What You Can't See
entries: Starting at 2nd level, you can use your Channel Divinity to augment your magical powers to afflict others, robbing them of their ability to see your actions. When you cast a spell that deals damage to one or more creatures, you can use your Channel Divinity to add your Wisdom modifier to the damage. Any creature you damage with this modified spell must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition blinded} until the end of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Unwitting Ally
entries: At 6th level, you can bind another creature to your will as an unwitting ally. As a bonus action you can expend a spell slot to mark a creature you can see within 30 feet of you with an occult brand. When you cast a spell, you can choose to use the marked creature to determine the range and point of origin of the spell, after which the creature is no longer marked. A marked creature must be within 150 feet of you for you to use it in this way. The creature remains marked for a number of hours equal to the level of the spell slot you expend.

subclassFeature.name: Potent Spellcasting
entries: Starting at 8th level, you add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any cleric cantrip.

subclassFeature.name: Irrefutable Power
entries: At 17th level, your pursuit of power reaches its ultimate end. When a creature you can see makes a saving throw caused by one of your spells, you can expend a use of your Channel Divinity to cause the saving throw to be made with disadvantage. If the result is a failure and your spell damages the target, you also mark your target as described in the Unwitting Ally feature for 1 hour without expending a spell slot, regardless of range.

subclassFeature.name: Obedience Domain Spells
entries: Clerics of the Lawgiver draw their power from preaching to the flock and maintaining their vows. They are stern, controlled, and disciplined individuals with the bearing of a soldier and the wisdom of a stern taskmaster.
You gain domain spells at the cleric levels listed in the Obedience Domain Spells table. See the Divine Domain class feature for how domain spells work.

entries.caption: Obedience Domain Spells
rows: 1st
{@spell Flames of Wrath|IRKCore}, {@spell Guided Blade|IRKCore}
3rd
{@spell Ashes to Ashes|IRKCore}, {@spell Lawgiver's Wrath|IRKCore}
5th
{@spell Cleansing Fire|IRKCore}, {@spell Rebuke the Lawbreaker|IRKCore}
7th
{@spell Lamentation|IRKCore}, {@spell Righteous Flames|IRKCore}
9th
{@spell Compelled Obedience|IRKCore}, {@spell Howling Flames|IRKCore}

subclassFeature.name: Bonus Proficiencies
entries: When you choose this domain at 1st level, you gain proficiency in the {@skill Intimidation} skill.

subclassFeature.name: Hymns
entries: At 1st level, you learn to inspire your allies with unifying hymns. As an action, you can perform a hymn. Any hymn you perform affects friendly creatures within 30 feet of you who can hear you and understand you, but a creature can be affected by only one hymn at a time. A hymn lasts until you lose your concentration (as if you were casting a concentration spell), you are incapacitated, or you use an action to perform another hymn.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum of once). You regain all uses of this feature when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Hymn of Battle
entries: Each affected creature gains a +2 bonus to the damage roll of the first weapon attack it makes each round.

entries.name: Hymn of Passage
entries: Whenever an affected creature takes bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage from a nonmagical weapon, that damage is reduced by 2.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: Lawgiver's Rebuke
entries: At 2nd level, as an action, you present your holy symbol and speak a prayer censuring those who deny the laws of your god, using your Channel Divinity. Each humanoid creature not of your faith or not friendly to you that can see or hear you within 30 feet must make a Wisdom saving throw. If the creature fails its saving throw, it is immediately knocked {@condition prone} and cowed for 1 minute.
If a cowed creature makes a weapon attack against you, a creature of your faith, or another creature friendly to you, it takes psychic damage equal to your proficiency bonus after the attack.

subclassFeature.name: Additional Hymns
entries: At 6th level, you learn additional hymns.

entries.name: Hymn of Divine Strength
entries: Any weapon attack made by an affected creature is considered a magical attack.

entries.name: Hymn of Obedience
entries: Affected creatures are immune to being {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened}.

subclassFeature.name: Divine Strike
entries: At 8th level, you gain the ability to infuse your weapon strikes with divine energy. Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra {@damage 1d8} fire damage to the target. When you reach 14th level, the extra damage increases to {@damage 2d8}.

subclassFeature.name: Choir
entries: At 17th level, your hymns inspire your companions to join you in your songs of devotion. As long as one other friendly creature affected by your hymns is not incapacitated, you cannot lose concentration on a hymn. In addition, the range of your hymns increases to 60 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Battle Chaplain Spellcasting
entries: When you reach 3rd level, you augment your martial prowess with the ability to cast spells.

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: You learn two cantrips of your choice from the {@filter cleric spell list|spells|class=Cleric}. You learn an additional cleric cantrip of your choice at 10th level.

entries.name: Spell Slots
entries: The Battle Chaplain Spellcasting table shows how many spell slots you have. To cast a spell, you must expend a slot of the spell's level or higher. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Spells Known
entries: You know two 1st-level cleric spells, both of which must be abjuration or evocation spells. The spells you learn at 8th, 14th, and 20th level can come from any school of magic. Whenever you gain a level in this class, you can replace one cleric spell you know with another spell from the {@filter cleric spell list|spells|class=Cleric}.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for your cleric spells.

subclassFeature.name: Bonus Proficiency
entries: Beginning when you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the {@skill Religion} skill.

subclassFeature.name: Sacred Ward
entries: Starting at 3rd level, the rites of your faith shield you from enemy magic. When a creature targets you with a spell attack, you can use your reaction to gain a bonus to your AC equal to your Wisdom modifier (with a minimum bonus of +1) against enemy spell attacks, and if the enemy spell causes you to make a saving throw, you make the roll with advantage.

subclassFeature.name: Battle Prayers
entries: Beginning at 7th level, when you use a spell to restore hit points to a creature, you can make one weapon attack as a bonus action.

subclassFeature.name: Spell Breaker
entries: Starting at 10th level, your strikes can mute an enemy's ability to work magic. When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, it has disadvantage on Constitution saving throws to maintain concentration. If you cause a spell to end in this way, you can restore an expended spell slot equal to or lower than the level of the spell you ended.

subclassFeature.name: Rise Above
entries: At 15th level, those who fight by your side are armored against the grip of death. Friendly creatures within 30 feet of you can add your Wisdom modifier to their death saving throws, and a roll of 1 on the d20 does not count as two failures.

subclassFeature.name: Blessed Tools of War
entries: At 18th level, your weapons and armor are imbued with holy radiance. Any weapons you wield are magic weapons, and while you are wearing medium or heavy armor, you have resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage caused by nonmagical weapons.

subclassFeature.name: Bodyguard
entries: Beginning when you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you can use your reaction to deflect blows when a friendly creature within 5 feet of you is hit by a melee weapon attack. When you do so, make a normal melee attack roll. If your result equals or beats the attacker's roll, the attacker's attack is deflected and automatically misses. If you are wielding a shield, you have advantage on this roll.

If you are wielding a weapon with the heavy and reach properties and you successfully deflect an attack, you can immediately make an opportunity attack against the attacker as part of the same reaction.

subclassFeature.name: Arm's Length
entries: At 7th level, you learn to master all forms of polearms, as well as new techniques for protecting your allies. Add 5 feet to your reach when you make an attack with a melee weapon, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks with it. The weapon must have the heavy and reach properties to gain these benefits.

If you are wielding a shield, when a friendly creature within 15 feet of you is hit by a melee weapon attack, you can use your reaction to move up to 15 feet toward the creature. If you end this movement within 5 feet of the creature, you can then use Bodyguard as part of this same reaction.

subclassFeature.name: Set Defense
entries: At 10th level, you gain a +2 bonus to AC against melee attacks if the attacker did not begin its turn within 10 feet of you.

subclassFeature.name: Pikeman
entries: Beginning at 15th level, you have advantage on melee attack rolls against mounted targets and their mounts. When you score a critical hit with a melee attack against a mounted target, the target is automatically dismounted and knocked {@condition prone}. Your weapon must have the heavy and reach properties to gain this benefit.
If you are wielding a shield, mounted creatures and their mounts have disadvantage on melee attack rolls against you and friendly creatures within 5 feet of you.

subclassFeature.name: Immovable Object
entries: At 18th level, you become an immovable wall of steel and determination. You cannot be stunned or knocked {@condition prone} by nonmagical effects.

If you are wielding a shield, you cannot be moved by nonmagical effects unless you want to be moved, and friendly creatures within 5 feet of you cannot be moved by nonmagical effects unless you want them to be moved.

subclassFeature.name: Training Complete
entries: Beginning when you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you have learned the fundamentals of wielding the unique arms and armor of the Storm Knight. You gain proficiency with Storm Knight armor and one of the following weapons of your choice: storm glaives, storm lances, storm throwers, or voltaic halberds.

If you are in good standing with the Storm Knight Academy in Cygnar, you receive one suit of Storm Knight armor and the storm weapon you gained proficiency with at no cost. In return, you must swear loyalty to both the Crown and the Storm Knights, and you may be called upon to serve in the Cygnaran military at the GM's discretion.

subclassFeature.name: Arc Lightning
entries: At 7th level, when you hit a target with a melee or ranged attack, you can arc lightning onto nearby enemies. When you do, choose another target within 5 feet of the creature you originally hit and make the same type of attack roll against the chosen target. If you hit, that target takes {@damage 1d8} lightning damage.

This additional attack cannot generate another Arc Lightning attack. Your weapon must have the storm property and must be activated to gain this benefit. Arc Lightning damage does not benefit from the additional damage granted by a weapon's storm property.

subclassFeature.name: Nobility
entries: Beginning at 10th level, your noble presence inspires your allies. Friendly creatures within 30 feet of you can use your Charisma modifier instead of their own when making a Charisma saving throw or a Charisma skill check.

Additionally, if you have not betrayed your loyalty to the Crown, you are granted noble status within the kingdom. You have advantage on Intimidation and Persuasion skill checks against Cygnaran citizens that are not royalty, and you are granted ownership of 10 acres of unsettled land within Cygnar.

subclassFeature.name: Veteran Leader
entries: At 15th level, you can direct the tides of battle by shouting commands through the din of combat. As a bonus action, you can use one of the following commands. Each command's benefit lasts until the start of your next turn. Friendly creatures cannot gain these benefits if they cannot see or hear you or cannot understand you. You can use this feature three times. You regain all expended uses after you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Hold the Line
entries: Creatures cannot score critical hits against you or friendly creatures within 30 feet of you. Additionally, you and friendly creatures within 30 feet of you cannot be killed by instant death or effects that kill you outright. Instead, affected creatures fall unconscious with 0 hit points.

entries.name: Press Forward
entries: You and friendly creatures within 30 feet of you increase their speed by 5 feet.

entries.name: Strike Back
entries: You and friendly creatures within 30 feet of you can use a reaction to make an opportunity attack with a melee weapon after a creature attacks them. This opportunity attack must target the attacking creature.

subclassFeature.name: Eye of the Storm
entries: At 18th level, you become a master of the Storm Knight tradition without equal. Your activated storm weapons deal {@damage 1d8} lightning damage instead of 1d4, your storm generator attacks deal {@damage 1d12} lightning damage instead of 1d8, and you can choose three different targets each time you use the Arc Lightning feature. Additionally, friendly creatures within 30 feet of you have immunity to lightning damage.
You must be wearing Storm Knight armor and wielding a weapon with the storm property to gain any benefits from this feature.

subclassFeature.name: The Art of the Twisted Visage
entries: Beginning when you choose this tradition at 3rd level, you learn to control your musculature to such a degree that you can alter your gait, facial expressions, voice, and physical demeanor, making you capable of becoming unrecognizable in a matter of moments.
You gain proficiency in the {@skill Deception} skill and proficiency with a disguise kit. Additionally, during your turn, you can spend 1 ki point to gain one of the following benefits.

entries.name: Blend In
entries: If you are in a crowd of humanoids, you can use a bonus action to make a Charisma ({@skill Deception}) check with advantage in an attempt to hide among the crowd, following the normal rules for hiding.

entries.name: Impersonate
entries: You can make your voice sound exactly like that of any humanoid you have heard speak before. This does not allow you to speak or understand its language. This benefit lasts for 1 hour. A creature that does not see you speaking cannot recognize that your voice is fake except by magical means.

entries.name: Mimic
entries: As a bonus action, you can change your face to look like another humanoid of the same race as your own. You are even capable of mimicking scars, but you cannot change your skin tone entirely. This benefit lasts for 1 hour. A creature can recognize that your face is fake by inspecting your appearance and succeeding on a Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) check contested by your Charisma ({@skill Deception}) check.

subclassFeature.name: Identity Thief
entries: At 6th level, you master a forbidden technique that allows you to absorb the memories of those you battle. When you target another creature with a melee weapon attack, you can spend 4 ki points to use this technique. If you hit, the attack deals an extra {@damage 2d8} psychic damage, and the target must succeed on an Intelligence saving throw or permanently lose one memory. If a humanoid target loses a memory in this way, you gain that memory with as much detail as the target had before losing it. If you are aware of knowledge the target has that you want, you can choose the memory the target loses; otherwise, the GM determines the memory. This feature cannot affect spells memorized by the target.

subclassFeature.name: Sense Emotions
entries: At 11th level, you can sense emotions, which appear to you as brightly colored auras that radiate around nearby creatures. You always know the emotional state of living sentient creatures within 60 feet of you.

Additionally, if you can sense a creature's emotional state, you have advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) and Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) checks to determine whether that creature is trying to deceive you. This emotional vision allows you to see creatures in complete darkness, as well as creatures that are {@condition invisible}. Physical objects block the glowing aura, thereby allowing creatures to hide from you normally.

subclassFeature.name: Master Manipulator
