entries: A spear with a reservoir of Menoth's Fury. On a hit, it deals an additional 1d6 fire damage. The reservoir holds enough fuel for 12 attacks before refueling. Refueling requires 5 gp worth of Menoth's Fury as an action or bonus action.

item.name: Springblade
entries: A dagger with a retractable blade released by pressing a button. You can draw or sheathe a springblade for free, even if you've already drawn or sheathed a weapon during your turn.
Hidden: The true nature of this weapon is concealed until revealed. A creature within 10 feet can discern it with passive Perception 20 or a DC 15 Perception check (or passive 10 if handling it).

item.name: Stave of Authority
entries: A weapon of steel or banded hardwood set with a heavy head, favored by the clergy of Menoth. Cost is GM-determined; not normally available for purchase. A stave of authority can be used as a holy symbol.

item.name: Bola
entries: A Large or smaller creature hit by a bola must succeed on a DC 12 Strength (Athletics) or Dexterity (Acrobatics) check or be knocked prone.

item.name: Pistol
entries: Magazine (1): Once you make 1 attack with this weapon, you must use an action or bonus action to reload it.

item.name: Pistol, Musket
entries: Magazine (1): Once you make 1 attack, you must use an action or bonus action to reload.

Misfire (4): If you roll a 4 or lower on the attack roll d20, the weapon jams. The attack automatically misses and the weapon can't be used again until you use an action or bonus action to clear the chamber.

item.name: Pistol, Repeating
entries: Magazine (5): Once you make 5 attacks, you must use an action or bonus action to reload.

item.name: Rivet Gun
entries: Magazine (10): Once you make 10 attacks, you must use an action or bonus action to reload.

item.name: Rynnish Holdout
entries: Magazine (2): Once you make 2 attacks, you must use an action or bonus action to reload.

item.name: Blunderbuss
entries: Magazine (1): Once you make 1 attack, you must use an action or bonus action to reload.
Misfire (3): If you roll a 3 or lower on the d20, the weapon jams. The attack misses and the weapon can't be used until cleared as an action or bonus action.

AOE (5): The ammunition explodes on impact. Each creature other than the target within 5 feet must make a DC 13 (or DC 10 + prof, whichever is higher) Dexterity saving throw or take half the damage dealt to the target.

item.name: Harpoon Gun
entries: A firearm designed to propel a harpoon. If a harpoon fired from a harpoon gun damages a creature, the harpoon embeds in it. As an action, you can attempt to pull a Large or smaller creature with an embedded harpoon 5 feet closer by making a contested Strength (Athletics) check. Dealing 5 slashing damage to the line (AC 7) destroys it.

item.name: Musket
entries: Magazine (1): Once you make 1 attack, you must use an action or bonus action to reload.
Misfire (4): If you roll a 4 or lower on the d20, the weapon jams. The attack misses and the weapon can't be used until cleared as an action or bonus action.

item.name: Rynnish Walking Stick
entries: Magazine (1): Once you make 1 attack, you must use an action or bonus action to reload.
Hidden: The true nature of this weapon is concealed until revealed. A creature within 10 feet can discern it with passive Perception 20 or a DC 15 Perception check.

item.name: Scattergun
entries: Magazine (1): Once you make 1 attack, you must use an action or bonus action to reload.

item.name: Blasting Pike
entries: The Iron Fang blasting pike is tipped with a powerful explosive charge. If you hit a target, you can choose to detonate the explosive head before rolling damage. The target takes an additional 1d10 bludgeoning damage and must make a Strength saving throw (DC = 10 or half total damage, whichever is higher) or be knocked prone. You cannot use this feature again until you rearm the pike (replacement heads cost 5 gp). A pike armed with a standard spearhead cannot be detonated but doesn't need rearming between attacks.
Rearm: You must have the explosive head component. Each time you use the explosive, you expend it and must spend a bonus action to attach a new head.

item.name: Nyss Claymore
entries: These weapons have become very rare since the disaster that befell the Nyss in 606 AR. Cost is GM-determined; the GM should first determine whether such a weapon is available before setting an appropriate price.

item.name: Sword Cane
entries: Hidden: The true nature of this weapon is concealed until revealed. A creature within 10 feet can discern it with passive Perception 20 or a DC 15 Perception check (or passive 10 / automatic if handling it and looking for concealed weapons).

item.name: Storm Glaive
entries: The fearsome mechanikal weapon of Cygnar's Stormblade infantry. Powered by a dedicated storm chamber (4 charges per minute of operation).

entries.name: Storm
entries: As a bonus action, you can activate or deactivate this weapon. While activated, it deals an additional 1d4 lightning damage per attack, but you also suffer 1d4 lightning damage after each attack.

entries.name: Storm Generator (range 20/60)
entries: You can use this weapon to discharge lightning as a ranged attack rather than a melee attack, but only while activated. Damage changes to 1d8 lightning damage.

item.name: Electro Lance
entries: A galvanically charged mechanikal lance, signature weapon of Cygnar's Storm Lances. Balanced for horseback charges, with a shorter blade above the pommel for close-in fighting. Powered by a storm chamber (4 charges per minute).

entries.name: Storm
entries: As a bonus action, activate or deactivate. While activated: +1d4 lightning per attack, and you suffer 1d4 lightning after each attack.

entries.name: Storm Generator (range 30/120)
entries: While activated, can be used as a ranged attack dealing 1d8 lightning damage instead.

item.name: Storm Thrower
entries: A complex weapon designed for Stormblade knights. The operator wears a dedicated storm chamber on a back mount connected by an arcane conduit. Each attack requires 4 charges.

entries.name: Storm
entries: As a bonus action, activate or deactivate. While activated: +1d4 lightning per attack, and you suffer 1d4 lightning after each attack.

entries.name: Storm Generator (range 80/320)
entries: While activated, can be used as a ranged attack dealing 1d8 lightning damage. When you hit a target with this weapon as a storm generator attack, the next attack against the target with lightning damage has advantage until the end of your next turn.

item.name: Trench Knife
entries: In addition to a melee attack, you can use a trench knife to make an unarmed strike. On a hit, you deal 1d4 + Strength modifier bludgeoning damage. If your unarmed strikes already roll a damage die, you can reroll a 1 but must use the new roll.

item.name: Trench Sword
entries: In addition to a melee attack, you can use a trench sword to make an unarmed strike. On a hit, you deal 1d4 + Strength modifier bludgeoning damage. If your unarmed strikes already roll a damage die, you can reroll a 1 but must use the new roll.

item.name: Voltaic Halberd
entries: The iconic weapon of the Cygnaran Stormguard. Powered by a storm chamber (4 charges per minute).

entries.name: Storm
entries: As a bonus action, activate or deactivate. While activated: +1d4 lightning per attack, and you suffer 1d4 lightning after each attack.
If you are a Storm Knight, you have advantage on attack rolls with this weapon when you use the Arc Lightning class feature.

item.name: Crossbow, Repeating
entries: Magazine (6): Once you make 6 attacks, you must use an action or bonus action to reload.

item.name: Hand Cannon
entries: Magazine (1): Once you make 1 attack, you must use an action or bonus action to reload.

item.name: Hand Cannon, Dual
entries: Magazine (2): Two barrels, each fired independently. Once both are fired, you must use an action or bonus action to reload.
When you attack with a dual hand cannon, you can choose to fire one or both barrels. If you fire both, you make the attack with disadvantage, but if it hits, you score a critical hit.

item.name: Pistol, Magelock
entries: Magazine (1): Once you make 1 attack, you must use an action or bonus action to reload.
Magelock: This weapon is specially hardened against the effects of a gun mage's rune shots. While in the hands of a gun mage, a magelock weapon is a magic weapon.

item.name: Pistol, Dual Magelock
entries: Magazine (2): Two barrels, each fired independently. Once both are fired, you must use an action or bonus action to reload.
Magelock: While in the hands of a gun mage, this is a magic weapon.

When you attack with a dual magelock pistol, you can choose to fire one or both barrels. If you fire both, you make the attack with disadvantage, but you can apply two of your Rune Shot options to that attack.

item.name: Radcliffe Quad-Iron
entries: Magazine (4): Once you make 4 attacks, you must use an action or bonus action to reload.

Volley: You can make an attack against a single target, or fire multiple shots into a 10-foot cube within normal range. Each creature in the cube must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC = 8 + your attack modifier) or take normal weapon damage. A volley cannot hit more targets than the magazine count when fired.

item.name: Cannon-Shield
entries: A heavy short-range rifle mounted in the center of a shield. Can be used as both a shield and a ranged weapon. A cannon-shield grants +2 AC while equipped. Magazine (1): Once you make 1 attack, you must use an action or bonus action to reload.

item.name: Carbine
entries: Magazine (5): Once you make 5 attacks, you must use an action or bonus action to reload.

item.name: Ogrun Battle Cannon
entries: Magazine (1): Once you make 1 attack, you must use an action or bonus action to reload.

AOE (5): The ammunition explodes on impact. Each other creature within 5 feet of the target must make a DC 13 (or DC 10 + prof, whichever is higher) Dexterity saving throw or take half the damage dealt to the target.

item.name: Rifle, Heavy
entries: Magazine (1): Once you make 1 attack, you must use an action or bonus action to reload.

item.name: Rifle, Magelock
entries: Magazine (1): Once you make 1 attack, you must use an action or bonus action to reload.
Magelock: While in the hands of a gun mage, this is a magic weapon.

item.name: Rifle, Military
entries: Magazine (1): Once you make 1 attack, you must use an action or bonus action to reload.

item.name: Rifle, Repeating
entries: Magazine (5): Once you make 5 attacks, you must use an action or bonus action to reload.

item.name: Slug Gun
entries: Magazine (1): Once you make 1 attack, you must use an action or bonus action to reload.

item.name: Sword-Cannon
entries: Magazine (1): Once you make 1 attack, you must use an action or bonus action to reload.
When used as a melee weapon, it is treated as a greatsword (2d6 slashing, heavy, two-handed).

item.name: Vanar Liberator
entries: Magazine (1): Once you make 1 attack, you must use an action or bonus action to reload.

item.name: Grenade, Explosive
entries: Grenade: You must throw this weapon to make a ranged attack with it. Add your Dexterity modifier to the attack roll but not the damage roll.
AOE (5): Each other creature within 5 feet of the target must make a DC 13 (or DC 10 + prof, whichever is higher) Dexterity saving throw or take half the damage dealt to the target.

item.name: Grenade, Incendiary
entries: Grenade: You must throw this weapon to make a ranged attack with it. Add your Dexterity modifier to the attack roll but not the damage roll.
AOE (5): Each other creature within 5 feet of the target must make a DC 13 (or DC 10 + prof, whichever is higher) Dexterity saving throw or take half the damage dealt to the target.

item.name: Light Rounds (5)
entries: Standard ammunition for pistols and most rifles. A pack of 5 rounds.

item.name: Heavy Rounds (5)
entries: Ammunition for heavy rifles, hand cannons, and mounted antipersonnel weapons. A pack of 5 rounds.

item.name: Slug Round
entries: Oversized ammunition for slug guns. About fifty percent larger than heavy rounds, transferring enormous energy to a target on impact. Also used as battle cannon shells.

item.name: Ammo Bandolier
entries: A simple leather crossbelt featuring ten to twelve leather loops suitable for holding firearm charges that can be easily accessed during combat.

item.name: Armor Resistance Treatment
entries: When armor you wear receives this treatment, choose one damage type: acid, cold, fire, or lightning. You have resistance to that damage type while wearing the treated armor.

item.name: Clockwork Injector
entries: A complex device with four vials on a gear-driven carousel. Each vial can hold one dose of an injectable alchemical substance or poison. Selecting a vial instantly rotates it into position. A creature hit takes 1 piercing damage and is exposed to the selected substance. Injecting an unwilling creature requires an attack roll (treated as an improvised weapon). Takes 1 minute to replace or refill.

item.name: Gas Mask
entries: Affixed tightly with adjustable buckles and straps, creating an impermeable seal. You can don or doff a gas mask as a bonus action. While wearing it, you suffer −5 to passive Perception but are immune to inhaled toxins (e.g., stinking cloud). Replacement filters cost 5 gp each and provide 1 hour of protection.

item.name: Goggles
entries: Thick glass goggles with adjustable leather straps. While wearing goggles, you have advantage on saving throws to avoid being blinded.

item.name: Grenade Bandolier
entries: A leather belt with loops that can store up to 6 grenades for easy access.

item.name: Gun Brace
entries: A heavy leather bandolier for pistols with sleeves to hold three or four pistols. A character wearing a gun brace can draw two pistols as part of a single attack action.

item.name: Coal, 20-Pound Bag
entries: Fuel for steam armor, arcane turbines, and steamjacks. An arcane turbine requires 1 gp of coal per day for 6 hours of continuous operation.

item.name: Bipod
entries: A lightweight brace for a rifle. A character who takes the time to set up and brace a rifle with a bipod ignores the weapon's heavy property. The character's first attack each turn with the braced rifle gains a +1 bonus to the attack roll.

item.name: Scope, Rifle or Pistol
entries: When attached to a rifle or pistol, when you take the Attack action and attack with the scoped weapon, you can use a bonus action to aim through the scope. This increases the weapon's normal and long ranges by 50 percent. A scope also functions as a spyglass.

item.name: Wrist-Spring Holster
entries: Leather bracers fitted with a spring-arm mechanism that holds a dagger or holdout pistol. Easily concealed beneath a loose sleeve. You can draw or sheathe a weapon in a wrist-spring holster for free, even if you've already drawn or sheathed a weapon during your turn.

item.name: Alchemical Waste, Crystal
entries: A crystalline byproduct of alchemical reactions. Used as a base ingredient in various alchemical formulae.

item.name: Alchemical Waste, Liquid
entries: A liquid byproduct of alchemical reactions. Harvested from spent alchemical or arcanodynamic capacitors.

item.name: Menoth's Fury
entries: A combustible alchemical fuel used to power flame spears and similar incendiary devices. 5 gp worth is enough to refuel a flame spear.

item.name: Field Alchemy Kit
entries: A carefully packed case including glass beakers and tubes, a ceramic pestle, miniature burners and fuel, mixing rods, cork stoppers, and essential alchemical ingredients. Has 10 uses. Refilling costs 5 gp. A character requires a field alchemy kit or a lab to create alchemical solutions.

item.name: Forensic Kit
entries: Contains fingerprint powder, alchemical solutions for identifying blood, tweezers, calfskin gloves, magnifying lenses, and other specialized investigation tools. Proficiency with this kit lets you add your proficiency bonus to ability checks to investigate crime scenes for clues.

item.name: Gunsmith's Kit
entries: Specialized tools for cleaning, disassembling, and reassembling firearms, plus a scale for measuring blast powder and molds for pouring shot. Proficiency lets you add your proficiency bonus to checks to maintain or repair a firearm or craft ammunition.

item.name: Mechanik's Toolkit
entries: A heavy box stuffed with mechanik's tools: wrenches, hammers, bolt drivers, ratchets, pliers, tongs, spanners, calipers, and more specialized instruments. Proficiency lets you add your proficiency bonus to checks to craft or repair mechanika, steamjacks, and similar devices.

item.name: Rune-Etching Kit
entries: An arcane mechanik's kit including conductance awls, etcher tubs, rune scrivers, conduit grips, glyph irons, precision calipers, arcanometric slide rulers, and jeweler's tools. Proficiency lets you add your proficiency bonus to checks to inscribe rune plates, craft mechanika, or maintain it.

item.name: Weatherometer
entries: A construction of wood and metal measuring wind speed and direction, precipitation, air pressure, and temperature. Setting up or breaking down takes 10 minutes. If you use a weatherometer, you have advantage on Wisdom (Nature) and Wisdom (Survival) checks to measure and predict weather conditions for the next 24 hours.

item.name: Alchemical Capacitor
entries: A small alchemical battery that powers most handheld mechanikal devices.
Power Output: 3 | Charges: 15 | Lifespan: 1 week
Loses charge after 1 week whether used or not. Cannot be recharged. An expended capacitor can be harvested for 1 unit of alchemical waste (liquid).
Crafting time: 4 hours.

item.name: Arcane Interval Generator
entries: A device containing rune-inscribed cylinders of precious metals on a rotary mount within a glass housing. Cylinders rotate at high speed, producing constant arcane charges.
Power Output: 5 | Charges: 20 (replenished on recharge) | Lifespan: 3 hours (rechargeable)
Completely rewinding the mainspring requires 30 minutes.
Crafting time: 7 weeks. Requires proficiency with glassblower's tools.

item.name: Arcane Turbine, Small
entries: A highly efficient generator transforming steam energy into arcane energy. Used in warcaster armor.
Power Output: 8 | Charges: 20 (replenishes 5 charges at the start of each of your turns) | Lifespan: 6 hours on a full load of coal and water
Requires 1 gp of coal per day for 6 hours of continuous operation.
Arcane conduits can connect the turbine to weapons and shields at 10 gp per item.
Crafting time: 10 weeks.

item.name: Arcane Turbine, Large
entries: A large arcane turbine used in steamjacks, powered by vents from the main steam engine.
Power Output: 8 | Charges: 20 (replenishes 5 charges at the start of each of your turns) | Lifespan: 6 hours
Crafting time: 10 weeks.

item.name: Arcanodynamic Accumulator
entries: Glass cylinders or spheres containing steel and gold scrolls etched with runes that generate an arcane charge.
Power Output: 4 | Charges: 16 | Lifespan: 1 month
Cannot be recharged. An expended accumulator can be harvested for 1 unit of alchemical waste (liquid).
Crafting time: 1 week.

item.name: Clockwork Capacitor
entries: An ingenious mechanikal device recharged by winding. Produced widely by the Cult of Cyriss.
Power Output: 2 | Charges: 10 (replenished on recharge) | Lifespan: 1 day (rechargeable)
Completely recharging requires 15 minutes of winding.
Crafting time: 1 week.

item.name: Runelock Capacitor
entries: A metal cylinder with runes on rotating rings. When aligned, the rings complete a formula and release an arcane charge.
Power Output: 5 | Charges: 20 | Lifespan: 1 day
At the end of each month, total charges decrease by 1 regardless of use. Cannot be recharged.
Crafting time: 1 week.

item.name: Storm Chamber
entries: Developed by the Cygnaran military. An alchemically hardened glass chamber powered by harnessed lightning.
Power Output: 5 | Charges: 20 (replenishes 1d8 charges at the start of each of your turns) | Lifespan: 1 year
A creature handling an activated storm chamber suffers 2d8 lightning damage. Cannot be recharged.
Crafting time: 5 weeks.

item.name: Mechanika Rune: Accuracy
entries: Rune Points: 1. Compatible with: Melee Weapon, Ranged Weapon.
As part of an attack, you can expend 1 charge of the item's capacitor to gain advantage on the attack roll.

item.name: Mechanika Rune: Aegis
entries: Rune Points: 1. Compatible with: Armor.
When inscribing or purchasing, choose one damage type: acid, cold, fire, or lightning. As a bonus action, expend 1 charge to gain resistance to that damage type for 10 minutes.

item.name: Mechanika Rune: Arcane Force
entries: Rune Points: 3. Compatible with: Melee Weapon.
As part of an attack, expend 3 charges to gain +2 to attack and damage rolls until the end of your turn. If you score a critical hit during this time, you push the target 10 feet away.

item.name: Mechanika Rune: Balefire
entries: Rune Points: 1. Compatible with: Melee Weapon, Ranged Weapon.
As part of an attack, expend 1 charge to deal an additional 2d8 radiant damage to fiends and undead creatures.

item.name: Mechanika Rune: Blast
entries: Rune Points: 2. Compatible with: Ranged Weapon.

As part of an attack, expend 2 charges to cause the attack to explode with violent force. Any creature within 10 feet of the target must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC = 8 + target's Dex modifier + proficiency bonus). On a failed save, the creature takes half the damage dealt to the target.

item.name: Mechanika Rune: Bond
entries: Rune Points: 2. Compatible with: Armor, Melee Weapon, Ranged Weapon.
This special rune helps warcasters bond with their weapons and armor. While powered, a warcaster can attune to the item as an action and use the Focus Manipulation feature with the attuned item.

item.name: Mechanika Rune: Cold
entries: Rune Points: 2. Compatible with: Melee Weapon, Ranged Weapon.
As part of an attack, expend 2 charges to deal an additional 2d8 cold damage. On a critical hit, the target is paralyzed until the start of your next turn.

item.name: Mechanika Rune: Compensator
entries: Rune Points: 1. Compatible with: Armor.
As a bonus action, expend 1 charge to ignore penalties to Dexterity (Stealth) checks caused by the armor for 10 minutes.

item.name: Mechanika Rune: Corruption
entries: Rune Points: 2. Compatible with: Melee Weapon, Ranged Weapon.
As part of an attack, expend 2 charges to deal an additional 2d8 acid damage.

item.name: Mechanika Rune: Disbinder
entries: Rune Points: 5. Compatible with: Melee Weapon.
As part of an attack, expend 1 charge to disrupt an opponent's concentration. If the attack hits a concentrating creature, the target rolls to maintain concentration with disadvantage.

item.name: Mechanika Rune: Electrocutioner
entries: Rune Points: 2. Compatible with: Melee Weapon, Ranged Weapon.

As part of an attack, expend 2 charges to deal an additional 2d8 lightning damage. If the attack hits a steamjack, the steamjack must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or be incapacitated. It can repeat the save at the start of each of its turns.

item.name: Mechanika Rune: Flame
entries: Rune Points: 2. Compatible with: Melee Weapon, Ranged Weapon.

As part of an attack, expend 2 charges to deal an additional 1d8 fire damage. The creature then takes 1d4 fire damage at the start of each of its turns. The creature can use its action to make a DC 10 Dexterity check to extinguish the flames.

item.name: Mechanika Rune: Fleet
entries: Rune Points: 3. Compatible with: Armor.
As a bonus action, expend 3 charges to gain 10 feet of movement and +2 AC for 1 minute.

item.name: Mechanika Rune: Fogbank
entries: Rune Points: 2. Compatible with: Armor.
As a bonus action, expend 2 charges to cast fog cloud centered on yourself without material components. The fog dissipates after 1 minute.

item.name: Mechanika Rune: Grievous Wounds
entries: Rune Points: 3. Compatible with: Melee Weapon.

As part of an attack, expend 3 charges to deal an additional 1d10 necrotic damage. If the attack hits a non-construct, non-undead creature, the target's hit point maximum is reduced by the necrotic damage dealt until a long rest. The target dies if this reduces its maximum to 0.

item.name: Mechanika Rune: Halo of Fire
entries: Rune Points: 3. Compatible with: Armor.

As a bonus action, expend 3 charges to fill a 10-foot-diameter circle centered on you with flames until the end of your next turn. A creature that enters or starts its turn in the area takes 3d6 fire damage. You are immune to the flames.

item.name: Mechanika Rune: Heightened Strength
entries: Rune Points: 1. Compatible with: Armor.
As a bonus action, expend 1 charge to make Strength checks with advantage for 1 minute.

item.name: Mechanika Rune: Light
entries: Rune Points: 1. Compatible with: Armor, Melee Weapon, Ranged Weapon.
As a bonus action, expend 1 charge. For the next 30 minutes, the item sheds bright light in a 20-foot radius and dim light for an additional 20 feet.

item.name: Mechanika Rune: Quicken
entries: Rune Points: 2. Compatible with: Armor.
As a bonus action, expend 2 charges. For 1 minute, you can take the Dash or Disengage action as a bonus action.

item.name: Mechanika Rune: Repulsor
entries: Rune Points: 2. Compatible with: Armor.
As a bonus action, expend 3 charges. For 1 minute, if an attacker hits you with a melee attack and it is Large or smaller, the attacker is pushed 15 feet away after the attack is resolved.

item.name: Mechanika Rune: Shadow
entries: Rune Points: 2. Compatible with: Armor.
As a bonus action, expend 3 charges. For 1 minute, when you are in dim light or darkness, you can use your action to become invisible until you move, take an action, or take a reaction.

item.name: Mechanika Rune: Silencer
entries: Rune Points: 1. Compatible with: Ranged Weapon.
As part of an attack, expend 1 charge to dampen the weapon's report. If you do, the attack produces no sound.

item.name: Mechanika Rune: Stall
entries: Rune Points: 2. Compatible with: Melee Weapon.
As part of an attack, expend 2 charges. If the attack hits a functioning steamjack, the steamjack must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or be incapacitated until the end of its next turn.

item.name: Mechanika Rune: Steady
entries: Rune Points: 2. Compatible with: Armor.
As a bonus action, expend 2 charges. For 1 minute, you cannot be involuntarily knocked prone.

item.name: Arcantrik Convergence Engine
entries: Also called a spell engine, this dedicated mechanikal device contains the runic formula of a single spell. When activated, it casts the inscribed spell (casting time equals the spell's normal casting time). Once cast, the arcane energy burns out the device, rendering it inoperable.
Cost: 100 gp base + 150 gp per rune point (determined by spell level).
Casting requires 1 charge + 1 charge per spell level. A character does not need to be a spellcaster to use a spell engine.

entries.caption: Spell Engine Runes
rows: Cantrip
1st
2nd
3rd
4th
5th

item.name: Arcantrik Scope
entries: Rune Points: 2. Capacitor: Clockwork capacitor.

Functions like a normal rifle scope but enables its user to pierce obscuring magic. While powered, activated, and affixed to a weapon, as a bonus action you can aim through the device to ignore penalties from darkness, lightly obscured areas, or heavily obscured areas (mundane or magical).
Requires 1 charge per minute of use. Also functions as a spyglass.

item.name: Farsight Goggles
entries: Rune Points: 3. Capacitor: Alchemical capacitor.
Triple-lensed goggles attached to a heavy belt with a low-slung alchemical capacitor. Originally developed for forward observers.
Function as a spyglass whether or not powered. While powered and activated, they provide darkvision up to 120 feet.
Requires 1 charge per hour of use.

item.name: Mechanikal Prosthetic
entries: Rune Points: 2. Capacitor: Clockwork capacitor.
A mechanikal prosthetic grafted directly to the body to replace a lost limb or organ. Functions like the missing body part but is reliant on the clockwork capacitor.
Consumes 1 charge per 2 hours of continuous function. The character must remove the limb to rewind the capacitor.
Can replace an arm, leg, or eye.

item.name: Execrator Weapon
entries: Rune Points: 3. Capacitor: Alchemical capacitor.
A heavy, silver-plated mechanikal weapon whose runeplate charges rounds with holy power. Produced exclusively by the Sancteum's armory, usually available only to members of the Order of Illumination.
As part of an attack action, expend 1 charge to imbue the attack with divine energy. On a hit, the attack deals an extra 1d10 radiant damage to fiends and undead creatures.
Cost is 450 gp + the cost of the weapon (dual hand cannon or heavy rifle).

item.name: Resolute Armor
entries: Rune Points: 4. Capacitor: Arcanodynamic accumulator.
Specialized steam armor provided exclusively to the Resolutes, the heavy infantry of the Order of Illumination. Incorporates holy runes to enable mortals to fight spectral beings.
As a reaction, expend 2 charges to gain advantage on Charisma saving throws to avoid possession until the start of your next turn.
A creature within 5 feet of a powered Resolute armor wearer cannot benefit from the Etherealness or Incorporeal Movement features.
Cost is 600 gp + the cost of the steam armor.

item.name: Firebrand
entries: A Caspian battleblade sacred to Menoth. Requires attunement by a follower of Menoth.
While attuned, this weapon deals an additional 2d6 fire damage on a hit. It sheds bright light in a 20-foot radius and dim light for an additional 20 feet.

item.name: Fellblade
entries: You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. You are immune to the frightened condition while attuned to it.

entries.name: Curse
entries: The Fellblade is cursed. If you kill a creature while wielding it, you must succeed on a DC 16 Charisma saving throw or fall into a murderous rage. While raging, you must attack the nearest creature on each of your turns until no creatures remain within reach, at which point the rage ends. Removing the weapon does not end the attunement or the curse.

item.name: Orgoth Staff
entries: A quarterstaff of ancient Orgoth make. You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls with this magic weapon.
While attuned to it, you have a +2 bonus to AC against spell attacks and advantage on saving throws against spells.

Fellblade wielders within 60 feet of the Orgoth Staff are charmed by the staff's wielder. As a bonus action, the staff's wielder can choose to end the rage of any charmed Fellblade wielder within 60 feet.

item.name: Relic Blade
entries: A greatsword sacred to Menoth. Requires attunement by a follower of Menoth.
When you hit a fey, fiend, or undead with this weapon, the target takes an additional 1d8 radiant damage.

item.name: Rune Axe, Greylords
entries: A battleaxe inscribed with the runes of the Greylords Covenant. You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls with this magic weapon.
On a critical hit, the target must succeed on a DC 14 Constitution saving throw or be paralyzed until the end of its next turn.

item.name: Grenade, Acid
entries: Grenade: You must throw this weapon to make a ranged attack with it. Add your Dexterity modifier to the attack roll but not the damage roll.
AOE (5): Each other creature within 5 feet of the target must make a DC 13 (or DC 10 + prof, whichever is higher) Dexterity saving throw or take half the damage dealt to the target.

item.name: Grenade, Fear Gas
entries: Grenade: You must throw this weapon to make a ranged attack with it. Add your Dexterity modifier to the attack roll but not the damage roll.
AOE (5): Creatures in the Area of Effect do not take damage but are exposed to fear gas on impact.

Special: A creature exposed to fear gas must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against the creator's alchemy save DC or be frightened of all other creatures for 1 minute. A creature that fails the saving throw can repeat it at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

item.name: Grenade, Flash
entries: Grenade: You must throw this weapon to make a ranged attack with it. Add your Dexterity modifier to the attack roll but not the damage roll.

AOE (5): Creatures in the Area of Effect do not take damage but must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw against the creator's alchemy save DC or be blinded for 1 minute. A creature that fails the saving throw can repeat it at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

item.name: Grenade, Knockout Bomb
entries: Grenade: You must throw this weapon to make a ranged attack with it. Add your Dexterity modifier to the attack roll but not the damage roll.
AOE (5): Creatures in the Area of Effect do not take damage but must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against the creator's alchemy save DC or fall unconscious.

item.name: Grenade, Rust Bomb
entries: Grenade: You must throw this weapon to make a ranged attack with it. Add your Dexterity modifier to the attack roll but not the damage roll.

AOE (5): Creatures in the Area of Effect do not take damage but must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw against the creator's alchemy save DC or take a −2 penalty to AC for 1 minute. Steamjacks, targets wearing metal armor, and metal objects have vulnerability to all damage until the end of your next turn if they were in the AOE. Creatures and steamjacks with immunity to acid damage are not affected.

item.name: Crucible Arms Model 609 Rifle
entries: Magazine (1): Once you make 1 attack with this weapon, you must use an action or bonus action to reload.

Special: As a bonus action, you can add an alchemical effect to the next ranged attack you make with this weapon. The attack deals an extra 1d4 acid, fire, or poison damage (your choice). After 10 such shots, you cannot use this feature again until you refill the system with 5 units of alchemical waste (liquid) or expend one use of a field alchemy kit.

item.name: Alchemical Blaster
entries: Alchemical Magazine (5): Refilling requires expending one use of a field alchemy kit.

Special: When you make an attack, choose between acid, cold, fire, or poison damage. You can make a normal single-target attack, or spray everything in a 20-foot cone by dispensing the weapon's full magazine. Each creature in the area must succeed on a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or take the weapon's normal damage, taking no damage on a successful save. The damage type listed (acid) is the default; you choose the type each attack.

item.name: Alkalic Fluid
entries: {@i Fast Brew.} Created as a bonus action with a field alchemy kit. Remains potent until the end of your next turn.
A syrupy blue liquid that neutralizes acids. A 1-ounce dose is able to render 1 pint of acid inert. A creature splashed with alkalic fluid has resistance to acid damage for 1 round.

item.name: Blackbond
entries: {@i Fast Brew.} Created as a bonus action with a field alchemy kit. Remains potent until the end of your next turn.
A thick black adhesive. As an action, you can apply blackbond to a surface. A 1-ounce dose can cover a 1-foot-square surface and hold up to 200 pounds. Blackbond loses coherency after 10 minutes.

item.name: Firestifle
entries: {@i Fast Brew.} Created as a bonus action with a field alchemy kit. Remains potent until the end of your next turn.

An expanding white foam that extinguishes flames. As an action, you can apply firestifle to a point within 5 feet. Any natural flames in a 5-foot area centered on that point go out immediately. A creature exposed to firestifle has resistance to fire damage for 1 round.

item.name: Simple Smoke
entries: {@i Fast Brew.} Created as a bonus action with a field alchemy kit. Remains potent until the end of your next turn.

A thick cloud of smoke. As an action, you can deploy the smoke to a point within 5 feet. The smoke fills a 5-foot sphere centered on the point and heavily obscures the area. The smoke dissipates after 1 round or if subjected to moderate winds.

item.name: Stink Gas
entries: {@i Fast Brew.} Created as a bonus action with a field alchemy kit. Remains potent until the end of your next turn.

A cloud of noxious gas. As an action, you can deploy the gas to a point within 5 feet. The gas fills a 5-foot sphere centered on the point. Until the end of your next turn, each creature that starts its turn in the cloud or that enters it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your alchemy save DC or become poisoned until the end of its next turn. Stink gas has no effect on constructs or the undead.

item.name: Simple Stimulant
entries: {@i Fast Brew (Synthesist archetype).} Created as a bonus action with a field alchemy kit. Remains potent until the end of your next turn.

A bitter, milky fluid that gives a creature a surge of energy and alertness. As an action, a creature can consume a dose to ignore the highest level of exhaustion it currently suffers. This effect lasts for 1 hour. A creature can also consume a dose during a short rest to reduce its exhaustion level by 1.

item.name: Glasseater
entries: {@i Fast Brew (Rogue Alchemist archetype).} Created as a bonus action with a field alchemy kit. Remains potent until the end of your next turn.
A thick blue-green paste that silently dissolves glass into a harmless vapor. One ounce of glasseater is enough to coat a 1-square-foot surface.

item.name: Umbroculant Drops
entries: {@i Fast Brew (Rogue Alchemist archetype).} Created as a bonus action with a field alchemy kit. Remains potent until the end of your next turn.

An inky-black substance. As an action, you can drip the substance into your own eyes or those of a willing creature. The drops grant the user darkvision out to 60 feet for a number of minutes equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum 1).

item.name: Alchemical Acid
entries: {@i Common Alchemical Formula.} Crafted during a short or long rest using alchemist's supplies and the required ingredients. Retains potency for 24 hours.

As an action, you can splash the contents of this vial onto a creature within 5 feet of you or throw the vial up to 20 feet, shattering it on impact. In either case, make a ranged attack against a creature or object, treating the acid as an improvised weapon. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 2d8} acid damage.
{@i Ingredients:} 1 unit of alchemist's stone, 2 units of organic acid.

item.name: Alchemical Restorative
entries: {@i Common Alchemical Formula.} Crafted during a short or long rest using alchemist's supplies and the required ingredients. Retains potency for 24 hours.

A fast-acting pharmaceutical that promotes rapid blood clotting and regeneration. A character who consumes it regains 1 spent Hit Die. If a creature taking damage from wounds or ongoing bleeding effects consumes alchemical restorative, the damage effect ends.
{@i Ingredients:} 2 units of alchemist's stone, 1 unit of mutagenic extract, 1 unit of organic oil.

item.name: Antitoxin
entries: {@i Common Alchemical Formula.} Crafted during a short or long rest using alchemist's supplies and the required ingredients. Retains potency for 24 hours.

When ingested, this pungent elixir helps counteract many natural and alchemical poisons. A creature that drinks a vial of antitoxin has advantage on saving throws against poison for 1 hour. This item confers no benefit to undead or constructs.
{@i Ingredients:} 1 unit of alchemist's stone, 1 unit of organic acid, 2 units of organic toxin.

item.name: Black Oil
entries: {@i Common Alchemical Formula.} Crafted during a short or long rest using alchemist's supplies and the required ingredients. Retains potency for 24 hours.

Black, viscous, and glue-like. As an action, throw a flask of black oil up to 20 feet, shattering it on impact. Make a ranged attack against a creature or object, treating the black oil as an improvised weapon. On a hit, the target is blinded for {@dice 1d4} rounds.

Eye protection such as goggles protects against the effects of black oil. Black oil is not water-soluble and can only be removed from the eyes by using an action to liberally apply strong alcohol or alchemical solvent.
{@i Ingredients:} 2 units of alchemical waste (liquid), 1 unit of alchemist's stone, 1 unit of organic toxin.

item.name: Bottled Light
entries: {@i Common Alchemical Formula.} Crafted during a short or long rest using alchemist's supplies and the required ingredients. Retains potency for 24 hours.

A two-part oil sealed in a jar. You can shake bottled light as a bonus action. Once shaken, it provides bright light in a 20-foot radius and dim light for an additional 20 feet for 1 minute. After a bottle goes dark, it can be shaken again to reactivate the light.
{@i Ingredients:} 1 unit of arcane extract, 2 units of bioluminescent extract.

item.name: Firebane Salve
entries: {@i Common Alchemical Formula.} Crafted during a short or long rest using alchemist's supplies and the required ingredients. Retains potency for 24 hours.

A heavy, foul-smelling ointment that protects skin, clothing, and other materials from fire. A single application can cover a single creature of up to Medium size or up to three feet of material. A creature or item treated by an application of firebane salve has resistance to fire damage for 3 hours.
{@i Ingredients:} 2 units of alchemical waste (liquid), 2 units of arcane extract.

item.name: Healing Liniment
entries: {@i Common Alchemical Formula.} Crafted during a short or long rest using alchemist's supplies and the required ingredients. Retains potency for 24 hours.
A divinely infused alchemical salve applied to grievous wounds. For 1 hour after you use this liniment, you regain {@dice 1d4} additional hit points whenever you regain hit points.
{@i Ingredients:} 1 unit of alchemist's stone, 1 unit of arcane minerals, 1 unit of organic oil.

item.name: Night's Black
entries: {@i Common Alchemical Formula.} Crafted during a short or long rest using alchemist's supplies and the required ingredients. Retains potency for 24 hours.
A thick, dull-black ointment applied to clothes and equipment. A creature that coats its equipment in an application of night's black has advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks for 1 hour.
{@i Ingredients:} 1 unit of alchemical waste (liquid), 1 unit of mutagenic extract, 1 unit of organic oil.

item.name: Vitriolic Fire
entries: {@i Common Alchemical Formula.} Crafted during a short or long rest using alchemist's supplies and the required ingredients. Retains potency for 24 hours.

A dangerous alchemical oil that bursts into superheated flame upon exposure to the air. As an action, throw a flask of vitriolic fire up to 20 feet, shattering it on impact. Make a ranged attack against a creature or object, treating the vitriolic fire as an improvised weapon. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 1d6} fire damage at the start of each of its turns. A creature can end this damage by using its action to make a DC 10 Dexterity check to extinguish the flames.
{@i Ingredients:} 2 units of alchemist's stone, 1 unit of heavy metals, 1 unit of Menoth's Fury.

item.name: Alchemical Explosive
entries: {@i Uncommon Alchemical Formula.} Crafted during a short or long rest using alchemist's supplies and the required ingredients (two uses of a field alchemy kit). Retains potency for 24 hours.

As an action, you can throw a flask of alchemical explosive up to 20 feet, shattering it on impact. Make a ranged attack against a creature or object, treating it as an improvised weapon. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 2d8} bludgeoning damage.
{@i Ingredients:} 1 unit of alchemist's stone, 2 units of mineral crystals, 2 units of organic acid.

item.name: Ashes of Urcaen
entries: {@i Uncommon Alchemical Formula.} Crafted during a short or long rest using alchemist's supplies and the required ingredients (two uses of a field alchemy kit). Retains potency for 24 hours.

An ashen powder faintly smelling of sulfur, laced with energy from beyond the living world. As an action, you can throw this powder into the air at a point up to 20 feet away. Creatures with the Etherealness feature within 20 feet of that point lose the Etherealness and Incorporeal Movement traits and all damage resistances for {@dice 1d4} minutes.
{@i Ingredients:} 1 unit of alchemist's stone, 1 unit of arcane minerals, 1 unit of ectoplasm.

item.name: Fear Gas
entries: {@i Uncommon Alchemical Formula.} Crafted during a short or long rest using alchemist's supplies and the required ingredients (two uses of a field alchemy kit). Retains potency for 24 hours.

A fog that causes terror in those who breathe its vapors. As an action, you can throw a flask of fear gas up to 20 feet, shattering it on impact. Make a ranged attack against a creature or object, treating the fear gas as an improvised weapon. On a hit, the target is exposed to fear gas.

A creature exposed to fear gas must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your Alchemy save DC or be frightened of all other creatures for 1 minute. A creature that fails the saving throw can repeat it at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.
{@i Ingredients:} 1 unit of alchemist's stone, 1 unit of arcane extract, 1 unit of arcane minerals, 1 unit of organic oil.

item.name: Fortemorphic Elixir
entries: {@i Uncommon Alchemical Formula.} Crafted during a short or long rest using alchemist's supplies and the required ingredients (two uses of a field alchemy kit). Retains potency for 24 hours.
A clear, red strength-enhancement serum designed to be injected into a creature. Administering a dose requires a clockwork injector or syringe.

When you use this elixir, you have advantage on Strength checks, and your melee attacks deal an extra {@damage 1d6} weapon damage. This bonus lasts for a number of rounds equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum of 1 round). Immediately after the elixir wears off, you suffer one level of exhaustion.
{@i Ingredients:} 1 unit of alchemist's stone, 1 burrow-mawg adrenal gland, 1 unit of mutagenic extract.

item.name: Persuasion Elixir
entries: {@i Uncommon Alchemical Formula.} Crafted during a short or long rest using alchemist's supplies and the required ingredients (two uses of a field alchemy kit). Retains potency for 24 hours.

A concoction that places a creature into a highly suggestible state. Must be injected or ingested. If ingested, the effect begins after 1 minute; if injected, it is instantaneous. When the elixir takes effect, the creature must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is charmed by other creatures it encounters for 1 hour. A creature that fails can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns. A creature that succeeds is immune to this elixir for 24 hours. If harmed by a creature that has charmed it, the charmed creature immediately becomes immune to the effect.
{@i Ingredients:} 1 unit of alchemist's stone, 2 units of organic acid, 1 unit of organic toxin.

item.name: Rust Agent
entries: {@i Uncommon Alchemical Formula.} Crafted during a short or long rest using alchemist's supplies and the required ingredients (two uses of a field alchemy kit). Retains potency for 24 hours.
A fast-acting compound that temporarily softens and corrodes metals. Consists of two reactive substances that must be mixed to take effect.

As a bonus action, you can mix the two parts of rust agent. Once mixed, it must be immediately applied or thrown. You can splash rust agent onto a creature within 5 feet or throw it up to 20 feet, shattering it on impact. Make a ranged attack against a creature or object treating it as an improvised weapon. Steamjacks and creatures wearing primarily metal armor that are hit by rust agent lose any resistances they have for 1 round. Creatures and steamjacks with immunity to acid damage are not affected.
{@i Ingredients:} 1 unit of alchemical waste (crystal), 1 unit of alchemist's stone, 1 unit of heavy metals, 1 unit of mineral acid.

item.name: Somnolence Elixir
entries: {@i Uncommon Alchemical Formula.} Crafted during a short or long rest using alchemist's supplies and the required ingredients (two uses of a field alchemy kit). Retains potency for 24 hours.

An opalescent liquid that dulls a creature's senses. A creature that consumes somnolence elixir must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature immediately falls unconscious for 1 hour. On a successful save, the creature remains conscious but has disadvantage on Intelligence and Wisdom checks for 1 hour. Constructs and undead are immune to somnolence elixir.
{@i Ingredients:} 1 unit of arcane minerals, 1 unit of organic acid, 2 units of organic oil.

item.name: Spirit Salts
entries: {@i Uncommon Alchemical Formula.} Crafted during a short or long rest using alchemist's supplies and the required ingredients (two uses of a field alchemy kit). Retains potency for 24 hours.

Alchemically treated salt that forms a barrier undead and infernals cannot cross. As an action, you can spread spirit salts into a line 10 feet long and 1 foot wide. Undead and infernals cannot cross this line. The effect lasts for {@dice 1d4} + 1 rounds and ends at the start of your turn.
{@i Ingredients:} 2 units of alchemist's stone, 1 unit of ectoplasm, 2 units of mineral crystals.

race.name: Gobber
entries: Gobbers are an amiable and curious people inclined to violence only as a last resort. They have had considerable success integrating into human society in both rural and urban areas, finding work as astute traders, skilled laborers, and innovative thinkers with a natural affinity for alchemy and engineering.

entries.name: Age
entries: Gobbers age more rapidly than many other races, reaching maturity in their early teens. On average, gobbers live 65 years.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Gobbers tend toward neutral alignments.

entries.name: Size
entries: Gobbers average about 3 feet tall and weigh about 40 pounds. Your size is Small.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your base walking speed is 25 feet.

entries.name: Beneath Notice
entries: You are proficient in the Stealth skill. Additionally, when you perform the Disengage action, after moving you can attempt to perform the Hide action to remain hidden from any creature you have concealment against.

entries.name: Superior Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 120 feet of you as if it were bright light and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Nimbleness
entries: You can move through the space of any creature whose size is larger than yours.

entries.name: Tinkerer
entries: You have proficiency with one set of artisan's tools of your choice: alchemist's supplies, mason's tools, smith's tools, or tinker's tools.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak Gobberish, which has no written form, and you can speak, read, and write one other language of your choice.

race.name: Human
entries: Humans are by far the most prevalent of all the settled peoples of western Immoren. Choose your National Origin to determine the traits reflecting your homeland.

entries.name: Age
entries: Humans reach adulthood in their late teens and live less than a century.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Humans do not tend toward any particular alignment. The best and the worst are found among them.

entries.name: Size
entries: Humans vary widely in height and build, from barely 5 feet to well over 6 feet tall. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your base walking speed is 30 feet.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write two languages of your choice.

entries.name: National Origin
entries: Choose the nation your character is from: Cygnar, Khador, Llael, Ord, Protectorate of Menoth, or Cryx (Scharde). Your nation determines your additional traits. See the corresponding subrace.

race.name: Iosan
entries: The learned and physically graceful people of Ios are the remnants of a once-vast empire. Iosans live considerably longer than most races and rarely show the ravages of time, even in their advanced years.

entries.name: Age
entries: Although they reach maturity at nearly the same age as humans, Iosans live far longer. Many live to be over 200 years old, with some living up to three centuries.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Iosans do not tend toward a particular alignment.

entries.name: Size
entries: Iosans tend to have slender builds and are roughly as tall as humans. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your base walking speed is 30 feet.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: Accustomed to the gloomy forests of Ios, you can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Keen Senses
entries: You have proficiency in the Perception skill.

entries.name: Martial Society
entries: When you make an attack roll and roll a 1, you can reroll the die but must use the new roll, even if it is another 1.

entries.name: Skill Versatility
entries: The long lifespan of Iosans gives them the opportunity to master many skills and crafts. You have proficiency with any combination of two other skills or tools of your choice.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Shyr and one other language you have picked up in your travels.

race.name: Nyss
entries: The Nyss are a rugged race that once inhabited the frozen north amid the imposing peaks of the Shard Spires. Driven from their home by the dragon Everblight, they have been forced to live among the Iron Kingdoms as refugees.

entries.name: Age
entries: Nyss are among the longest-lived peoples in western Immoren. They reach maturity in their early twenties and can live for over 400 years, barring violence or disease.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Nyss tend toward chaotic and neutral alignments.

entries.name: Size
entries: Nyss are slightly taller than the average human and have slender builds. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your base walking speed is 30 feet.

entries.name: Child of Winter
entries: Accustomed to the frozen mountains of your homeland, you have resistance to cold damage and are naturally adapted to cold climates.

entries.name: Keen Senses
entries: You have proficiency in the Perception skill.

entries.name: Nyss Weapon Training
entries: You are proficient with the Nyss longbow and Nyss claymore.

entries.name: Quick Response
entries: When you roll initiative, you may do so with advantage. You can't use this feature again until you complete a long rest.

entries.name: Light Resilience
entries: Accustomed to the glare of sunlight off of snow, you have advantage on saving throws against being blinded.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Shyr and one other language you have picked up in your travels.

race.name: Ogrun
entries: Ogrun are fierce-looking, formidable creatures who tower over most other races and are capable of remarkable feats of strength. They are also a tremendously honorable people who have earned a place in the Iron Kingdoms, primarily as laborers and peerless warriors.

entries.name: Age
entries: Ogrun reach maturity in their midteens, but they do not live as long as other races and seldom live past 75 years.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Ogrun tend toward lawful alignments. When an ogrun swears an oath to a leader or cause, breaking that oath is all but unthinkable.

entries.name: Size
entries: Ogrun range between 7 and 8 feet tall, with massive bodies weighing in excess of 300 pounds. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your base walking speed is 30 feet.

entries.name: Huge Stature
entries: Your large size allows you to wield two-handed weapons in one hand. When you use a weapon with the versatile property, you always use the damage value in parentheses that appears with the property.

entries.name: Imposing Presence
entries: You have proficiency in one skill of your choice: Intimidation or Persuasion.

entries.name: Powerful Build
entries: You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift.

entries.name: Thick-Skinned
entries: You have a +1 bonus to AC when not wearing heavy armor.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak the Molgur-Og dialect and can speak, read, and write one other language.

entries.name: Variant: Rhulic Ogrun
entries: Ogrun who grew up in Rhul alongside the dwarven clans may replace the Imposing Presence trait with Lessons of Ghord and Oath of Fealty. See the Rhulic subrace.

race.name: Trollkin
entries: Trollkin retain a nigh-insatiable hunger and are known for their feasts, but intelligent races no longer tend to make the menu. They are captivating singers, skilled weavers, and members of tightly knit clans called kiths.

entries.name: Age
entries: Trollkin mature at the same rate as humans but live nearly twice as long—about 150 years, on average.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Most trollkin are lawful, and their culture is organized in accordance with their ancient traditions. They tend to be good-natured but can become ruthless and ferocious when their people are threatened.

entries.name: Size
entries: Trollkin stand between 6 and 7 feet tall and average about 250 pounds. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your base walking speed is 30 feet.

entries.name: Born to Be Wild
entries: You have proficiency in the Survival skill.

entries.name: Ceaseless Stamina
entries: You ignore the effects of exhaustion until you have suffered 3 or more levels of exhaustion. When you finish a long rest, your exhaustion level is reduced by 3 instead of 1.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of grey.

entries.name: Regenerative
entries: When you roll a 1 or 2 when spending a Hit Die to regain hit points at the end of a short rest, you can reroll the die but must use the new roll, even if it is a 1 or 2.

entries.name: Trollkin Toughness
entries: You have advantage on saving throws against poison and disease, and you have resistance to poison damage.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write the common language of your native kingdom (Cygnaran, Khadoran, etc.). Additionally, you speak the Molgur-Trul dialect, a tribal language that has no written form.

entries.name: Subrace
entries: Choose one of the following subraces: Woodland Trollkin, Northkin Trollkin, or Albino Trollkin.

entries.name: Ability Score Increase
entries: Your Intelligence and Charisma scores each increase by 1.

entries.name: Right to Bear Arms
entries: Cygnar's firearms are among the best in the Iron Kingdoms, and its citizens are very familiar with their operation. You gain +1 to attack rolls made with simple and martial firearms.

entries.name: Well Educated
entries: You have proficiency in two of the following skills: Arcana, Insight, Medicine, Nature, and Religion.

entries.name: Industrial Revolution
entries: When you make an ability check using artisan's tools, you may roll a d4 and add the number rolled to the ability check.

entries.name: Ability Score Increase
entries: Your Constitution and Wisdom scores each increase by 1.

entries.name: Legacy of the North
entries: You have proficiency in two of the following skills: Animal Handling, Athletics, Intimidation, and Survival.

entries.name: Iron Will
entries: You have advantage on saving throws against being frightened.

entries.name: Northern Resilience
entries: You gain a +2 bonus to Constitution checks and Constitution saving throws.

entries.name: Ability Score Increase
entries: Your Intelligence and Charisma scores each increase by 1.

entries.name: Keen Mind
entries: You have proficiency in two of the following skills: Deception, Intimidation, Perception, and Stealth.

entries.name: Blood of Liberty
entries: When you make an Intelligence (Investigation) or Wisdom (Insight) check, you may roll a d4 and add the number rolled to the ability check.

entries.name: Dueling Code
entries: You have proficiency with one martial weapon of your choice.

entries.name: Ability Score Increase
entries: Your Strength and Dexterity scores each increase by 1.

entries.name: Shore Leave
entries: You have proficiency in two of the following skills: Acrobatics, Athletics, Performance, and Survival.

entries.name: Sea Legs
entries: You have a swimming speed of 30 feet. You can't use your swimming speed while wearing medium or heavy armor.

entries.name: Coastal Home
entries: You have proficiency with your choice of one waterborne vehicle and one set of the following tools: carpenter's tools, cartographer's tools, navigator's tools, or cook's utensils.

entries.name: Ability Score Increase
entries: Your Strength and Constitution scores each increase by 1.

entries.name: Pilgrim's Path
entries: You have proficiency in the History, Religion, and Survival skills.

entries.name: Citizen Soldiers
entries: You have proficiency with simple melee weapons, martial melee weapons, light armor, medium armor, and shields.

entries.name: Ability Score Increase
entries: Your Dexterity and Wisdom scores each increase by 1.

entries.name: Smuggler's Heritage
entries: You have proficiency in two of the following skills: Deception, Intimidation, Perception, and Stealth.

entries.name: A Pirate's Life
entries: You are proficient with the cutlass and simple pistols.

entries.name: Touch of Blight
entries: You have advantage on saving throws against poison and disease, and you have resistance to acid damage and necrotic damage.

entries.name: Ability Score Increase
entries: Your Constitution score increases by 1.

entries.name: Woodland Survivalist
entries: Nonmagical difficult terrain within forests doesn't slow your travel. Additionally, you have advantage on Wisdom (Survival) checks to hunt wild game within forests.

entries.name: Ability Score Increase
entries: Your Strength score increases by 1.

entries.name: Northkin Resilience
entries: Accustomed to the harsh winters of your homeland, you are naturally adapted to cold climates and have resistance to cold damage.

entries.name: Ability Score Increase
entries: Your Charisma score increases by 1.

entries.name: Cantrip
entries: You know two cantrips of your choice from the sorcerer spell list. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for them.

subrace.name: Rhulic
entries: The Rhulfolk of the mountainous kingdom of Rhul have long embraced mechanika alongside their ancient dwarven traditions.

entries.name: Ability Score Increase
entries: Your Intelligence score increases by 1.

entries.name: Firearms Training
entries: You have proficiency with carbines.

entries.name: Master Craftsman
entries: You have proficiency with tinker's tools. You can craft magic items in 50 gp increments instead of the normal 25 gp increments, and you can craft mundane items in 15 gp increments instead of the normal 5 gp increments.

entries.name: Oathbound
entries: A Rhulic dwarf's oath cannot intentionally be broken in any way, nor do Rhulfolk look for loopholes to do so. When performing a task directly related to a sworn oath, you receive a +1 bonus on all skill checks.

You may have one sworn oath at 1st level, a second at 7th level, and a third at 12th level. Oaths must be incredibly specific and long-lasting tasks that will take most of your life to complete. Good examples include "I swear to protect the children of my fallen comrade" or "I will never allow anyone to steal from the poor."

subrace.name: Rhulic
entries: Rhulic Ogrun replace the Imposing Presence trait with the following two traits.

entries.name: Lessons of Ghord
entries: You benefit from the teachings of the Rhulfolk to the ogrun. You are proficient with your choice of mason's tools or smith's tools and have proficiency in the History skill.

entries.name: Oath of Fealty
entries: Choose another friendly Rhulic or ogrun character to be your korune. You swear fealty to that character and are incapable of betraying that oath. You may disobey specific orders but must always work toward your korune's greater goals.

If your korune is within 5 feet of you when you take the Dodge action, you can choose to have your korune benefit from the action instead of you. After you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Human
entries: Of all the peoples of western Immoren, humanity is perhaps the most perplexing to consider We have adapted to live in a bevy of different conditions, from freezing tundra to parched desert. Countless variations are found among us, making it difficult to find any description that fully encompasses the breadth of all we humans have to offer. But do not allow yourself to be mistaken. Despite all our advancements, philosophies, and sciences, and despite the favor of the gods we worship and the Gift of Magic that has been bestowed upon us, we have within ourselves the same inclination toward violence and warfare that made us so welcome among the Molgur tribes We may dress up our violence with modern conveniences and brilliant strategies, but something within us calls us to shed blood —Professor Viktor Pendrake Mankind holds sway from the far north of Khador all the way to the southern tip of Bloodshore Island. There is great diversity among humanity—so much so that a traveler across western Immoren can expect to encounter numerous ethnic and regional groups, most of which identify themselves based on the most prominent old kingdoms from the Thousand Cities Era before the arrival of the Orgoth. These cultural identities are deeply rooted despite the rise of the modern kingdoms defined by the Corvis Treaties. Cultures and appearance vary dramatically among these groups, as most of these identifications are based on region rather than bloodline.

entries.name: Iosan
entries: The learned and physically graceful people of Ios are the remnants of a once-vast empire that has long since collapsed. For many centuries, their former strength as a nation has dwindled, as have their numbers, leaving their forested cities a twilight kingdom of empty relics and unoccupied streets with only a fraction of their former numbers to inhabit them.

entries.name: Centuries Facing Doom
entries: Iosans are physically similar to humans, with males and females standing 6 feet tall on average. They tend to have a slenderer build than humans but are nearly as robust. Iosans live considerably longer than most races, which has given rise to a reputation for agelessness. Many enjoy lives over two centuries long, for they are not prone to disease and rarely show the ravages of time, even in their advanced years. Given the imminent doom their race faces, their longevity is not seen as a great advantage. Iosans are generally pale and have a wide range of hair colors, with some dying their hair exotic shades. Certain sects wear tattoos as a way to show solidarity, and a number have also chosen to shave their heads.

entries.name: History
entries: Although Ios has been a part of western Immoren for almost as long as human civilization has existed, the Iosans originally came from eastern Immoren. The disaster that destroyed their old civilization led to the gradual decline of their entire race. Not many non-Iosans know it, but the few Iosans they encounter are part of a dwindling generation facing the prospect of an extinction that might occur within their lifetimes. This existential crisis has led to xenophobia and extreme secrecy among most Iosans. Outsiders who come to know Iosans soon realize they are an intelligent and skilled people equally well versed in combat and the arcane arts. Iosans are deeply private about religious matters, but when they discuss such topics, they do so with a mixture of deep devotion and great sadness.

entries.name: Grim Outlook
entries: Iosans have been reticent for as long as humanity can remember, but their isolationism has taken an extreme turn in the last few decades, during which the nation of Ios has closed its borders and cut off all trade with foreigners. Its forests are a strange and silent place from which no Iosans emerge, and intruders who venture beyond its borders are never seen again. Rumors circulate of some great downfall, perhaps matching the Cataclysm of ancient days, but neither scholars nor treasure hunters have returned to confirm the truth. Despite all this, a number of Iosans live among the human kingdoms. These tend to be exiles, pilgrims, or well-armed agents serving vital missions. Two Iosan religious sects have specific reasons to be abroad in human lands: the Seekers and the Retribution of Scyrah, with the former tending to be much friendlier to humans than the latter. The people of Ios have long been a secretive sort, living in seclusion among their forests The rare ambassadors who emerged to make contact with the human nations were tight-lipped about their homeland even before they shut off their borders to trade. Yet recent changes have caused a few more of them to trickle out into the wider world—sadly, not always for the betterment of both peoples The assassins among them who seek out the magically gifted are all but common knowledge to the upper chambers of power in the Iron Kingdoms, as are the seekers of knowledge who search every darkened corner of the world for a way to reverse the fate that has befallen their nation Those who still live in the cities of the Iron Kingdoms, if they know anything about these groups, are unlikely to reveal any secrets Yet do not dismiss them due to their privacy. Iosans live long and storied lives, and if you are privileged enough to be considered a friend, you will find them loyal and capable companions. —Lynus Wesselbaum former assistant to Viktor Pendrake

entries.name: Nyss
entries: The Nyss are a rugged race that once inhabited the frozen north amid the imposing peaks of the Shard Spires—a region so inhospitable that even the hardy Khadorans ceded it to them. They lived there as seminomadic tribes with permanent structures reserved for houses of worship to their god, Nyssor. Driven from their home by betrayal and corruption by the dragon Everblight, they have been forced to live among the Iron Kingdoms as refugees. Although Nyss walk a difficult road, few others possess their strength of will.

entries.name: Pale As Ice
entries: Physically, Nyss are somewhat reminiscent of Iosans, but there is no mistaking the two. Nyss are tall—taller than Iosans—with skin to match their snowy realm and stark hair that's either jet black or very pale. Their eyes are like ice, cold and blue or sometimes violet. Nyss mark themselves with intricate tattoos called siyaeric, or "letters of the skin," which relate to traditions that mark an individual as a member of a particular "shard," or tribe. Additional modifications to these tattoos express personal convictions, family history, noteworthy deeds, or religious, magical, or spiritual beliefs.

entries.name: Cold and Distant
entries: The Nyss were once more similar to the Iosans and lived alongside them, although they were never as xenophobic or insular as their cousins. Centuries ago, they undertook a spiritual exodus to the frozen wilds and adopted a new way of life, forever changing them. They adapted to the frozen peaks of the Shard Spires and became accustomed to snow and ice. They lived in small tribal villages, protecting their mountain homes from the intrusions of northern trollkin, wild human tribes, and opportunistic bogrin.

entries.name: Prodigal Returns
entries: The Nyss are a tribal people who live close to the land as superlative hunters, trackers, archers, and swordsmen. One need look no further than the shanty villages in the poorer districts of any major city to witness the remainder of what were, formerly, the most elusive peoples in western Immoren The Nyss who fled their mountain homes have found a new life among the human kingdoms, though not an easy one Years spent as refugees have hardened these already cold people. While some have managed to rebuild their lives and could hardly be considered "refugees" any longer, there is a deep sadness within those I encounter on the streets—a void of having lost their homes and families forever Rumors of an exodus of the Nyss to Ios have circulated for some years now If true, I suspect that those who now live among the forests of Ios find themselves as discontented as the Nyss who live by the dockyards of the City of Ghosts —Professor Viktor Pendrake

entries.name: Character Options
entries: Inheritors of a long and ancient line, they have mastered the forging of superior weapons and the crafting of supple leather armor. Considered the chosen people of the winter god Nyssor, the Nyss have an affinity for cold. Sorcery is common among them and is seen as a blessing that allows them to manifest the power of cold against their enemies. The Nyss were traditionally a strongly devout people, but their culture was recently shattered by the arrival of the dragon Everblight, who worked through his minions to enslave most of the Nyss as part of his legion. The few who escaped the dragon's blight fled south as refugees to eke out livings as mercenaries, hunters, or criminals. Even as they tried to preserve their old ways, they relied on other peoples to ensure their survival. The recovery and return of the god Nyssor to Ios has caused many Nyss to return to their former homeland. They still live as outsiders among the Iosans, gathering in smaller communities in the forests, but some have chosen to see it as a homecoming. Those who have not returned to Ios still live among the kingdoms of humanity, doing what they must to survive.

entries.name: Ogrun
entries: The noble ogrun who live among the kingdoms of humanity often face discrimination and fear born from their great size and tremendous strength, yet they have continued to live among us with no more incidents than any other people—far fewer, in most cases In Rhul, ogrun are as much a part of society as the dwarves in many of their communities. The Rhulfolk's proficiency at negotiation and trade has no doubt rubbed off on their overlarge neighbors, arming them with skills that help them fold into our larger societies These talents, coupled with unshakable codes of honor and fealty, make ogrun ideal bodyguards and soldiers As can easily be seen among the ranks of Cygnar's trenchers, ogrun treat their responsibility to a commanding officer with the same gravity as they would to one of their "korune" masters Even in less-regimented circumstances, such as in a workshop or on the docks, ogrun are inclined to deep-seated loyalty An employer can be certain that any ogrun in their employ will fill their duties to the best of their abilities —Beldam Sigmur Rhulic scholar Ogrun are fierce-looking, formidable creatures who tower over most other races and are capable of remarkable feats of strength. They are also a tremendously honorable people who have earned a place for themselves in the Iron Kingdoms, primarily as laborers and peerless warriors.

entries.name: High and Mighty
entries: Ogrun are known for their size. With most standing at least 7 feet tall, their broad frames of burly muscle have given them a reputation as brutal warriors and skilled laborers. Ogrun skin ranges in color from pale hues to deep black. Many have a ruddy, brownish skin. Their hair is typically dark and bristly, although males typically lack hair on their heads and prefer to wear rugged beards or other facial hair.

entries.name: Loyal to a Fault
entries: Ogrun are one of the major Dhunian races, and they remain a spiritual people despite the changes their society has undergone within Rhul. Traditional ogrun culture is rooted in a simple but effective system of feudalism whereby young warriors called bokurs must eventually find worthy leaders to serve. Elder ogrun in turn hope to prove worthy enough to earn the binding oaths of young bokurs, for whom they then carry a certain responsibility. The ogrun in the leadership position is a korune—a lord—and there can be multiple layers of vassalage in an ogrun tribe, with the leader being whoever boasts the largest chain of subordinates. General obligations are secondary to personal oaths, and even Rhulic ogrun feel the urge to find a worthy korune, generally after an extended period of wandering and self-testing.

entries.name: Tribes Among Kingdoms
entries: Scattered tribes of ogrun still exist in isolated pockets, but most have integrated into the cultures of the kingdoms of western Immoren. The largest concentration of ogrun is found in Rhul, where ogrun are counted as full members of the dwarven clans to whom they have sworn fealty. Ogrun

entries.name: Character Options
entries: Variant: Rhulic Ogrun Ogrun in the mountainous region of Rhul have long had a relationship with the dwarves. If you want to play an ogrun with a close connection to the Rhulfolk, you can use these variant traits, both of which replace the Imposing Presence trait. Lessons of Ghord. You benefit from the teachings of the Rhulfolk to the ogrun. You are proficient with your choice of mason's tools or smith's tools and have proficiency in the History skill. Oath of Fealty. Choose another friendly Rhulic or ogrun character to be your korune. You swear fealty to that character and are incapable of betraying that oath. You may disobey specific orders but must always work toward your korune's greater goals, such as the goal of a Rhulic dwarf's Oathbound trait. If your korune is within 5 feet of you when you take the Dodge action, you can choose to have your korune benefit from the action instead of you. After you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest. loyalty is legendary, and they have a reputation for being among the most dedicated and fiercest bodyguards in western Immoren. Mercenary ogrun are deemed more trustworthy and reliable than other sell-swords and can command a premium wage. Rhulic ogrun have adopted many of the beliefs and priorities of dwarven society, including taking great pride in their work. While most ogrun choose to master the combat arts, there are many accomplished ogrun smiths, masons, and mechanics who tackle these professions with the same dedication as a bokur mastering his battle-glaive.

entries.name: Trollkin
entries: The order inherent in the trollkin way of life is reflected in their stonemasonry—some of the oldest existing architecture in all of western Immoren—and their various cultural habits Indeed, their strange mating rituals, peculiar diet of vastly undercooked (if not raw) foodstuffs, and impressive vocal range mark them as the oddest of the enlightened races (And this is to say nothing of the "tohmaak," a traditional greeting between young bravos and warriors involving solid headbutts rather than firm handshakes!) If anything, trollkin truly have a great capacity for song Among their males, I have heard exceptionally gifted individuals capable of a warbling chant known as "fell calling " In fact, this trill vocalization is so powerful that some use it as a weapon, and to great effect.
